observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
a) Acting City Accountant together with the City Treasurer to review and analyze prior year’s reconciling items and exert effort to facilitate the adjustments in the books for a fair presentation of cash in bank balances; and
b) City Treasurer coordinate with the concerned depository banks the submission of necessary documents related to the identified reconciling items in the bank reconciliation statement like debit memos and credit memos.
observations.reason:
Absence of submission of necessary properly designated employees to handle this task.
Reiterated in current audit findings.
(Observation No. 2 in Part II of this report)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and CGSO agreed to work for the application and/or renewal of LTO registration of all serviceable motor vehicles and secure insurance coverage with the GSIS. Also, refrain from using motor vehicles with expired registration.
We also recommended and the City Accountant agreed to refrain from paying expenses related to the use of motor vehicles with expired registration and require that the same must be renewed and insured first before payment will be made.
We also recommended and the City Budget Officer agreed the inclusion in the annual appropriations, sufficient funds for the payment of registration and insurance premiums of all serviceable motor vehicles.
observations.reason: Not all LTO registrations were renewed due to numerous city vehicles.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
a) Acting City Accountant and City General Services Officer to exert extra effort to retrieve the records of 30 PPEs with no details, and some need to update and complete the PPELCs and PCs, and thereafter, reconcile the PPE balances between the book balance and the RPCPPE;
b) City General Services Officer and the Inventory Committee to conduct the complete annual physical count of all PPEs in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006, to ensure that the existence, condition, and accountability of recorded PPE balances are established; and
c) Acting City Accountant to take up necessary accounting entries to write-off/drop from the books of accounts the remaining balances as of December 31, 2022, of amounts lumped under “No Details,” which were created to facilitate conversion of accounts from the Old Government System to New Government Accounting System.
observations.reason: There are no inventory conducted on 30 PPEs with no details, and some need to be cleansed since it is way lower than the threshold. Continuous updating of records and conduct of physical count on-going. Per verification, reclassification was made on some amounts that were lodged as “no details”, however, there were still balances as of year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the City Mayor and the City Accountant agreed to:
a) Require the officials and employees to submit liquidation documents and/or refund immediately the long overdue cash advances. Otherwise, require them to refund the amount advanced to avoid institution of administrative and criminal charges against the erring officials pursuant to COA Circular 2012-004; and
b) Expedite the preparation of additional documents to facilitate the request for authority to write-off of long outstanding cash advances, which has remained non-moving for more than ten years and where settlement/accountability could no longer be made, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason:
The Management is having a hard time establishing the current addresses of accountable officers. Most of these employees are not in the service anymore.
A partial request for write-off was already made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that:
a) The AOs who are in custody of these unserviceable properties return these to the GSO through Property Return Slip for cancellation of ARE; and
b) The Head of the Agency thru the General Services Officer file a request for appraisal of unserviceable properties for disposal with the COA Technical Team supported with necessary documents needed for technical evaluation and appraisal; and
c) (implied) dispose of unserviceable properties to avoid overstatement of PPE accounts and to derive possible additional income.
observations.reason:
GSO has informed the concerned AOs, however some have not yet responded.
Batch 1 of the unserviceable properties were already auctioned off to the highest bidder.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the GSO to:
a) Fully update property card records especially on acquisitions and disposals, including unserviceable items which cannot be located for reconciliation with subsidiary ledgers maintained at the OCA;
b) Conduct regular reconciliation with the OCA to ascertain the correctness of the PPE accounts balances and for fair financial presentation; and
c) Maintain property cards on Land, Land Improvements, Buildings and Other Structures with complete data on acquisition/construction cost, appraised value, date and mode of acquisition for reconciliation with balances shown in the Subsidiary Ledgers maintained at the OCA.
We further recommended that OCA should reclassify items below the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00 as semi-expendable inventory or expense accounts for fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.reason:
There is difficulty in accessing support documents due to voluminous files involved.
On-going reconciliation between the two concerned offices.
Per record, only a List of Properties with Transfer Certificate of Title is maintained and included in the RPCPPE with information only on the TCT number, Lot Number, location, and area. Data on the acquisition cost and appraised/market values are also not indicated.
Reclassification on time was made by the Accounting Office, but this time on ₱50,000.00 threshold.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
a) Direct the City Accountant and the City Engineer’s Office to exert extra efforts for the speedy reconciliation of their records regarding on-going projects; and
b) Address the problems encountered on unimplemented projects preferably on six projects with total appropriation of ₱31,850,000.00, and instruct the City Engineer to speed up its implementation so that constituents who are beneficiaries of the projects will enjoy the benefits therefrom.
observations.reason: There are still discrepancies with the records of both departments since the CEO report is based on actual accomplishment, while the OCA is based on payments made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
a) Through the City Legal Office and Office of the City Accountant, require the NGOs/POs to immediately submit liquidation report for financial assistance whose programs, projects or activities were already completed, especially those with long overdue accounts; and
b) Through the City Legal Officer, institute legal action against defaulting NGOS/POs which failed to complete a project covered by the MOA or has committed material violation on the provisions of the MOA or of COA Circular No. 2007-001.
observations.reason: There are still financial assistance to NGOs that were not liquidated. Some of the NGOs/POs were no longer existing. Also, the officers/directors of some of these NGOs/POs are either abroad or cannot be located so the present set of officers cannot yet submit the liquidation report since their records are incomplete. No action with regard to instituting legal action on this audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor demand immediate liquidation of financial assistance from the various leagues and other federations, and require the return or refund of the unexpended or unutilized amount pursuant to 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; otherwise, institute appropriate legal action against the erring officials.
observations.reason: The financial assistance to Liga ng mga Barangay/Noli Villarosa amounting to ₱3.8 million was suspended in audit, but were partially settled with the issuance of NSSDC No. 19-003 dated September 6, 2019 in the amount of ₱1.121 million. The amount left of ₱2.679 million were without supporting documents hence, ND# 19-001-101 (2014) was issued. The persons liable filed an appeal with the Office of the Regional Director. A decision affirming ND No. 19-001-101(2014) was issued by the ORD. One of the persons liable in the said ND filed an appeal to the Commission Proper, however, no decision has been issued yet.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant:
a) Prepare aging schedule on dormant Other Receivables accounts and exert efforts in securing the required supporting documents and other data on existing conditions for validation so that request for write-off on accounts for write-off on accounts of over 10 years be facilitated. Consequently, prepare adjusting entries for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statement; and
b) Verify Other Receivables accounts with abnormal credit balances to determine valid reasons and conditions for reclassification to appropriate accounts.
observations.reason:
Awaiting decision of COA on the write-off request so that adjusting entries can be passed. For amounts exceeding ₱100,000.00, the request was forwarded to the ORD.
There are still accounts with abnormal balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor thru the City Engineer require the Contractor to speed-up the processing of the required license with MARINA for the full completion of the Sum-ag Development Project, which is the Construction/Fabrication of Floating Restaurant to attain the promotion of eco-tourism in the City.
We recommended that the City Engineer thru the Project Engineer coordinate with the City Treasurer for the imposition of liquidated damages from any succeeding payments or billing due to Contractor, if found warranted.
observations.reason: No other updates were given to the Audit Team regarding the imposition of liquidated damages.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Administrator impose the provision of Section 46 of the Revised Market Code of Bacolod City by revoking or cancelling his lease right and ejectment, after observance of due process in case of failure of concerned stallholders to pay their overdue market rental fees despite the demand. The City Administrator also revoke, after observance of due process, the lease contract of stall owners who sub-leased their stalls or have their privileges as market stall awardees sold to other persons contrary to Sections 19 and 46 of the Revised Market Code of Bacolod City.
observations.reason: Some delinquent stallholders have requested for more time to pay their rentals in arrears citing the long downturn of the economy due to COVID pandemic over the past 2 years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Treasurer and the City Accountant reconcile their records per cashbook, general Fund and subsidiary ledgers immediately while documents are still available for references. Henceforth, the two offices must conduct reconciliation at least every end of each quarter and any due items subject for correction must be adjusted promptly to present a reliable Cash – Local Treasury balance or the accountable officer’s balance of accountability at any given time as required under Sec. 181 (c), GAAM, Volume I.
For easier reconciliation, the following action plans were adopted: Upon receipt, the Cashier and her Disbursing Officers would go over the Subsidiary Ledgers attached to the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Reports provided for by the Office of the City Accountant; and the Heads of OCA and CTO would formally recommend the installation of ENGAS 2 (Viewing Only) @ CTO – Cash Division as per inquiry and verification.
observations.reason: Of these, two Trust Fund Accounts were already reconciled, to wit: HCI Charges (LBP C/A #0422-1318-84), and PROF. FEE – LYING IN (LBP C/A #0422-1318-92) as per Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Administrator impose the provision of Section 46 of the Revised Market Code of Bacolod City by revoking or cancelling the lease rights and ejectment, after observance of due process in case of failure of concerned stallholders to pay their overdue market rental fees despite the demand; and ensure that the lease contract shall be strictly renewed every two years from date the contract is signed pursuant to the provision of the Contract of Lease.
observations.reason:
Some delinquent stallholders have requested for more time to pay their rentals in arrears citing the long downturn of the economy due to COVID pandemic over the past 2 years.
No status was given on this recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor/Board of Trustees of the Provident Fund submit immediately the authorization and guidelines from the DBM allowing subsequent monetary contributions to the Provident Fund.
observations.reason: The City Mayor made an appeal to the Secretary of the Dept of Budget and Mgt through its Director for Region VI on these contributions of the City Govt to the Provident Fund. COA-Bacolod was furnished a copy of this appeal. Awaiting decision of the DBM Secretary on the appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Property Officer a) to inspect the above donated properties and to determine their existence and whereabouts, their present condition, the provision of protection against further deterioration and losses cannibalization; b) maintain record and submit the documents for these properties like the Property Ledger Cards, ARE, Property Return Slip; c) request for the proper disposal if found unserviceable; d) make a report on the irregular and improper use, losses and cannibalization of the said properties and recommend for the filing of charges against all persons liable; and e) revisit and review the system of property management observed by the agency and recommend measures to correct the lapses in the system.
observations.reason: The donated properties are fully depreciated hence, amount in books will be at salvage value. These properties will be included in the disposal of the said properties. Awaiting the City’s disposal of the said properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City General Services Officer to regularly reconcile his records of TCTs with that of the City Assessor and the City Accountant and adopt procedures to address the exception noted above.
observations.reason: Reconciling the status of the land titles not yet named under City of Bacolod.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor demand from the nine (9) accountable officers enumerated above the withholding of payments of any money due to them and that the accountable officers who incurred shortages in the total amount of P1,856,235.43 be held accountable.
observations.reason: One AO already liquidated her cash advance through deduction on Terminal Pay. Action on cash advance of the other AO will await the results of the inquiry with the Ombudsman on any appeal she may have filed with the said Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor demand from FAT Petron: a) the return of the balance of P1.213M; b) the return of additional cost incurred due to price increase per liters of fuel of P50,255.48; and c) legal interest on the P1.217M from November 2007 up to time of refund.
observations.reason: The project engineer in charge was Absent Without Leave (AWOL) when this AOM was issued. The City Mayor demanded for the return of the said amount from FAT Petron but the company was unable to make the refund. Consequently, a collection case under Civil Case No. 13-14174 was filed by the City Legal Office. Legal process still on going. Awaiting the results of pending court case. (Civil Case No. 13-14174 against Francis Albert A. Torres of FAT Petron Service Station)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and make the necessary adjusting entries; and 





Undertake regular and timely review and reconciliation of the accounts in compliance to the above-mentioned rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The Management has not acted upon with the recommendation.





The Management has not acted upon with the recommendation.
observations.reason:
The Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts showed a balance of P2.5 million and P3.9 million, respectively, as of December 31, 2024, hence a net variance of P(1.445 million).

Due to voluminous work of the MA, the reconciliation between the two accounts were not yet finished.

(All recommendations were reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 3, page 41 to 44.)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory count of all PPE and submit an updated and corrected RPCPPE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006, dated January 31, 2020. 



Instruct the General Services Officer/Supply Officer-Designate and the MA to regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate classification of Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

The MA should review previous records to come up with the purchase amount of PPEs on the prior year’s.
observations.managementAction:
.

The Management has not acted upon with the recommendation.

The Designated GSO is yet to complete the physical count. Reconciliation will follow after the physical count.

The MA is yet to go over with the old records for the purchase amount of PPEs.

(All recommendations were reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 1, page 35 to 38.)

observations.reason:
As of December 31, 2024, No LRNLCs prepared and maintained by the Management. The beginning balance of the local road networks were not yet recorded in the books.

As of December 31, 2024, the MA has not prepared any JV for the LRN.


The MA has not prepared nor maintained a subsidiary records for road networks.

(All recommendations were reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 2, page 38 to 41.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant for the timely submission of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) and its corresponding reconciliation statement to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Management has not acted upon with the recommendation.
observations.reason:
No SCBAA was submitted for CY 2024.

(Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 6, page 50 to 51)

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the MA to submit the Disbursement Vouchers, Obligation Requests, Journal Entry Vouchers, and its Supporting Documents relative to the utilization of the 20% Development Fund  to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Management has not acted upon with the recommendation.
observations.reason:
As of December 31, 2024, the management was able to submit Disbursement Vouchers, Obligation Requests, JEVs and other supporting documents relative to the utilization of the 20% Development Fund for January to October but beyond the prescribed period. Moreover, the November and December was not yet submitted.

(Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 8, page 55 to 58)

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the MA to refrain from charging the 30% Quick Response Fund without the support of a valid resolution of the Local Sanggunian declaring the LGU under the state of calamity or a Presidential declaration of state of calamity relevant thereto upon recommendation of the NDRRMC, and to charge proper and eligible disbursements to the 70% Mitigation Fund or unexpended balances of prior-years in accordance with the approved LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction: The Management has not acted upon with the recommendation.
observations.reason:
Inspection of submitted vouchers revealed that expenses charged to the QRF in CY 2024 were still not supported by a Local Sanggunian resolution declaring the Municipality under the State of Calamity or a Presidential declaration of state of calamity.

(Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 9, page 59 to 61)

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit the lacking RCIs and RCDs with the JEVs and its supporting documents and to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of such reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor in order to facilitate the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts
observations.managementAction: The Management has not acted upon with the recommendation.
observations.reason:
The management was unable to comply with the submission of the RCI together with the DVs and supporting documents, RCDs together with the ORs and Validated Slips, and JEVs within the prescribed period.

(Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 13, page 70 to 72)

observations.recommendation:
1.13 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Acting City
Treasurer to:
a) Locate all validated copies of the bank deposit slips that were not
recorded in the books and furnish copies to the Acting City
Accountant; and
b) Submit to the Acting City Accountant all CMs that were not
recorded in the books, otherwise follow up with the concerned
Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) all the CMs and
furnish copies to the Acting City Accountant.

1.14 We also recommended and Management agreed to direct the Acting
City Accountant to:
a) Coordinate with the Acting City Treasurer for the details and
supporting documents of the check deposit that was returned by the
bank, unreconciled items and all other deposits including online
payments that were not recorded so that appropriate journal entries
could be drawn; and
b) Cause the immediate preparation of Journal Entry Voucher (JEV)
on the reconciling items of the CY transaction that require
adjustments.

2.8 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the fair
presentation of cash and liability accounts by:
a) Requiring the Acting City Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of
Unreleased Checks at the end of each reporting period and submit
this to the Acting City Accountant; and
b) Upon receipt of the Schedule of Unreleased Checks, the Acting City
Accountant shall post an adjusting journal entry in the books of the
City in order to reflect the correct amount of cash and liability
accounts at year-end.

3.14 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
a) City General Services Officer to:
 Formulate plans and timelines in the conduct of semestral
physical count of inventories, reconcile the balances, prepare the
RPCI and submit to the Audit Team Leader pursuant to Section
124 of the Manual of NGAS, Volume I for LGUs;
 Secure Certification from the respective requisitioning
department heads for the balance of inventories of all prior
years. Based on the Certification, prepare the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit the same to
the Accounting Office so appropriate adjustments could be
prepared to record the expense incurred; and
 Install a system of procedures on the management of inventories
from requisition to issuance including the maintenance of stock
cards and preparation of the required reports and reconciliation
thereof.

b) Acting City Accountant to:
 Demand from the CGSO the submission of SSMI for the
inventories of prior year’s which were not yet recorded in the
books, particularly those that are dormant for more than eight
years already; and
 Ensure that all inventories utilized, issued and distributed are
recognized as expenses in the year incurred and properly
recorded in the books of accounts so that inventory and expense
accounts are fairly presented in the year-end financial
statements.

4.14 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Direct the CGSO to reconcile the results of their verification and
physical count of Land account with the records of Accounting so
that proper adjustment could be drawn; and
b) Instruct the CGSO and other concerned Departments to
immediately process and expedite the documentation and other
requirements for the transfer of titles of those untitled parcels of
land with the Register of Deeds under the name of the City
Government in order to protect its interest from third party.

5.10 In view of the foregoing, we recommended and the Acting City
Accountant agreed to:
a) Prepare the necessary accounting entry for the recognition of the
completed project to the appropriate PPE account, supported with
relevant reports/documents;
b) Record the corresponding depreciation expense therefore, including
those pertaining to prior years: and
c) Strictly observed the guidelines on the recording of infrastructure
projects under the TF books pursuant to Section 97 of the Manual
on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

6.10 In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed to
direct the:
a) Acting City Accountant to prepare the necessary accounting entries
for the reclassification of the completed projects from CIP accounts to
the appropriate PPE accounts, supported by relevant
reports/documents;
b) Acting City Accountant to record the corresponding depreciation
expense therefore, including those pertaining to prior years as an
adjustment to Government Equity; and
c) CEO and OCA to review and reconcile all ongoing construction
projects from prior years that remain unresolved and lacking status
updates. Additionally, periodic reconciliation should be made
between the CIP ledger accounts and project records to ensure the
fair presentation and accuracy of CIP balances in the financial
statements.

7.9 We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to review and
analyze the details of the PPE accounts, compute the depreciation and
take up the necessary adjusting entry to correct the Government Equity
and the carrying amounts of the 23 PPE items accordingly.

8.10 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed through the
Acting City Accountant to enforce the liquidation of outstanding fund
transfers. If liquidation is not possible, the funds should be refunded to
facilitate the necessary adjusting entries.

9.11 We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to prepare
the necessary adjusting entries to correct the overstatement in the
Subsidy and Accounts Payable accounts, ensuring the fair and accurate
presentation of the financial statement.

10.6 We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to properly
record the five percent DPWH share from collections under the Due to
NGAs account in compliance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS Volume 1
and COA Circular No. 2015 -009 dated December 1, 2015.

Recommendations
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and make the necessary adjusting entries; and
Undertake regular and timely review and reconciliation of the accounts in compliance to the above-mentioned rules and regulations.

Recommendations
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to submit the monthly BRS together with its corresponding Bank Statements and SLs within the prescribed period; and instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries in the books for book reconciling items and to submit the pertinent documents necessary for the said adjustment.

Recommendations
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit the SCBAA and its corresponding reconciliation statement to the Office of the Municipal Accountant; and
Hereafter, require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records to properly present the adjusted amount of expenditure to the SCBAA before submitting the report to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to secure a copy of the certificate of turnover and acceptance from the DPWH to support the recording of the completed building in the books to reflect the correct balance of the PPE and Grants and Donations in Kind revenue accounts.
Correspondingly, depreciation of said recorded asset must be calculated and recognized based on its useful life.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to direct the:
a) City Treasurer to immediately send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the stale checks and hereafter see to it that a written notice is sent to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale; and
b) Acting City Accountant to make the necessary adjustment/s in the books of accounts to restore the liability to creditors pursuant to Section 97 of PD No. 1445 and Section 59, MNGAS for LGU, Volume I.

We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to take up the following adjusting entry as follows:
Debit: Other Payables 411,201.11
Credit: Government Equity 411,201.11
We also recommended and the City Accounting Office agreed to furnish the City Budget Officer of the documents regarding the reversion of the unutilized LDRRMF reverted back to the GF so that the amount could be included in the report of the unappropriated surplus of the GF and include the same in the preparation for the succeeding year’s budget for social services subject to enactment by the local sanggunian.
Henceforth, Management agreed to strictly observe the accounting procedure in the reversion of the unutilized LDRRMF balances after five years under the STF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to prepare adjusting entries to correct the erroneous accounting entries made in CY 2016 of the unexpended LDRRM Fund (STF CY 2013) balance of ₱23.453 million by debiting Trust Liability-LDRRMF and crediting Government Equity.
We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to recognize in the TF books the receipt of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF QRF and MOOE from the GF.

We recommended and the City Treasurer agreed to instruct the person in-charge to follow up with the Bureau of Treasury on the applications for the renewal of bonds of all AOs with expired fidelity bonds and ensure that all AOs are properly bonded.
We recommended and Management agreed through the City Treasurer to increase the fidelity bonds of the concerned AOs upon renewal in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Premium Rates.
observations.reason: Bonds of the remaining AOs will be increased in the next bond renewal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the City Treasurer agreed to direct the concerned accountable officers to remit immediately and intact all collections still in their possession to the liquidating officer. Hereafter, the City Treasurer enforce remittance of collections daily and intact or once collections reached ₱5,000.00 for those in the field pursuant to Section 29 of COA Circular 92-382 dated July 3, 1992 and other existing regulations.
We also recommended and the City Treasurer agreed to closely monitor the remittance of collections by the accountable officers to safeguard funds from possible loss due to misapplication, misuse, theft, or robbery or personal use.

We recommended and the City Mayor and/or the City Accountant agreed to strictly observe the guidelines on the limitations in granting the cash advances for CF and the submission of vouchers within seven days to the COA ATL in consonance with the applicable provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG - DND Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
a) City General Services Officer to:
i. Formulate plans, strategies and timelines in the conduct of semestral physical count of inventories, reconcile the balances, prepare the RPCI and submit to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual of NGAS, Volume I for LGUs;
ii. Require all requisitioning office to submit the Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) of all prior years issued, consumed and distributed inventories, prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit the same to the Accounting Office so appropriate adjustments could be prepared to record the expense incurred;
iii. Maintain supplies stock cards for each type of supplies procured and reconcile these with the ledger cards maintained by the Accounting Office to effectively monitor any loss or misuse of the inventory items; and
iv. Install a system of procedures on management of inventories from requisition to issuance and preparation of the required reports.
b) City Accountant to periodically remind the CGSO of the weekly submission of SSMI and RIS, and also demand the submission of prior year’s issuances which were not yet recorded in the books, particularly those that were non-moving for the six years.
We recommended and Management agreed that Cash in Bank balance with PNB under the Trust Fund – PCF be reactivated, make the necessary correcting entry, and transfer the funds to appropriate account.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a) City Engineer (a) conduct an investigation on all infrastructure projects with negative slippage and expired contracts and identify the causes thereof and submit to the City Mayor the results for appropriate action; and (b) adopt and strictly implement policy for addressing negative slippage; and
b) City Mayor (a) refer the result of the investigation to the Legal Office for appropriate legal action; and (b) henceforth, make sure that all infrastructure projects are implemented in accordance with the time frame as stipulated in the contract agreement and that any negative slippage is addressed accordingly; and
c) BAC disqualify all subsequent bids of contractors at fault, if warranted, pursuant to existing rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Out of seven contract projects, two were fully implemented, one is on-going and two were terminated. Unutilized funds for the two terminated projects were already returned to the DILG.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor facilitate the issuance of the appropriate Executive/Administrative Order establishing the City’s GAD M&E Team, formalize the monitoring of GAD related activities and ensure its success pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the City Treasurer institutes corresponding actions to collect from delinquent taxpayers the amount involved otherwise, perform proper cancellation of returned checks and coordinate with the OCA for appropriate adjustments in the books.

We recommended that the City Health Officer facilitate the immediate distribution and use of larvicides/insecticides and test kits to the intended recipients/beneficiaries for the immediate prevention and control of dengue epidemic, thus, 30% Quick Response Fund be properly used and applied, and prevent possible loss or waste of government funds and resources.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to account for the total government share given to the Provident Fund since the time of the creation of the fund in 1995 up to December 31, 2009.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following: 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the purchase of supplies and materials for stock, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period, as inventory following the perpetual inventory method; to prepare JEVs based on transaction documents presented in accordance with Section 31 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II; and to carefully analyze the transactions before taking them up in the books of accounts in order to properly account for the acquisition and issuance of the supplies and materials inventory based on IPSAS 12, Inventories and Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009;

Instruct the MGSO and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the general process to be followed in the control of inventory as provided in Section 122 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; to properly prepare and maintain complete and updated Stock Cards (SCs) and Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs); and to reconcile their records regularly; and

Require the Inventory Committee to submit Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
observations.managementAction:
The Management has complied with the said recommendations.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to do the following:
Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory count of all PPE and submit an updated and corrected Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006, dated January 31, 2020.

The Municipal Accountant should review previous records to come up with the purchase amount of PPEs on the prior year’s.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the following department heads’ compliance with the requirements in COA Circular 2015-008:
Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting procedures:
Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively;
Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
Direct the General Services Officer-Designate to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements together with its corresponding Bank Statements and Subsidiary Ledgers within the reglementary period;  and to instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries in the books for book reconciling items and to submit the pertinent documents necessary for the said adjustment.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the purchase of supplies and materials for stock, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period, as inventory following the perpetual inventory method; to prepare JEVs based on transaction documents presented in accordance with Section 31 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II; and to carefully analyze the transactions before taking them up in the books of accounts in order to properly account for the acquisition and issuance of the supplies and materials inventory based on IPSAS 12, Inventories and Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009;
Instruct the MGSO and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the general process to be followed in the control of inventory as provided in Section 122 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; to properly prepare and maintain complete and updated Stock Cards (SCs) and Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs); and to reconcile their records regularly; and
Require the Inventory Committee to submit Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to stop the outright recording of 30% QRF of current-year appropriations as Trust Liabilities – DRRM in the Trust Fund books and to record the allocation of current-year appropriations of the 5% LDRRMF comprising of the 70% Mitigation Fund and the 30% Quick Response Fund and the charging of expenses thereto in the General Fund books;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to reflect the actual unexpended/unobligated balances in the Special Trust Fund in the Trust Liabilities-DRRM account; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate Subsidiary Ledgers for the 70% Mitigation Fund and the 30% Quick Response Fund in the General Fund Books, and the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit the separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) or the SAAOB for the QRF, MOOE, and Capital Outlay.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Engineer to verify the on-going PPAs of the Municipality in order to come up with the updated Report on Government PPAs which shall be submitted quarterly to the Auditor; and to require the Municipal Accountant to review the CIP balances of each funds, determine those that have been completed in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, and to comply with the proper reclassification to PPE based on the accounting guidelines set forth in the foregoing provisions.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant for the timely submission of the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) and its corresponding reconciliation statement to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
a) City General Services Officer to:
i. Formulate plans, strategies and timelines in the conduct of semestral physical count of inventories, reconcile the balances, prepare the RPCI and submit to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual of NGAS, Volume I for LGUs; ii. Require all requisitioning office to submit the Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) of all prior years issued, consumed and distributed inventories, prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit the same to the Accounting Office so appropriate adjustments could be prepared to record the expense incurred; iii. Maintain supplies stock cards for each type of supplies procured and reconcile these with the ledger cards maintained by the Accounting Office to effectively monitor any loss or misuse of the inventory items; and iv. Install a system of procedures on management of inventories from requisition to issuance and preparation of the required reports.
b) City Accountant to:
i. Periodically remind the CGSO of the weekly submission of SSMI and RIS, and also demand the submission of prior year’s issuances which were not yet recorded in the books, particularly those that were non-moving for the six years; and ii. Ensure that all inventory utilized, issued and distributed are recognized as expenses in the year incurred and properly recorded in the books of accounts so that inventory and expense accounts are fairly presented in the year-end financial statements.

b) Expedite the preparation of additional documents to facilitate the request for authority to write-off of long outstanding cash advances, which has remained non-moving for more than ten years and where settlement/accountability could no longer be made, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
c) Ensure that all cash advances to officers and employees are liquidated within the prescribed periods pursuant to Item 5.0 COA Circular No. 97-002 and no additional cash advance shall be granted to any official or employee unless a settlement of the previous cash advance or a proper accounting thereof has been made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card and to furnish the Municipal Accountant the certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible to support the recording of the RPT and SET receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accounting Office to submit to COA, certified true copies of the lacking supporting documents for further evaluation of the transactions and to demand from the vendor the execution of the Deed of Absolute Sale for a valid sale and to effect conveyance and the transfer of title to the Municipal government.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has complied with the said recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to utilize the 20% Development Fund exclusively for the specific projects for which they have been appropriated, and to require prior authority and approval from the Sanggunian for contracts entered by the LCE which are not part of the approved appropriation ordinance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has complied with the said recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to complete the details that should be provided in the Obligation Request in compliance with the instruction in filling up the Obligation Request as provided in COA Circular 2006-002 dated January 31, 2006.
observations.managementAction: The new Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) assumed the position on August 10, 2023 after her designation resulting from the retirement of the previous MBO. The new MBO has complied with the said recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to comply with the regulations and furnish the Audit Team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within five (5) days after the perfection thereof for the timely auditorial review of the same. Likewise, for those beyond the threshold of the audit team, after auditorial and legal review, it will be forwarded to the Technical Audit Sector at the COA Regional Office
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has complied with the said recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
a.) Adhere strict compliance with the provisions prescribed by Section 325(a) of R.A. No. 7160, Section 14 of Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 and Section 4.1 and 4.2 of Local Budget Circular No. 98 dated October 14, 2011 instead of hearing recommendations from a co-personnel.  

b.) Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Local Finance Committee to perform a thorough review and evaluation of the budget, whether annual or supplemental, prior to its submission to the Sangguniang Bayan to ensure that all Personal Services items are properly identified and classified as PS and that appropriation for PS items shall not exceed the PS limitation at any point in time during the budget year. 

c) Make an effort in raising revenue/receipts to be able to implement and provide additional PS costs or by reducing its PS expenses.

d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly record the RATA to proper personal services accounts in order to present the actual PS expenditures of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The recommendations were discussed with the Local Finance Committee, and that the Municipal Annual Budget was reviewed and deliberated during the committee meeting before its scheduled submission to the Sangguniang Bayan for approval.
observations.reason: The Municipality appropriated RATA under the MOOE in the CY 2021 Annual Budget. Hence, disbursements relating thereto have also been made for the same year.

observations.recommendation:
a.)	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit disbursement vouchers after payment with complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant.  	

b.)	Direct the Municipal Accountant to: 
strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.

closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the latter to submit immediately the required reports within the prescribed period, and to facilitate the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.

c.) Augment additional personnel even Job Orders who are Accounting Majors to assist in the preparation of the reports and possibly train them both in the Treasury and the Accounting Department.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was advised to comply with the submission of the financial reports to COA within the time frame, as well as the Municipal Treasurer to submit disbursement  vouchers after payment with complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant.  The  hiring Job Order personnel who can help in the preparation of the reports and train them both in the Treasury and the Accounting Department was also discussed.

Ensure that the 20% Development Fund of the Municipality be utilized only to fund priority programs or projects included in the approved Annual Investment Plan (AIP) which partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in compliance and in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 to ensure its optimal utilization.

b.)	Require the MPDC to prepare and submit to the audit team the accomplishment report for the 20% development fund.
