observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to do the following:
Direct the Inventory Committee to comply with the guidelines, procedures, and roles of the department heads in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.
Instruct the General Services Officer/Supply Officer-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate classification of Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to review previous records to come up with the purchase amounts of PPEs in the prior years.
Require the General Services Officer/Supply Officer-Designate to issue property tags/ stickers for each PPE in order to identify and trace the location of the said PPEs and to issue corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for all the Properties, Plant and Equipment issued to officials and employees who have the custody thereof and to facilitate such undertaking, and assign additional competent personnel in order to complete the needed property records.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a) Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
b) Direct the General Services Officer-Designate to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map and a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and their components; and
c) Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting procedures:
i) Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry.
ii) Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
iii) Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
d) Require the Concerned Personnel to attend training and seminar on Local Road Asset Management to ensure compliance with the transitory provision under COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We recommended to Local Chief Executive to:
Strictly comply with Section 325(a) of R.A. No. 7160, Section 14 of Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 and Section 4.1 and 4.2 of Local Budget Circular No. 98 dated October 14, 2011.
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Local Finance Committee to perform a thorough review and evaluation of the budget, whether annual or supplemental, prior to its submission to the Sangguniang Bayan to ensure that all Personal Service items are properly identified and classified as PS and that appropriation for PS items shall not exceed the PS limitation at any point in time during the budget year.
Make an effort in raising revenue/receipts to be able to implement and provide additional PS costs by reducing its PS expenses.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly record the RATA in order to present the actual PS expenditures of the Municipality.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive as Chairman of the LSB to:
Direct the concerned local officials to provide written justifications/ explanations on the disbursements as to how they relate to the proper utilization of SEF and to submit the lacking supporting documents as discussed.
Refrain from charging to SEF the programs/projects/activities that are not authorized under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and implement the proper programs in order to maximize the use of the SEF for its intended purpose only which shall mean government financed and managed schools which include pre-school, elementary, secondary schools, national and general comprehensive high schools and their annexes and those public elementary and secondary schools established by law and should not include private educational institutions.
Require the Municipal Accountant to review the charging of the Special Education Fund strictly in accordance with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations, and to submit each transaction with complete documentation and signatures;
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit SEF Quarterly Utilization report faithfully as required under DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Ensure that the 20% Development Fund of the Municipality be utilized only to fund priority programs or projects included in the approved Annual Investment Plan (AIP) which partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in compliance and in accordance with the provisions of DILG- DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 to ensure its optimal utilization.
To require the Municipal Accountant to support any disbursements with proper and complete documentation.
Require the Municipal Planning & Development Coordinator to prepare and submit to the audit team the accomplishment report for the 20% development fund.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents for CY 2021 to the Office of the Auditor, and to strictly adhere to the prompt submission of said documents for the ensuing years to enable the timely post-audit of the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and GAD Focal Person to use the revised GAD Plan and Budget.
Direct the GAD Focal Person to accompany GAD Accomplishment Report with the brief summary of the reported program or project, copies of the reported policy issuances, results of the HGDG – PIMME/FIMME checklist for PPAs attributed to GAD Budget, and Report on the Actions taken by the LGU on the COA Audit findings and recommendations
Require the GAD Technical Working Group:
Require that the provisions of the HGDG be complied with by subjecting the GAD PPAs to the GAD Checklist during the project identification and designing programs to determine whether the same fully address gender related issues and HGDG test be conducted at the end of project implementation and incorporate the results in determining the percentage of PPA expenditures attributable to GAD.
Diligently monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs.

We recommended to Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
Explain why these checks were not covered with disbursement vouchers and were not supported by documents.
Submit the aforementioned Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor;
Make sure that all payments are duly covered by an approved disbursement vouchers and supported with complete documentary requirements.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
i.  Strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
ii. Closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the latter to submit immediately the required reports within the prescribed period, and to facilitate the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit disbursement vouchers after payment with complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and the OIC-Municipal Engineer to comply with the regulations and furnish the Audit Team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within five (5) days after the perfection thereof for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same. Likewise, for those beyond the threshold of the audit team, after auditorial and legal review, it will be forwarded to the Technical Audit Sector at the COA, Regional Office X.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the to submit monthly pre-closing trial balances for each fund shall be submitted not later than the twentieth day after the end of the month, as well as the schedule of subsidiary ledger balances of the controlling accounts in the General Ledger as supporting document for the trial balances at the end of the quarter; and
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit Monthly Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations, for both the current and continuing appropriations, not later than the twentieth day after the end of the month.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following: 

a.)	Direct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the preparation and submission of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements to achieve an accurate financial reporting of Cash in Bank account and reconcile with the Bank Reconciliation with the general ledgers. Also require him to get the monthly bank statements from the depository bank promptly and regularly;
	
b.)	Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to reconcile his cashbooks entries with that from the accounting records at least quarterly; and

c.)	Give authority to the Municipal Accountant to enroll in LBP i-access so that bank balances can be viewed and printed anytime with the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have submitted the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS), however, majority of the BRS were submitted all at once in 2022. As per Municipal Accountant, the possibility of enrolling in LBP i-access has been discussed and studied with Local Finance Committee.

observations.recommendation:
Create an Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory count of all PPE and submit an updated and corrected Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). 

 Instruct the General Services Officer/Supply Officer-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate classification of Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

The Municipal Accountant should review previous records to come up with the purchase amounts of PPEs on the prior year’s. 

Require the General Services Officer/Supply Officer-Designate to issue property tags/ stickers for each PPE in order to identify and trace the location of the said PPEs and to issue corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for all the Properties, Plant and Equipment issued to officials and employees who have the custody thereto and to facilitate such undertaking, assign additional competent personnel in order to complete the needed property records.
observations.managementAction: The Property Officer – Designate has initially conducted partial physical count of PPE, however, due to her concurring work in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, the continuous conduct of physical inventory would be difficult. Also, the Property Officer initially issued property stickers for various PPE owned by the Municipality  and made Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for issued PPEs.
observations.reason: The conduct of the Inventory Committee of the physical count of PPE is still ingoing.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;

Direct the General Services Officer-Designate to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and

Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting procedures:

i.)	Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry. 

ii.)	Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and 
		
iii.)	Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

d.)	Require the Concerned Personnel to attend training and seminar on Local Road Asset Management to ensure compliance with the transitory provision under COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive tasked the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with the complete description and cost of all road projects. Further, the Municipality has submitted a local road network plan to the Province. The Province then consolidated the status of the road networks by Municipalities.
observations.reason: The LGU of Bacolod have data as of CY2021 and will submit a copy of the existing local road networks to the Municipal Accountant but with lacking data for the civil works from prior year.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the head of Human Resource Management Office to submit the complete contract of service of the job orders with specific duties and functions and reconcile the manpower complement and monthly compensation based on the actual and instruct to use the computer-generated DTRs based on the captured biometric date as basis for their preparation of payrolls for the establishment of a reliable number of days worked as the basis for the computation of their wages. 

Comply with the aforementioned rules and regulations in the hiring of Job Orders, prepare and issue policies and guidelines to address the issue on the necessity and propriety of hiring JOs. Also, examine the reasons for the increase number of job order personnel and the need to hire such personnel. 

Immediately strengthen the controls in the hiring of job order by requiring them to submit personal records, undergo interview and document the evaluation of performance to give assurance that the risks identified above are addressed. 

Consider either one of the following:

Appoint Job Order personnel as regular employees if they are qualified and believe that their services are essential and contributory to the improvement of agency’s operations or, 

Stop hiring Job Order and fill up the vacant plantilla positions in accordance with the Civil Service Commission rules and regulations. Prioritize the division with undermanned in the filling-up of vacant plantilla position. 

Formulate a policy and internal control in the hiring of job order workers such as but not limited to requiring specific and detailed description of duties and responsibilities to eliminate workings having no definite purpose, if any. Hiring of job order personnel be based on necessity in order to avoid waste of much needed funds. 

f.) Submit justification on the sources of fund for the excessive payment of Job Orders compared to its budgeted amount. Also, explain why other plantilla positions remained vacant/unfilled.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive directed the Human Resource Management Officer to require all Job Order personnel to prepare and submit duly signed and verified DTR and accomplishment report and the complete contract of service of the job orders with specific duties and functions. Because of the pandemic, the biometric system was suspended for the time being. A logbook was instead maintained in each office for the recording of time in/time out of the JO personnel as basis for the computation of their wages. Pursuant to the provisions of the Labor Code, the Local Chief Executive instructed the Human Resource Management Officer to recommend the regularization of Job Order personnel as regular employees if they are eligible and qualified where their services are necessary or desirable to the operation of the local government unit, and to prepare and issue policies and guidelines to address the issue on the necessity and propriety of hiring JOs in accordance with the Civil Service Commission rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to require all Job Order personnel to prepare and submit duly signed and verified DTR. 
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims should be supported with complete supporting documents and duly signed by appropriate signatories before payments are made by Municipal Treasurer or Special Disbursing Officer.
observations.managementAction: The Human Resource Management Officer required the Job Order personnel to prepare and submit DTR, Accomplishment Report and Contract of Service upon claiming their monthly wages as supporting documents duly signed by their office head and the Municipal Mayor.

observations.recommendation:
a) Direct the Municipality’s officers and employees to impose proper application of the entitlement of traveling allowance as well as completeness and regularity of the essential documents like travel orders, bus tickets, e-ticket, boarding pass and itinerary of travel. 

b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely review the liquidation reports submitted by the officers and employees and require immediate refund for excessive claims.
observations.managementAction: Employees who claimed for advances for travelling expenses were instructed to impose proper application of the entitlement of traveling allowance as well as completeness and regularity of the essential documents like travel orders, bus tickets, e-ticket, boarding pass and itinerary of travel. The Municipal Accountant was also directed to require the officers and employees who have travel claims the submission of liquidation reports with regard to their expenditures during their travel.
The Property Officer issued property tags/ stickers for various PPEs in order to identify and trace the location of the said PPEs and issued corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE)

observations.recommendation:
Implement an adequate monitoring system that tracks the loan payables of its employees from authorized agencies.

Direct the Municipal Accountant the following to:
Refrain from approving the loan applications of those employees whose net take pays are below the mandated minimum net take home pay and
Implement the P5,000.00 net take home pay required for each employee as provided under the General Provisions of the General Appropriation Act (GAA) for FY 2019 also known as Republic Act No. 11260.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive required the Municipal Accountant to disapprove loan applications of those employees whose net take pays are below the mandated minimum net take home pay and implement the P5,000.00 net take home pay required for each employee as provided under the General Provisions of the General Appropriation Act (GAA) for FY 2019 also known as Republic Act No. 11260.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to remit promptly the balances of liabilities due to the government agencies/corporations concerned in order not to compromise the benefits that is due to the intended beneficiaries and at the same time avoid penalties/surcharges arising from late payments. It shall be made on or before the due date to avoid incurrence of unnecessary and irregular expenditures. 

b.) Direct the Office of The Municipal Treasurer to explain the delayed payment of remittances, if proven to be out of neglect or laxity of duty, refund the amount paid for the penalties and interest.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive advised the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to promptly make payments on or before the due date to avoid incurrence of unnecessary and irregular expenditures.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and the OIC-Municipal Engineer to comply with the regulations and furnish the Audit Team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within five (5) days after the perfection thereof for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same. Likewise, for those beyond the threshold of the audit team, after auditorial and legal review, it will be forwarded to the Technical Audit Sector at the COA, Regional Office X. Otherwise, the Audit Team will issue Notice of Suspension.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive directed the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and the OIC-Municipal Engineer to comply with the regulations and furnish the Audit Team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within five (5) days after its perfection. And to forward to the Technical Audit Sector at the COA, Regional Office X, those beyond the threshold of the audit team, after auditorial and legal review.
observations.reason: Various copies of contracts were submitted beyond the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
a.) Direct the General Services Officer-Designate to accomplish properly and religiously the purchase orders and see to it that the required conditions are indicated therein. 

b.) Submit the purchase orders to the Office of the Auditor irrespective of the amount within five (5) working days pursuant to the abovementioned regulations.

c.) Submit copies of inspection of deliveries of goods and services within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance to enable this office to conduct timely review and validation.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer-Designate was directed to prepare properly  the purchase orders and submit them to the Office of the Auditor irrespective of the amount within five (5) working days pursuant to the abovementioned regulations. Copies of inspection of deliveries of goods and services are also to be submitted within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance to enable this office to conduct timely review and validation.
observations.reason: Various purchase orders were submitted beyond the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
a.) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents to ensure the completeness and propriety of the required supporting documents for all disbursements using the Bayanihan Grant to Municipalities.

b.) Advice the BAC, Municipal Accountant and other Department Heads to be well-informed and well-equipped of the new laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive directed the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents for all disbursements using the Bayanihan Grant to Municipalities.

observations.recommendation:
a.)	Direct the Acting Human Resource Management Officer to publish the vacancy of MHO position until an interested applicant has manifested his intent.

b.)	Demand the Municipal Mayor the immediate filling-up of MHO vacant positions for a more effective implementation of programs projects on health and prompt delivery of the health services needed by the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Human Resource Management Officer was instructed to publish the vacancy of MHO position so that it will be immediately filled up for a more effective implementation of programs projects on health and prompt delivery of the health services needed by the constituents.

observations.recommendation:
Orient the Local School Board on the eligible expenditures allowed on the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations.  

Require the Local School Board to strictly follow the provisions of RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars No. 1, s. 2017 in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto. Any issues or conflicts arising from its implementation and cases not covered by the provisions shall be submitted to the DepEd for resolution and in consultation with the municipal government.

Refrain from charging to SEF the programs/projects/activities that are not authorized under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and implement the same in order to maximize the use of the SEF for its intended purpose only which shall mean government financed and managed schools which include pre-school, elementary, secondary schools, national and general comprehensive high schools and their annexes and those public elementary and secondary schools established by law and should not include private educational institutions.

Have a conscious and concerted effort of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute.

Require the Municipal Accountant to review the charging of the Special Education Fund strictly in accordance with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations.

f) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit SEF Quarterly Utilization report faithfully as required under DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive, also the Chairman of the Local School Board,  acquainted the LSB with regard to the eligible expenditures allowed by law and to strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars No. 1, s. 2017 in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget. The Municipal Accountant was also instructed to prepare and submit SEF Quarterly Utilization report faithfully as required under DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017.
observations.reason: Various expenditures

observations.recommendation: Require the LDRRMO Officer and other concerned employees to submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund, Separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations and the Annual DRRM Plan, Budget and Programs to the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO Officer was instructed to submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.
observations.reason: The separate Registry of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations, for the LDRRMF were not submitted. Also, Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents for CY 2021 were not completely submitted, as stated in Finding No. 7 in this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and GAD Focal Person to use the revised GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report.

Require the GAD Technical Working Group: 
that the provisions of the HGDG be complied with by subjecting the GAD PPAs to the GAD Checklist during the project identification and designing programs to determine whether the same fully address gender related issues and HGDG test be conducted at the end of project implementation and incorporate the results in determining the percentage of PPA expenditures attributable to GAD.

to diligently monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and GAD Focal Person was instructed to use the revised GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report. Furthermore, they were reminded that the provisions of the HGDG shall be strictly complied with and that the implementation of GAD PPAs shall be diligently monitored.
observations.reason: The submitted GAD Accomplishment Report, it was observed that it was not accompanied by the following: a) Brief summary of the reported program or project; b) Copies of the reported policy issuances; c) Results of the HGDG – PIMME/FIMME checklist for PPAs attributed to GAD Budget; and d) Report on the Actions taken by the LGU on the COA Audit findings and recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to initiate the closure of dormant accounts by securing the approval of the Sangguniang Panlungsod, and remitting it to the National Treasury, if necessary.

Further, we recommended and Management agreed that Cash in Bank balance with PNB under the Trust Fund – PCF be reactivated, make the necessary correcting entry, and transfer the funds to appropriate account.
We recommended and Management agreed to increase the fidelity bonds of the Accountable Officers accordingly be adjusted upon renewal in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Premium Rates.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the concerned personnel/department to conduct periodic physical count of supplies and materials and submit Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to concerned offices not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively.

Furthermore, we recommended and Management agreed that the personnel in-charge of respective departments with non-moving inventory accounts totaling ₱99,358,684.29 prepare and submit the necessary Reports of Supplies and Materials Issuances to the Accounting Office which shall be the basis of the adjustments on the accounts and accurately reflect the correct amount in the financial statements.

We also recommended and Management agreed that the GSO shall maintain supplies stock cards for each type of supplies procured, and reconcile these with the ledger cards maintained by the Accounting Office to effectively check the integrity of custodianship.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant:

Demand the immediate liquidation of financial assistance from the barangays and validate their compliance with the terms and conditions in the MOA; and
Refrain from releasing any subsequent fund transfers to barangays with unliquidated balance.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the concerned offices to submit the documents necessary for the liquidation of the CAs of the stated accountable officers. The AOs should immediately prepare and submit the LRs with complete documentation and refund excess CA, if any. Henceforth the City Accountant should ensure and monitor the prompt liquidation of all CAs.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the concerned offices to submit the documents necessary for the liquidation of the CAs of the stated accountable officers. The AOs should immediately prepare and submit the LRs with complete documentation and refund excess CA, if any. Henceforth the City Accountant should ensure and monitor the prompt liquidation of all CAs.

We recommended and the Acting City Accountant and Management agreed that necessary adjusting entries should be made to recognize the advance payment made for the purchase of the COVID-19 vaccines pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We also recommended and Management agreed to record first the procured and delivered COVID-19 vaccines as inventories to recognize the existence of assets, and as expense only when it is already consumed or used.

Further, we recommended and Management agreed that vaccine stock cards should be maintained by the Cold Room Coordinator for the LGU procured COVID-19 vaccines for reconciliation with the ledger cards at the Office of the City Accountant so that correctness of the balances can be easily verified and validated.

We recommended and Management agreed to make representation with the national government agency in charge of the vaccination program to immediately issue clear guidelines as to the proper disposal of expired vaccines if an extension of emergency use authority (EUA) cannot be warranted.

We also recommended and Management agreed through the Cold Room Coordinator to observe and expedite the use of about to expire vaccines to prevent wastage, and formulate internal control policies necessary to minimize the occurrence of expired/damaged vaccines.

We further recommended and Management agreed that reverse logistics activity be made for the return of empty and defective vials for possible recovery and/or replacement with the supplier.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:

For the Cold Room Coordinator to furnish the Accounting Office with a detailed list of allocated vaccines received by the City;
For donations received with costs, the City Accountant shall recognize in the books the donations in accordance with existing regulations; and
For donations received without costs, the City Accountant shall recognize the donations at their fair value as at the date of receipt, which may be ascertained by reference to an active market, or by appraisal as provided in Paragraph 97 of the IPSAS No. 23.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Health Officer in coordination with the City Accountant, shall submit the liquidation report for the fund transfers and refund the unutilized balance to DOH as stipulated in the MOA.

We recommended and Management agreed to demand the immediate submission of Fund Utilization Report from the recipient LGUs and ascertain that the funds were utilized based on the purpose intended for.
Henceforth, we recommended and Management agreed to ensure that required documentary requirements are duly attached to the disbursement vouchers before approving any release of transfers of funds pursuant to existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to record the acquired asset in the Trust Fund books and subsequently transfer it to the General Fund books with the following correcting entries:
Under the books of Trust Fund:
Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment	14,190,000.00
Other Payables						14,190,000.00
To record purchase of emergency response vehicle
Trust Liability – LDRRMF		14,190,000.00
Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment			14,190,000.00
To record transfer of emergency response vehicle to Gen. Fund
Under the books of General Fund:
Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment	14,190,000.00
Equity						14,190,000.00
To record receipt of emergency response vehicle transferred from Trust Fund

We recommended and Management agreed to recognize the financial assistance of this kind as trust liability in the General Fund book in consonance with DBM issuances.

Likewise, the City Budget Officer must properly appropriate the funds received through budget legislation following pertinent provisions of DBM circular and the related PPAs and, expenses should be included in the Annual Investment Plan of the City.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
City Engineer (a) conduct an investigation on all infrastructure projects with negative slippage and expired contracts and identify the causes thereof and submit to the City Mayor the results for appropriate action; and (b) adopt and strictly implement policy guidelines in addressing negative slippage;
City Mayor (a) refer the result of the investigation to the Legal Office for appropriate legal action; and (b) henceforth, make sure that all infrastructure projects are implemented in accordance with the time frame as stipulated in the contract agreement and that any negative slippage is addressed accordingly; and
BAC disqualify all subsequent bids of contractors at fault, if warranted, pursuant to the existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to facilitate the issuance of the appropriate Executive/Administrative Order establishing the City’s GAD M&E Team, formalize the monitoring of GAD related activities and ensure its success pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
We recommended and Management agreed to submit on time to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe and require the posting of relevant information on government PPAs as provided under COA Circular No. 2013-004 to ensure transparency and accountability on implemented infrastructure and non-infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the City Treasurer institutes corresponding actions to collect from delinquent taxpayers the amount involved otherwise, perform proper cancellation of returned checks and coordinate with the OCA for appropriate adjustments in books.

Further, inform all Collecting Officers under his jurisdiction on the list of taxpayers whose checks were previously dishonored to prevent the same incident to happen in the future.
observations.managementAction: Dishonored checks totaling ₱942,217.79 have already been redeemed by taxpayers concerned. The drawers of the remaining checks worth P 84,351.56 however, cannot yet be located as they might have closed their businesses. The City Treasurer’s Office will be advised to provide the Office of the City Accountant with the information and documents needed for appropriate income adjustments in the books for these “bounced” checks that have not been redeemed to date.
observations.reason: The remaining dishonored checks worth ₱84,351.56 will be subject to appropriate adjusting entries on the income accounts in the books once supporting documents have been completely gathered and provided to the Office of the City Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The City Accountant, together with the City Treasurer, review and analyze prior years’ reconciling items and exert effort effecting required adjustments; and

The City Treasurer coordinate with the concerned depository banks the complete submission of necessary documents related to any reconciling items.
observations.managementAction: Several adjustments have already been made: (181,499.98) for CY 2019 and   ₱16,237,382.15 for CY 2020.
observations.reason: Supporting documents needed for reconciliation of the remaining amount representing items from many years past are still being gathered.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The OCA periodically coordinate with the Office of the City Treasurer regarding the existence of stale checks;

The City Treasurer send written notices to the payee of the existence of stale checks and exert effort by sending notices to the concerned payee to return the stale checks in their possession for cancellation and replacement; and

In the event that stale checks are unclaimed by the payee, the City Treasurer cancel the said checks, furnishing the original Disbursement Voucher (DV) and its supporting documents to the Accounting for appropriate adjustment in the books.
observations.managementAction: Of the stale checks in question, ₱227,333.26 worth were already adjusted. The remaining ₱90,802.10 will be adjusted as soon as the City Treasurer’s Office is able to provide the Office of the City Accountant with the original DVs and its supporting documents.
observations.reason: Awaiting for the City Treasurer to compile the original DVs and its supporting documents for the remaining stale checks totaling ₱90,802.10 so that the Office of the City Accountant can pass the proper adjusting entries to the income accounts in the books of the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant prepare the aging of dormant account receivables in the prescribed form, and exert efforts in securing the required supporting documents and other data applicable to present conditions so that request for authority to write-off of dormant receivables maybe filed pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Accountant submitted the request to write-off dormant accounts to the COA. The dormant balances of Due from NGAs & GOCCs emanated from the implementation of eNGAS in 2007 & there were already no details then on the said dormant accounts, hence the difficulty in providing supporting documents.
observations.reason: For those amounts exceeding ₱100,000.00, the request was forwarded to the Office of the Regional Director.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the City Treasurer agreed to increase the fidelity bond of AO with insufficient coverage in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Premium Rates.

We recommended that the concerned AOs return the unused cash and/or to submit all documents to fully liquidate their cash advances immediately.

We also recommended that the City Accountant monitor the cash advances and send written notices to the Accountable Officers to settle their past due cash advances, and effect the necessary correcting entries to record the cash advances to appropriate funds and keenly review journal entries onwards before approving the same.
observations.managementAction: Of the cash advance in question, ₱160,959,113.75 or 87.51% thereof have already been liquidated. The remaining amount of ₱22,970,500.00 is still on process for liquidation. Being followed up with the Special Disbursing Officers concerned.
observations.reason:
Special Disbursing Officers have been multi-tasking during the pandemic leaving them with little time to attend to the tedious work of compiling the supporting documentary requirements for liquidation of the remaining ₱22,970,500.00. They have committed to complete their liquidations shortly.

Breakdown as follows:

Lordielyn Joy S. Pereyra – 6,000,000.00
Vi Augustos Ceasar Lopez – 11,970,500.00
Jocelyn Abao – 5,000,000.00
Total – 22,970,500.00

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City strictly follow the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and the relevant GPPB issuances in the procurement of needed goods and supplies in times of pandemic to ensure transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process.

We recommended that the Office of the City Accountant ensure that the required complete supporting documents were attached to the liquidation report for ease of verification, confirmation, and validation of the purchases made out of BGCM Funds.
observations.managementAction: The SDO reasoned out that her task was to ensure that delivery was complete according to specifications, inspected and accepted by the end-user as payment. The CHO as end-user attached the issuance slips on the recipients of the goods.
observations.reason: As per verification, no additional documents were submitted to us for the deficiencies noted in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the BAC Secretariat agreed to require duly complied Omnibus Sworn Statements from the suppliers and strictly require the submission of the OSS for subsequent procurement pursuant to the requirement of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR and GPPB issuances.

We recommended and the City Engineer agreed to submit the copy of the approved variation order for the Improvement of Bio-Marine and Toxicology Office (DOH-CLMMRH and Bacolod City Biosafety Laboratory Testing Center for COVID-19 amounting to ₱1,275,364.59 and all the documents forming part thereof, otherwise final payment of the project will be suspended in audit.

We also recommended that management ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 on the submission of copy of government contracts and other documents to the Commission on Audit at all times.

We recommended that the City Accountant recognize and record the acquired asset of ₱112,100,000.00 in the Trust Fund books and subsequently transfer it to the General Fund books. Ensure that subsequent acquisitions charged against Trust Fund are properly recorded pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the concerned SDOs agreed to submit the lacking signed payrolls to established validity and propriety to the payment, otherwise refund the amount of ₱408,000.00 representing excess SAP beneficiaries reported as paid.

We recommended that the City through the Emergency Operations Center to expedite the use of about to expire kits to prevent wastage, and formulate internal control policies necessary to minimize the occurrence of expired swab kits such as establishing adequate control on custodianship, issuances and stock level monitoring through the maintenance of inventory database or computerized system to facilitate retrieval of data on inventory balances, actual condition of stocks, and expiry date at any given time.  
We also recommended that the City Health Office exercise prudence in the use of government resources by making sure that purchase of supplies limited to what is urgently needed.

We further recommended that the City Mayor’s Office as the requesting office of the said purchase review the contract with supplier with regard to the subject expired Swab Kits, and consider returning the excess VTM kits delivered, otherwise do not pay the VTM Kits delivered in excess of the contracted quantity.

We recommended and the management agreed to strictly enforce the timely compliance of the preparation, posting and notification requirements within the prescribed period as required under with Section 4 of the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020. 

The concerned officials should look into the cause of the delay in the preparation and posting of accumulated reports and take necessary action to address the problem.

observations.managementAction: Updating of records at the GSO & reconciliation between the GSO and the Office of the City Accountant is on-going.
observations.reason: Difficulty in accessing support documents due to voluminous files involved, especially for those items acquired or disposed of many years ago. On-going construction of GSO Bodega will help organize these old records more systematically for easier research.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

Direct the City Accountant and the City Engineer’s Office to exert extra efforts for the speedy reconciliation of their records regarding completed and on-going projects; and

Address the problems encountered on unimplemented projects preferably on six projects with total appropriation of ₱31,850,000.00, and instruct the City Engineer to speed up its implementation so that constituents who are beneficiaries of the projects will enjoy the benefits therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Contractors refused to continue implementing the projects awarded to them unless their partial billings are paid first. However, the City Legal Office (CLO) has advised them to file a petition for money claim with the COA and get a decision favorable to them first so our LGU can pay for their partial and future work accomplishments on the projects in question. This was because the CLO discovered that the bidding process for these projects awarded to them by previous Puentevella administration was shortened to escape the election ban in 2016 in violation of IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason:
Legal impediment as explained in the column “Management Action".

There are still discrepancy with the records of both departments, since the CEO report is based on actual accomplishment, while the OCA is based on payments made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

The City Mayor thru the City Accountant or personnel in-charge require the officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately unliquidated cash advances, especially those which remained outstanding for more than one year, otherwise, refund the amount advanced to avoid institution of administrative and criminal charges against the erring officials; and

The City Accountant ensure that all cash advances to officers and employees are liquidated within the prescribed periods pursuant to Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 and do not allow additional cash advance to any official or employee who have unliquidated cash advances unless settlement or proper accounting thereof is made.
observations.managementAction:
₱40,011,149.36 of these advances or 69.21% thereof were already liquidated. Continued follow-ups are being made with the Officials and Employees concerned either by the Office of the City Accountant or by the designated Focal Persons at the Office of the City Mayor.

Policy about no further cash advances unless prior cash advances are liquidated is being complied with
observations.reason:
Accountable Officers and employees for the remaining cash advances are still compiling supporting documents needed for liquidation.

City Accountant periodically sends demand letters for liquidation of cash advances but compliance by the accountable officers and employees is beyond her control.

A request for authority to write-off was filed with COA for those dormant accounts and beginning balance in CY 2007 with no available records/data to validate the account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

Through the City Legal Office and Office of the City Accountant, require the NGOs/POs to immediately submit liquidation report for financial assistance whose programs, projects or activities were already completed, especially those with long overdue accounts;

Designate a focal person/office to take charge in the regular monitoring and inspection of the accomplishment of the intended purpose to which the assistance is granted; and

Through the City Legal Officer, institute legal action against defaulting NGOS/POs which failed to complete a project covered by the MOA or has committed material violation on the provisions of the MOA or of COA Circular No. 2007-001.

We also recommended that only NGOs/POs who have complied with the terms and conditions of the MOA and undertaking, and have fully settled their previous financial assistance be given further financial assistance by the City Mayor.
observations.managementAction:
Of the 30 financial assistance, 13 have already liquidated.  

The designated focal person continues to send demand letters.
observations.reason:
Some of the NGOs/POs were no longer existing. Also, the officers/directors of some of these NGOs/POs are either abroad or cannot be located so the present set of Officers cannot yet submit the liquidation report since their records are incomplete.

To date, out of ₱6,891,300.03, ₱2,379,300.03 or 35% already liquidated, leaving the balance to only ₱4,512,000.00.

Extrajudicial efforts are being pursued first.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

Demand immediate liquidation of financial assistance from the various leagues and federations, and require the return or refund of the unexpended or unutilized amount pursuant to 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, otherwise, institute appropriate legal action against the erring officials; and

Designate personnel to conduct regular monitoring on project implementation to ascertain that funds granted as financial assistance to league/federation were utilized based on the purpose intended for.

We also recommended that only leagues/federations who have liquidated previous financial assistance be given the opportunity to receive future assistance from the City.
observations.managementAction:
The Focal person in charge continues to send demand letters.

Focal Person has been designated.

Only the Vice Mayor’s League of the Philippines-Negros Occidental Chapter has liquidated their FA amounting to ₱100,000.00.
observations.reason:
They are awaiting response from the other leagues/ federations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant:

Prepare aging schedule on dormant Other Receivables accounts and exert efforts in securing the required supporting documents and other data on existing conditions for validation so that request for write-off on accounts of over 10 years be facilitated. Consequently, prepare adjusting entries for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements; and

Verify Other Receivables accounts with abnormal credit balances to determine valid reasons and conditions for reclassification to appropriate accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Aging Schedules prepared and write-off request submitted to COA for the following:

Calamity -          49,645.00
Contractors - 3,688,109.74
Total –           3,737,754.74

Those needed to be reclassified were already reclassified to appropriate accounts.
observations.reason:
Awaiting decision of COA on the write-off request submitted so that adjusting entries can be passed.

For amount exceeding ₱100,000.00, the request was forwarded to the ORD.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The City Accountant record first the procured and delivered supplies and materials to inventory account to recognize existence of assets, and as expense only when it is already consumed or used; and

The General Services Officer maintain stock card for each supply and other inventory items delivered and accepted for reconciliation with supplies ledger cards at the Office of City Accountant so that correctness of these accounts balances can be verified and validated.
observations.managementAction:
The Office of the City Accountant has complied with the recording of the procurement of supplies by initially recording to inventory account upon receipt of supplies and materials and subsequently expensed upon consumption by the end user through the Requisition and Issuance Slip.

The GSO is continuously updating and maintaining the stock cards for each supplies and other inventory items delivered and accepted as recommended in the audit observation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Planning and Development Officer:

Jointly conduct with City Engineer regular monitoring and evaluation of 20% Development Fund projects to immediately address the causes of delays and to fast track the implementation of the uncompleted projects; and

Install a project monitoring system to properly keep track the implementation and to ensure the achievement of desirable outcomes from the delivery of socio-economic development and environmental services to the constituents.

We recommended that the Local School Board closely coordinate with the City BAC for the timely processing of complete procurement documents and in titling of land to maximize the utilization of the Special Education Fund appropriated for various PPAs.

We recommended that the City Health Officer facilitate the immediate distribution and use of larvicides/insecticides and test kits to the intended recipients/beneficiaries for the immediate prevention and control of dengue epidemic, thus, 30% Quick Response Fund be properly used and applied, and prevent possible loss or waste of government funds and resources.

We also recommended that the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer thru the City Health Officer determine the appropriate quantity of larvicides/insecticides, test kits and other commodities needed for dengue prevention and control program in the subsequent periods to ensure the necessity and economy in the procurement.
observations.reason:
No updates were made as to the remaining stocks, but the concerned officials will comply with the recommendation.

During exit conference last May 30, 2022, the OIC-CHO was asked as to the status of the stocks and assured us to give us an update.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor thru the City Engineer require the Contractor to speed-up the processing of the required license with MARINA for the full completion of the Sum-ag Development Project, which is the Construction/Fabrication of Floating Restaurant to attain the promotion of eco-tourism in the City.

We recommended that the City Engineer thru the Project Engineer coordinate with the City Treasurer for the imposition of liquidated damages from any succeeding payments or billing due to Contractor, if found warranted.
observations.managementAction: Will be considered in computing payment/s to contractor once licensing issue has been settled.
observations.reason: Licensing process still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor closely monitor the liquidation of financial assistance/transfer to NGOS/POs, leagues/confederations and barangays to ensure the settlement within 60 days upon completion of the project or activity or within 30 days upon receipt of the grant per MOA to ensure the implementation of the intended purpose and to safeguard the fund from possible loss.

We recommended that the City Treasurer immediately send collection or demand letters to 820 delinquent stallholders at Libertad South Public Market, Burgos North Public Market and Central Market.

In case of failure of concerned stallholders to pay their long overdue market rental fees despite the demand, it is recommended that the City Administrator impose the provision of Section 46 of the Revised Market Code of Bacolod City by revoking or cancelling his lease right and ejectment, after observance of due process.

The City Administrator also revoke, after observance of due process, the lease contract of stall owners who sub-leased their stalls or have their privileges as market stall awardees sold to other persons contrary to Sections 19 and 46 of the Revised Market Code of Bacolod City.
observations.managementAction:
Periodically being done.

As per records, a total of 547 out of 820 delinquent stallholders whose lease rights has been cancelled due to non-payment of monthly rental.

Out of 547 stalls, 99 stalls were already bid, while 448 stalls will be scheduled for public bidding.
observations.reason:
Revocation or cancellation is pending due to the request of stallholders and financial incapability.

Some delinquent stallholders begged for more time to pay their rentals in arrears citing economic downturn of the COVID pandemic over that past years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Treasurer and the City Accountant reconcile their records per cashbook, general and subsidiary ledgers immediately while documents are still available for references. Henceforth, the two offices must conduct reconciliation at least every end of each quarter and any items subject for correction must be adjusted promptly to present a reliable Cash – Local Treasury balance or the accountable officer’s balance of accountability at any given time as required under Sec. 181 (c), GAAM, Volume I.

Moreover, the City Treasurer require the Cashier to rule, foot and certify the cashbook pursuant to Section 26, Volume II of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction:
The City Treasurer’s Office (CTO) and the Office of the City Accountant (OCA) were required to conduct reconciliation meetings at least every quarter. Automation of recording in the CTO for disbursements and collections will also be launched soon as part of the Smart City initiatives of our LGU.

The City Treasurer, in her letter reply on this observation indicated that the recommendation to rule, foot and certify the cashbook by the Cashier has been followed accordingly and has furnished a copy of certified cashbook as proof.
observations.reason:
The CTO still manually posts disbursements and collections to cashbook while the OCA is automated. Thus, the time lag in these two departments contributes to the number of reconciling differences.

As per letter dated June 9, 2022, reconciliation amounting to ₱72,733,188.03 were already determined. The difference amounting to ₱144,763.72 remained unreconciled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Treasurer immediately send collection or demand letters to 23 delinquent stallholders at Manokan Country.

In case of failure of concerned stallholders to pay their long overdue market rental fees despite the demand, it is recommended that the City Administrator impose the provision of Section 46 of the Revised Market Code of Bacolod City by revoking or cancelling their lease rights and ejectment, after observance of due process.

The City Mayor make sure that contract of lease shall be strictly renewed every two years from date the contract is signed pursuant to the provision of the Contract of Lease.
observations.managementAction:
The City Administrator, per Section 5 of City Ordinance No. 160 series of 1996, has sent out demand letters to delinquent stallholders at Manokan Country.

As part of Action Plan for this year, they will sending out notices to the delinquent stallholders of their unpaid rental arrears and to demand for payment.
observations.reason:
Some delinquent stallholders have begged for more time to pay their rentals in arrears citing the economic downturn of Covid pandemic over the past 2 years. These stallholders cannot just be replaced by any other chicken barbeque outlet. These long-time vendors at the Manokan Country have earned the reputation for producing that authentic, distinctive and famed “Bacolod Chicken Inasal taste”, which attracted the patronage of local and foreign tourist over the years. Thus, we also have to factor in the tourism aspect of this rentals-in-arrears issue.

In order not to destroy the tourism value of these outlets for the famed Bacolod Chicken Inasal, maximum consideration is being extended to these stallholders in the updating of their rentals in arrears, which is a prior condition for the renewal of their contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare as soon as possible the necessary adjustment to book up the reversion of the remaining unutilized PDAF funds to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund and the City Treasurer to draw the disbursement voucher covering the return of the unutilized PDAF funds to the National Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The amount of ₱4,476,417.61 were all returned to the National Bureau of Treasury

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that –

The City Mayor/Board of Trustees of the Provident Fund submit immediately the authorization and guidelines from the DBM allowing subsequent government monetary contributions to the Provident Fund.

The City Accountant ensure that no fund transfers to the Provident Fund will be released without prior authority and guidelines issued by the DBM.
observations.managementAction:
The City Mayor made an appeal to the Secretary of the Dept of Budget and Mgt through its Director for Region VI on these monetary contributions of the City Govt to the Provident Fund. COA-Bacolod was furnished a copy of this appeal.

The City Accountant, in her letter reply to COA also noted that upon receipt of the AOM, the Office of the City Accountant has complied with the recommendation and up to date, no transfer has been made to the Provident Fund.
observations.reason: Awaiting decision of the DBM Secretary on the appeal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the City Mayor:

refrain from appropriating further any public funds to be contributed as government share to the Provident Fund.

direct the City Budget Officer and the City Treasurer to refrain from obligating and disbursing any amount already earmarked as government share to the Provident Fund for Calendar Year 2010.

direct the City Accountant to account for the total government share given to the Provident Fund since the time of the creation of the fund in 1995 up to December 31, 2009.
observations.managementAction:
The City Mayor made an appeal to the Secretary of the Dept of Budget and Mgt through its Director for Region VI on these monetary contributions of the City Govt to the Provident Fund. COA-Bacolod was furnished a copy of this appeal.

The City Accountant, in her letter reply to COA also noted that upon receipt of the AOM, the Office of the City Accountant has complied with the recommendation and up to date, no transfer has been made to the Provident Fund.
observations.reason: Awaiting decision of the DBM Secretary on the appeal.

observations.managementAction: Investigation done by the CLO in 2012 could not identify the culprits with reasonable certainty.

The donated properties are fully depreciated hence, amount in books will be at salvage value. These properties will be included in the disposal of unserviceable properties.

Further, proper property management is being worked on by the General Services Office in coordination with proper recording with the Office of the City Accountant.
observations.reason: Awaiting for the City’s disposal of the said properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City General Services Officer:

to secure and complete his file of the owner’s copy of the TCTs owned by the City; and

to regularly reconcile his records of TCTs with that of the City Assessor and the City Accountant and adopt procedures to address the exception noted above.
observations.managementAction: GSO already has 724 TCTs in its custody.
observations.reason: Still conducting inventory and reconciling records for another 594 identified lots whose TCTs are still not with the GSO. Examples are road lot donations that require subdividing from its present TCT or those for open spaces in subdivisions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the City Mayor direct the withholding of payments of any money due to:

nine (9) accountable officers enumerated above;

Atty. Nilo T. Alejandrino
Leo M. Bentinganan
Nilda Cioco
Moises SD G. de la Cruz
Atty. Caesar Distrito
Teresita Gonzales
Imogene S. Kana-an
Daniel C. Ramos
Jose Marie Sun

accountable officers who incurred shortages in the total amount of P1,856,235.43;

accountable officers not examined whose cash advances remained unliquidated; and

designate only permanent employees to act as special disbursing officers.

We further recommend that the City Accountant:

require the immediate settlement of all outstanding cash advances;
stop the granting of additional cash advances to officials and employees with unsettled cash advances; and

refrain from processing and approving reimbursements of travel or other expenses of officials/employees with unsettled cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
8 of the 9 AOs concerned had already fully liquidated their cash advances worth ₱1,496,259.50.

These shortages have been reduced to ₱1,313,706.78. Of this, ₱360,000.00 is earmarked vs. terminal benefits of one AO (Ma Elma Gerasmo) who has retired. The remaining ₱953,706.78 is still due from another AO (Lilia Ursos) who was dismissed by the Ombudsman. Inquiry will be made if AO Ursos has any pending appeal with the Ombudsman on her termination from the service.

Performance -based bonuses for 2020 of these AOs were withheld until they are able to fully liquidate their o/s cash advances.

City Accountant periodically sends demand letters to these employees. Focal Persons at the City Mayor’s Office also periodically makes personal follow-ups to assist.

Done but with some exceptions as when said employees have to go on travel from time to time to carry out their assigned duties or to train them to be more effective at it. In such cases, we allow them to reimburse their official travel expenses due to exigency of the service.
observations.reason:
The remaining cash advance of ₱135,000.00 of AO Gonzales is under review. This AO insisted that she has already liquidated the same because she was cleared by the Office of the City Accountant when she retired in 2017.

Action on cash advance of AO Ursos will await results of inquiry with the Ombudsman on any appeal she may have filed with the said Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the City Mayor, together with the OIC GSO, the BAC Chairman and members and the OIC City Engineer review and investigate the breakdown of control in the mode and the process of procurement.

We further recommend that the City Mayor demand from FAT Petron:

the return of the balance of P1.213M;

the return of additional cost incurred due to price increase per liters of fuel of P50,255.48; and

legal interest on the P1.217M from November 2007 up to time of refund.
observations.managementAction: The project engineer in charge was on AWOL when this AOM was issued. The City Mayor demanded for the return of the said amount from FAT Petron but the company was unable to make the refund. Consequently, a collection case under Civil Case No. 13-14174 was filed by the CLO. Legal process still on going.
observations.reason: Awaiting results of pending court case. (Civil Case No. 13-14174 against Francis Albert A. Torres of FAT Petron Service Station)

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

The City Mayor:

Direct the OIC City Accountant, the GSO and the City Engineer to strictly observe and implement the perpetual method of recording of inventory in accordance with the rules cited above.

File appropriate administrative and criminal charges against persons liable for the shortage of cement stocks at CEO bodega. 

Engr. Belly Aguillon, OIC City Engineer, direct:

The reconciliation of the inventory records of stocks of cement at the CEO bodega and establish who are accountable for the discrepancy of 1,500 bags of cement as reported by the OIC of the Property and Supply Management Section of the CEO.  

The strengthening and strict observance of internal control measures with regards to inventory handling.
observations.managementAction:
Memo issued to the department heads concerned years ago.

The City Legal Office (under then City Legal Officer Alan Zamora) conducted an investigation. One employee was found guilty of Gross Negligence and was meted the penalty of dismissal by the City Mayor Leonardia. Employee appealed the ruling with the Civil Service Commission (CSC) during the succeeding Puentevella Administration. However, due to failure of the Puentevella administration to provide the CSC with the records of the case and to counter the appeal, the CSC, on the basis of declarations made by the employee in his appeal, reversed the ruling made by Mayor Leonardia and reinstated the employee into the service.

City Engineer Aguillon has already issued years ago the required memos to personnel concerned to comply with these recommendations.

Appropriate memos required by these recommendations have been issued years ago.

However, past and present GSO staff find it impossible to locate the equipment under this audit observation because identifying tags like plate nos. have not been provided in the AOM of COA

With the passage of time and the difficulty of identifying the equipment concerned, may we most respectfully request that these findings be considered closed.

The current GSO officials have already been alerted to these findings though, for them to take proper action to continue complying with the same recommendations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the current audit findings.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor thru the City Engineer require the Contractor to speed-up the processing of the required license with MARINA for the full completion of the Sum-ag Development Project, which is the Construction/Fabrication of Floating Restaurant to attain the promotion of eco-tourism in the City.

We recommended that the City Engineer thru the Project Engineer coordinate with the City Treasurer for the imposition of liquidated damages from any succeeding payments or billing due to Contractor, if found warranted.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | The City Mayor requested for the legal opinion of the CLO regarding the issuance of license/authority from MARINA.

The liquidated damages will be computed and deducted from the final billing..
observations.reason: The City has not yet submitted its update regarding this issue.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to Local Chief Executive the following:

Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;

Direct the General Services Officer-Designate to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and

Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting procedures:

Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry.

Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and

Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

Require the Concerned Personnel to attend training and seminar on Local Road Asset Management to ensure compliance with the transitory provision under COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
observations.managementAction: CY2019 AAR | Tasks the inventory committee to conduct an inventory for Road Network and prepare the necessary documents to support the Road Networks recognized in the Financial Statement
observations.reason: Had difficulty to conduct inventory for Road Network accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to Local Chief Executive to: 

a.) Adhere strict compliance with the provisions prescribed by Section 325(a) of R.A. No. 7160, Section 14 of Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 and Section 4.1 and 4.2 of Local Budget Circular No. 98 dated October 14, 2011.  

b.) Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Local Finance Committee to perform a thorough review and evaluation of the budget, whether annual or supplemental, prior to its submission to the Sangguniang Bayan to ensure that all Personal Services items are properly identified and classified as PS and that appropriation for PS items shall not exceed the PS limitation at any point in time during the budget year. 

c) Make an effort in raising revenue/receipts to be able to implement and provide additional PS costs or by reducing its PS expenses.

d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly record the RATA to proper personal services accounts in order to present the actual PS expenditures of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: CY2020 AAR | The Municipal Budget Officer  has difficulty to transfer the RATA budget from MOOE to PS for CY2020 and CY 2021.However, she ensures that for CY 2022, the LGU will be adhering with the provisions prescribed by law.
observations.reason: The Municipal Budget Officer still continue charging RATA under MOOE accounts for the reason not to exceed the 55% PS Limitation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Direct the head of Human Resource Management Office to submit the contract of service for the job order personnel to the Audit Team with specific duties and functions and reconcile the manpower complement based on the actual manpower and instruct to use the computer-generated DTRs based on the captured biometric date as basis for the preparation of payrolls for the establishment of a reliable number of days worked as the basis for the computation of their wages. 

Prepare and issue policies and guidelines to address the issue on the necessity and propriety of hiring Job Orders.

Require the Job Order personnel to submit personal records, undergo interview and document the evaluation of performance to give assurance that the risk identified above are addressed inorder to strengthen the controls in the hiring of job order personnel.

Formulate a policy and internal control in the hiring of job order workers such as but not limited to requiring specific and detailed description of duties and   responsibilities to eliminate workings having no definite purpose, if any. 

Consider either one of the following:

Appoint Job Order personnel as regular employees if they are qualified and believes that their services are essential and contributory to the improvement of agency’s operations or, 

Stop hiring Job Order and fill up the vacant plantilla positions in accordance with the Civil Service Commission rules and regulations. Prioritize the division with undermanned in the filling-up of vacant plantilla position.
observations.managementAction:
CY2019 AAR | The Human Resource Management Office does not submit the contract of service of the job orders and consultants as of December 31, 2019. Moreover, there is an increase number of Job Orders and no reconciliation as to the manpower complement based on the actual manpower. 
The Management does not formulate a policy and internal control in the hiring of job order workers such as but not limited to requiring specific and detailed description of duties and   responsibilities to eliminate workings having no definite purpose, if any.
observations.reason: Lacked of employment opportunity in the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Require the Property Officer to issue property tags/ stickers for each PPE in order to identify and trace the location of the said PPEs and to issue corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for all the Properties, Plant and Equipment issued to officials and employees who have the custody thereto and to facilitate such undertaking, assign additional competent personnel in order to complete the needed property records;

Renew the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) every January of the third year after issuance; and

Strictly comply with the provisions of Section 492 of GAAM Vol. I for the succeeding procurement and issuance of Properties, Plant and Equipment.
observations.managementAction: CY2019 AAR | The Local Chief Executive directed the new General Services Designate to comply with the said requirements in regards to preparation and maintenance of record of Property Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment (PARE/ARE)
observations.reason: The previous General Services Designate overlooked the issuance of PARE on issued properties and equipment to its employees.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive as Chairman of the LSB to:

To orient the Local School Board on the eligible expenditures allowed on the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations.  If possible, coordinate with the Department of Education so that possible double budgeting and overlapping of charging can be avoided.

Require the Local School Board to strictly follow the provisions of RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars No. 1, s. 2017 in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto. Any issues or conflicts arising from its implementation and cases not covered by the provisions shall be submitted to the DepEd for resolution and in consultation with the municipal government.

To refrain from charging to SEF the programs/projects/activities that is not authorized under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and implement the same in order to maximize the use of the SEF for its intended purpose.
