To have a conscious and concerted effort of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute.

Require the Municipal Accountant to review the charging of the Special Education Fund strictly in accordance with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2019
AAR | The Municipal Accountant ensured to review strictly the charging of Special Education Fund in accordance with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Notice of Suspension has already been issued.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Treasurer institutes corresponding actions to collect from delinquent taxpayers the amount involved otherwise, perform proper cancellation of returned checks and coordinate with the OCA for appropriate adjustments in books.

Further, inform all Collecting Officers under his jurisdiction on the list of taxpayers whose checks were previously dishonored to prevent the same incident to happen in the future.

We recommended that:
The City Accountant, together with the City Treasurer, review and analyze prior years’ reconciling items and exert effort effecting required adjustments; and
The City Treasurer coordinate with the concerned depository banks the complete submission of necessary documents related to any reconciling items.

We recommended that:
The OCA periodically coordinate with the Office of the City Treasurer regarding the existence of stale checks;
The City Treasurer send written notices to the payee of the existence of stale checks and exert effort by sending notices to the concerned payee to return the stale checks in their possession for cancellation and replacement; and
In the event that stale checks are unclaimed by the payee, the City Treasurer cancel the said checks, furnishing the original Disbursement Voucher (DV) and its supporting documents to the Accounting for appropriate adjustment in the books.

We recommended that the City Accountant prepare the aging of dormant account receivables in the prescribed form, and exert efforts in securing the required supporting documents and other data applicable to present conditions so that request for authority to write-off of dormant receivables maybe filed pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
We recommended and the City Treasurer agreed to increase the fidelity bond of AO with insufficient coverage in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Premium Rates.

We recommended that:
The CGSO file a request for disposal of unserviceable properties supported with necessary documents needed for technical evaluation and appraisal; and
After the disposal, the City Accountant drop from the books of accounts the disposed property and take up any proceeds from the sale thereof, in compliance with paragraph 67 of PAS 16 and Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the concerned AOs return the unused cash and/or to submit all documents to fully liquidate their cash advances immediately.
We also recommended that the City Accountant monitor the cash advances and send written notices to the Accountable Officers to settle their past due cash advances, and effect the necessary correcting entries to record the cash advances to appropriate funds and keenly review journal entries onwards before approving the same.

We recommended that the City strictly follow the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and the relevant GPPB issuances in the procurement of needed goods and supplies in times of pandemic to ensure transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process.

We recommended that the Office of the City Accountant ensure that the required complete supporting documents were attached to the liquidation report for ease of verification, confirmation, and validation of the purchases made out of BGCM Funds.

We recommended and the BAC Secretariat agreed to require duly complied Omnibus Sworn Statements from the suppliers and strictly require the submission of the OSS for subsequent procurement pursuant to the requirement of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR and GPPB issuances.

We recommended and the City Engineer agreed to submit the copy of the approved variation order for the Improvement of Bio-Marine and Toxicology Office (DOH-CLMMRH and Bacolod City Biosafety Laboratory Testing Center for COVID-19 amounting to ₱1,275,364.59 and all the documents forming part thereof, otherwise final payment of the project will be suspended in audit.
We also recommended that management ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 on the submission of copy of government contracts and other documents to the Commission on Audit at all times.

We recommended that the City Accountant recognize and record the acquired asset of ₱112,100,000.00 in the Trust Fund books and subsequently transfer it to the General Fund books. Ensure that subsequent acquisitions charged against Trust Fund are properly recorded pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the concerned SDOs agreed to submit the lacking signed payrolls to established validity and propriety to the payment, otherwise refund the amount of ₱408,000.00 representing excess SAP beneficiaries reported as paid.

We recommended that the City through the Emergency Operations Center to expedite the use of about to expire kits to prevent wastage, and formulate internal control policies necessary to minimize the occurrence of expired swab kits such as establishing adequate control on custodianship, issuances and stock level monitoring through the maintenance of inventory database or computerized system to facilitate retrieval of data on inventory balances, actual condition of stocks, and expiry date at any given time.
We also recommended that the City Health Office exercise prudence in the use of government resources by making sure that purchase of supplies limited to what is urgently needed.
We further recommended that the City Mayor’s Office as the requesting office of the said purchase review the contract with supplier with regard to the subject expired Swab Kits, and consider returning the excess VTM kits delivered, otherwise do not pay the VTM Kits delivered in excess of the contracted quantity.

We recommended and the management agreed to strictly enforce the timely compliance of the preparation, posting and notification requirements within the prescribed period as required under with Section 4 of the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.
The concerned officials should look into the cause of the delay in the preparation and posting of accumulated reports and take necessary action to address the problem.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the preparation and submission of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements to achieve an accurate financial reporting of Cash in Bank account and reconcile with the Bank Reconciliation with the general ledgers. Also require him to get the monthly bank statements from the depository bank promptly and regularly;
Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to reconcile his cashbooks entries with that from the accounting records at least quarterly; and
Give authority to the Municipal Accountant to enroll in LBP i-access so that bank balances can be viewed and printed anytime with the Municipal Treasurer.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Create an Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory count of all PPE and submit an updated and corrected Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
Instruct the General Services Officer/Supply Officer-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate classification of Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.
The Municipal Accountant should review previous records to come up with the purchase amount of PPEs on the prior year’s.
Require the General Services Officer/Supply Officer-Designate to issue property tags/ stickers for each PPE in order to identify and trace the location of the said PPEs and to issue corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for all the Properties, Plant and Equipment issued to officials and employees who have the custody thereto and to facilitate such undertaking, assign additional competent personnel in order to complete the needed property records.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
Direct the General Services Officer-Designate to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting procedures:
i.) 	Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry.
ii.) Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
iii.) Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
Require the Concerned Personnel to attend training and seminar on Local Road Asset Management to ensure compliance with the transitory provision under COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Adhere strict compliance with the provisions prescribed by Section 325(a) of R.A. No. 7160, Section 14 of Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 and Section 4.1 and 4.2 of Local Budget Circular No. 98 dated October 14, 2011 instead of hearing recommendations from a co-personnel.
b.) Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Local Finance Committee to perform a thorough review and evaluation of the budget, whether annual or supplemental, prior to its submission to the Sangguniang Bayan to ensure that all Personal Services items are properly identified and classified as PS and that appropriation for PS items shall not exceed the PS limitation at any point in time during the budget year.
c) Make an effort in raising revenue/receipts to be able to implement and provide additional PS costs or by reducing its PS expenses.
d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly record the RATA to proper personal services accounts in order to present the actual PS expenditures of the municipality.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the head of Human Resource Management Office to submit the complete contract of service of the job orders with specific duties and functions and reconcile the manpower complement and monthly compensation based on the actual and instruct to use the computer-generated DTRs based on the captured biometric date as basis for their preparation of payrolls for the establishment of a reliable number of days worked as the basis for the computation of their wages.
Comply with the aforementioned rules and regulations in the hiring of Job Orders, prepare and issue policies and guidelines to address the issue on the necessity and propriety of hiring JOs. Also, examine the reasons for the increase number of job order personnel and the need to hire such personnel.
Immediately strengthen the controls in the hiring of job order by requiring them to submit personal records, undergo interview and document the evaluation of performance to give assurance that the risks identified above are addressed.
Consider either one of the following:
Appoint Job Order personnel as regular employees if they are qualified and believe that their services are essential and contributory to the improvement of agency’s operations or,
Stop hiring Job Order and fill up the vacant plantilla positions in accordance with the Civil Service Commission rules and regulations. Prioritize the division with undermanned in the filling-up of vacant plantilla position.
Formulate a policy and internal control in the hiring of job order workers such as but not limited to requiring specific and detailed description of duties and responsibilities to eliminate workings having no definite purpose, if any. Hiring of job order personnel be based on necessity in order to avoid waste of much needed funds.
Submit justification on the sources of fund for the excessive payment of Job Orders compared to its budgeted amount. Also, explain why other plantilla positions remained vacant/unfilled.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to require all Job Order personnel to prepare and submit duly signed and verified DTR.
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims should be supported with complete supporting documents and duly signed by appropriate signatories before payments are made by Municipal Treasurer or Special Disbursing Officer.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipality’s officers and employees to impose proper application of the entitlement of traveling allowance as well as completeness and regularity of the essential documents like travel orders, bus tickets, e-ticket, boarding pass and itinerary of travel.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely review the liquidation reports submitted by the officers and employees and require immediate refund for excessive claims.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the Property Officer to issue property tags/ stickers for each PPE in order to identify and trace the location of the said PPEs and to issue corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for all the Properties, Plant and Equipment issued to officials and employees who have the custody thereto and to facilitate such undertaking, assign additional competent personnel in order to complete the needed property records;
Renew the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) every January of the third year after issuance; and
Strictly comply with the provisions of Section 492 of GAAM Vol. I for the succeeding procurement and issuance of Properties, Plant and Equipment.

Recommendations:
We have recommended to Local Chief Executive to:
a.) 	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit disbursement vouchers after payment with complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant.
b.) 	Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the latter to submit immediately the required reports within the prescribed period, and to facilitate the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.
c.) 	Augment additional personnel even Job Orders who are Accounting Majors to assist in the preparation of the reports and possibly train them both in the Treasury and the Accounting Department.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Implement an adequate monitoring system that tracks the loan payables of its employees from authorized agencies.
Direct the Municipal Accountant the following to:
Refrain from approving the loan applications of those employees whose net take pays are below the mandated minimum net take home pay and
Implement the P5,000.00 net take home pay required for each employee as provided under the General Provisions of the General Appropriation Act (GAA) for FY 2019 also known as Republic Act No. 11260.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to remit promptly the balances of liabilities due to the government agencies/corporations concerned in order not to compromise the benefits that is due to the intended beneficiaries and at the same time avoid penalties/surcharges arising from late payments. It shall be made on or before the due date to avoid incurrence of unnecessary and irregular expenditures.
Direct the Office of The Municipal Treasurer to explain the delayed payment of remittances, if proven to be out of neglect or laxity of duty, refund the amount paid for the penalties and interest.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and the OIC-Municipal Engineer to comply with the regulations and furnish the Audit Team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within five (5) days after the perfection thereof for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same. Likewise, for those beyond the threshold of the audit team, after auditorial and legal review, it will be forwarded to the Technical Audit Sector at the COA, Regional Office X. Otherwise, the Audit Team will issue Notice of Suspension.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Direct the General Services Officer-Designate to accomplish properly and religiously the purchase orders and see to it that the required conditions are indicated therein.
b.) Submit the purchase orders to the Office of the Auditor irrespective of the amount within five (5) working days pursuant to the abovementioned regulations.
Submit copies of inspection of deliveries of goods and services within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance to enable this office to conduct timely review and validation.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents to ensure the completeness and propriety of the required supporting documents for all disbursements using the Bayanihan Grant to Municipalities.
b.) Advice the BAC, Municipal Accountant and other Department Heads to be well-informed and well-equipped of the new laws, rules and regulations.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Acting Human Resource Management Officer to publish the vacancy of MHO position until an interested applicant has manifested his intent.
Demand the Municipal Mayor the immediate filling-up of MHO vacant positions for a more effective implementation of programs projects on health and prompt delivery of the health services needed by the constituents.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive as Chairman of the LSB to:
Orient the Local School Board on the eligible expenditures allowed on the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations.
Require the Local School Board to strictly follow the provisions of RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars No. 1, s. 2017 in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto. Any issues or conflicts arising from its implementation and cases not covered by the provisions shall be submitted to the DepEd for resolution and in consultation with the municipal government.
Refrain from charging to SEF the programs/projects/activities that are not authorized under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and implement the same in order to maximize the use of the SEF for its intended purpose only which shall mean government financed and managed schools which include pre-school, elementary, secondary schools, national and general comprehensive high schools and their annexes and those public elementary and secondary schools established by law and should not include private educational institutions.
Have a conscious and concerted effort of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute.
Require the Municipal Accountant to review the charging of the Special Education Fund strictly in accordance with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations.
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit SEF Quarterly Utilization report faithfully as required under DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017.

Recommendations:
We recommended to Local Chief Executive to:
a.) 	Ensure that the 20% Development Fund of the Municipality be utilized only to fund priority programs or projects included in the approved Annual Investment Plan (AIP) which partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in compliance and in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 to ensure its optimal utilization.
b.)	Require the MPDC to prepare and submit to the audit team the accomplishment report for the 20% development fund.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the LDRRMO Officer and other concerned employees to submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund, Separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations and the Annual DRRM Plan, Budget and Programs to the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and GAD Focal Person to use the revised GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report.
Require the GAD Technical Working Group:
that the provisions of the HGDG be complied with by subjecting the GAD PPAs to the GAD Checklist during the project identification and designing programs to determine whether the same fully address gender related issues and HGDG test be conducted at the end of project implementation and incorporate the results in determining the percentage of PPA expenditures attributable to GAD.
to diligently monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs.

observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | The City Mayor issued Memorandum No. 109 creating the Physical Inventory team of 12 members, 6 members of which are from the GSO, to conduct inventory on Government Assets in all Office.

The City Accountant designated a staff to reclassify items below the threshold of ₱15,000.00 for previous years transactions already encoded as asset and record the incoming transactions directly to semi-expendable inventory.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation of PPE balances by GSO with OCA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

Direct the City Accountant and the City Engineer’s Office to exert extra efforts for the speedy reconciliation of their records regarding completed and on-going projects; and

Address the problems encountered on unimplemented projects preferably on six projects with total appropriation of ₱31,850,000.00, and instruct the City Engineer to speed up its implementation so that constituents who are beneficiaries of the projects will enjoy the benefits therefrom.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | The City Mayor has required the CEO and OCA to reconcile semi-annually their records on completed projects.

The City Accountant furnished the CEO a copy of the status of expenses and balances of various projects charged to AIP 2013 up to 2019 as of December 31, 2019 and asked for the status of implementation of some projects which were not reported as 100% complete in the reports submitted to COA.

The City Mayor has assigned the City Legal Office to handle the legal issues involved in these projects as a consequence of shortened bidding timelines under the previous administration (thus, a violation of bidding rules), which prevented payment of progress billings by the current administration.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation of records and balances between CEO and OCA; CEO still coordinating with the contractors for full implementation of said projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

The City Mayor thru the City Accountant or personnel in-charge require the officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately unliquidated cash advances, especially those which remained outstanding for more than one year, otherwise, refund the amount advanced to avoid institution of administrative and criminal charges against the erring officials; and

The City Accountant ensure that all cash advances to officers and employees are liquidated within the prescribed periods pursuant to Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 and do not allow additional cash advance to any official or employee who have unliquidated cash advances unless settlement or proper accounting thereof is made.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | The City Mayor designated a Focal Person to take charge in following-up the liquidation of cash advances with personnel concerned.
The City Accountant periodically sends demand letters for the liquidation of cash advances while the focal person makes personal follow-ups with personnel concerned. The City Accountant also does not allow those with unliquidated cash advances to reimburse their expenses for official travel.
observations.reason:
There is still a total amount of ₱242,076,526.65 remained. 

There are employees who have retired from service that their addresses cannot be located. There are also employees who had separated/absconded from their work leaving cash advances unliquidated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

Through the City Legal Office and Office of the City Accountant, require the NGOs/POs to immediately submit liquidation report for financial assistance whose programs, projects or activities were already completed, especially those with long overdue accounts;

Designate a focal person/office to take charge in the regular monitoring and inspection of the accomplishment of the intended purpose to which the assistance is granted; and

Through the City Legal Officer, institute legal action against defaulting NGOS/POs which failed to complete a project covered by the MOA or has committed material violation on the provisions of the MOA or of COA Circular No. 2007-001.

We also recommended that only NGOs/POs who have complied with the terms and conditions of the MOA and undertaking, and have fully settled their previous financial assistance be given further financial assistance by the City Mayor.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | Demand Letters was sent to NGOs/POs concerned.

The City Mayor designated a Focal Person to take charge in the monitoring and inspection of the activities/ accomplishments of NGOs/ POs and follow up on the submission of their liquidation report.


NGOs/POs reached have asked for more time to complete their liquidation reports as they are hampered by changes in the line-up of their officers.
observations.reason: There were still NGOs/POs with unliquidated FAs due to unlocated addresses and officials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

Demand immediate liquidation of financial assistance from the various leagues and federations, and require the return or refund of the unexpended or unutilized amount pursuant to 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, otherwise, institute appropriate legal action against the erring officials; and

Designate personnel to conduct regular monitoring on project implementation to ascertain that funds granted as financial assistance to league/federation were utilized based on the purpose intended for.

We also recommended that only leagues/federations who have liquidated previous financial assistance be given the opportunity to receive future assistance from the City.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | Demand Letters was sent to various leagues and federations.

The City Mayor designated a Focal Person to monitor and follow up liquidation of financial assistance given to various barangays.
observations.reason: There were still Leagues with unsettled FAs due to unlocated addresses and change of officers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

Thru a designated officer in-charge, demand the immediate liquidation of financial assistance granted to various barangays, reconcile the unliquidated accounts with barangay records and regularly monitor the compliance by barangays with the terms and conditions in the MOA; and

Disapprove the release of any subsequent financial assistance to the barangays with existing unliquidated amount if not supported with Certification by Accountant that previous assistance has been liquidated and accounted in the books.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | The City Mayor designated a Focal Person to follow up liquidation of financial assistance given to various barangays.

CY 2019 financial assistance were all fully liquidated.

Demands were made for the immediate liquidation of financial assistance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant:

Prepare aging schedule on dormant Other Receivables accounts and exert efforts in securing the required supporting documents and other data on existing conditions for validation so that request for write-off on accounts of over 10 years be facilitated. Consequently, prepare adjusting entries for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements; and

Verify Other Receivables accounts with abnormal credit balances to determine valid reasons and conditions for reclassification to appropriate accounts.
observations.managementAction: 2019 AAR | Prepare aging schedule on other dormant receivables accounts and inform employees with “underpayment of withholding tax” to settle these, reach out to families of deceased employees with dormant accounts and require them to submit copy of death certificates for write off. Also, where feasible, the receivable items will be deducted from Terminal Leave Benefits.
observations.reason: No request for write-off were filed in COA Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The City Accountant record first the procured and delivered supplies and materials to inventory account to recognize existence of assets, and as expense only when it is already consumed or used; and

The General Services Officer maintain stock card for each supply and other inventory items delivered and accepted for reconciliation with supplies ledger cards at the Office of City Accountant so that correctness of these accounts balances can be verified and validated.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | The City Accountant instructed the bookkeeper in charge of encoding supplies and materials to record first to inventory account and then as expense once utilized.

The City Mayor will issue a Memorandum requiring all Offices to prepare the quarterly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued and submit the report to the GSO for consolidation and the consolidated report to the OCA and will serve as a basis in preparing the JV to record the supplies and materials issued.
observations.reason: They were still procurement of supplies charged directly to expense.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) in accordance with the prescribed format of GPPB for submission by HoPE to GPPB and the same be posted for transparency and measurement purposes.
observations.managementAction: 2019 AAR | The BAC complied with the preparation of PMR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Chairperson on the Secretariat of the City GAD Focal Person System closely follow-up with DILG the approval of 2019 GAD Plan and Budget for submission to COA Audit Team and submit GAD accomplishment report in subsequent years in accordance with the prescribed period provided in COA Circular No. 2014-001.


We recommended that the Chairperson of GAD Focal Point System Secretariat closely monitor and exert extra efforts for the maximum utilization of PPAs covered in GAD Plan and Budget and those attributed to other funds to ensure full attainment of women’s empowerment and gender equality.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | CY 2019 and 2020 GAD Plan and Budget has been submitted to COA.





The GAD Focal Point Secretariat will closely monitor and exert extra efforts to maximize the utilization of PPAs covered in GAD Plan and Budget.

There were PPAs which were not accomplished during the current year specifically projects which require mass gathering, due to COVID-19 restrictions, which the Audit Team finds justifiable.

For ensuing years, the GAD Plan and Budget were duly received by the DILG and deemed approved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Planning and Development Officer:

Jointly conduct with City Engineer regular monitoring and evaluation of 20% Development Fund projects to immediately address the causes of delays and to fast track the implementation of the uncompleted projects; and

Install a project monitoring system to properly keep track the implementation and to ensure the achievement of desirable outcomes from the delivery of socio-economic development and environmental services to the constituents.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | The City Mayor issued a Memorandum for quarterly meetings of CEO and CPDO for info-sharing and monitoring of project implementation.

The City Mayor issued Executive Order No. 7 series of 2020, reconstituting the Project Monitoring Committee of the City of Bacolod.
observations.reason: There were still unimplemented projects as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board closely coordinate with the City BAC for the timely processing of complete procurement documents and in titling of land to maximize the utilization of the Special Education Fund appropriated for various PPAs.
observations.managementAction: 2019 AAR | The Local School Board will maximize the utilization of the Special Education Fund appropriated for various PPAs.
observations.reason: Some of the PPAs were partially implemented and on-going. They reasoned out that due to delay in procurement process and lack of final POWs and supporting documents some of the projects were not implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Health Officer facilitate the immediate distribution and use of larvicides/insecticides and test kits to the intended recipients/beneficiaries for the immediate prevention and control of dengue epidemic, thus, 30% Quick Response Fund be properly used and applied, and prevent possible loss or waste of government funds and resources.

We also recommended that the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer thru the City Health Officer determine the appropriate quantity of larvicides/insecticides, test kits and other commodities needed for dengue prevention and control program in the subsequent periods to ensure the necessity and economy in the procurement.
observations.managementAction: 2019 AAR | The CHO Officer and the DRRMO will comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Remaining stocks were not fully used as of CY 2020 since new stocks donated by DOH were received and both stocks are used depending on expiration dates.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of Department of Public Services require the project in-charge to prepare and submit revised Program of Work and Detailed Estimate on Management and Operation of Sanitary Landfill with complete details, specifications, descriptions, other quantifiable and relevant data, incorporating any modifications made for proper monitoring and evaluation of actual accomplishments and to ascertain validity on payment of monthly billings.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | The Solid Waste Management Board passed Resolution No. 54 directing the contractor to answer and comply with the AOM.


The contractor revised the program of work based on actual accomplishment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of Department of Public Services require the Contractor to prepare a revised Schedule of Prices or Program of Work and Detailed Estimate of Expenses with complete details, specifications and other quantifiable relevant data for proper monitoring and evaluation of actual accomplishments as against monthly billing to ascertain validity of payments on Collection, Hauling and Disposal of Garbage services.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | The Solid Waste Management Board passed Resolution No. 54 directing the contractor to answer and comply with the AOM.

Contractor provided the updated schedule of prices, Program of Work and Detailed Estimate of Expenses.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor thru the Head of Department of Public Services monitor the immediate implementation by the Contractor of the proposed corrective actions by its own Pollution Control Officer. 




We recommended that the Head of Department of Public Services thru the Contractor, immediately act for the demolition of temporary structures near the sanitary landfill to avoid the imposition of fines and penalty, and possible suspension, cancellation or termination of the contract, and to ensure effective and proper maintenance, management and operations of the facility.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | The improper storage of hazardous materials and chemicals has been taken care of with its transfer to a more appropriate area.




The Head of DPS requested the assistance of CLO, BHA and CEO re: demolition of temporary structures in the area. They inspected the area and all structures were found within the 200 meters distance from the sanitary landfill but the structures are situated outside government property and are on privately-owned lands, thus it is not covered by Sec. 28(a) and (b), Article VII of RA 7279. However, possible relocation of occupants will be explored by the CLO and BHA prior to demolishing their privately-owned structures.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Head of Department of Public Services prepare and submit Program of Work and Estimate on the Construction of Bacolod City Sanitary Landfill Cell No. 2 which includes additional details, specifications and other relevant data and the basis of unit cost on all equipment and consumables, and the number of days required to facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation of project accomplishments. 

We recommended that the Head of Department of Public Services (DPS):

Thru the project engineer/technical person submit a status report pertaining to the implementation of the project, with supporting documents for evaluation and possible imposition of liquidated damages; and

Require the Contractor to submit detailed and quantifiable accomplishment report with complete supporting documents for verification and validation on any payment of progress billing.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | The Solid Waste Management Board passed Resolution No. 54 directing the contractor to answer and comply with the AOM.




The Contractor will submit the required documents per directive of the Board Resolution.

The Contractor will submit the detailed and quantifiable accomplishment report to COA per Board Resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that City Department of Social Services and Development Officer thru personnel in-charge, prepare and submit to DSWD Field Office the required periodic nutrition status report on the implementation of the Supplementary Feeding Program in the different barangays for monitoring and evaluation purposes.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | The City Department of Social Services and Development Office will comply with the requirements.

The Monitoring Reports were submitted to the COA Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor thru the City Engineer closely monitor the implementation of the project, and require the Contractor for the immediate completion of the project in accordance with approved plans, design and specifications, otherwise suspend issuance of certificate of completion and acceptance of the project, and impose the prescribed liquidated damages under the circumstances.
observations.managementAction: 2019 AAR | The project was forwarded to the TSO for technical inspection.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

Thru the City Budget Officer strictly allocate in the Annual Budget the amount equivalent to at least one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for LCPC related activities; and

Direct the LCPC Secretariat/focal person to prepare LDPC and LIPC and submit the approved copy to DILG and Auditor for monitoring and evaluation on the implementation of the covering PPAs.
observations.managementAction:
2019 AAR | The required appropriation for LCPC was incorporated in the budget of various offices.

PPAs were made through attribution under different departments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

Require the GAD Focal Person to prepare on time the annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) for review and approval by DILG and submit the approved copy and the corresponding Accomplishment Report to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed period provided in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014; and

Approve charges and disbursements on GAD Fund only if supported with DILG reviewed and approved GPB to ascertain validity and propriety of disbursements.
observations.managementAction:
2018 AAR | For the CY 2019 the department responsible in the control and preparation of reports has designated a focal person and report is being made periodically and reconciled with the Office of the City Accountant.

The GAD Plan and Budget for ensuing years were duly received by the DILG.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor closely monitor the liquidation of financial assistance/transfer to NGOS/POs, leagues/confederations and barangays to ensure the settlement within 60 days upon completion of the project or activity or within 30 days upon receipt of the grant per MOA to ensure the implementation of the intended purpose and to safeguard the fund from possible loss.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Treasurer immediately send collection or demand letters to 820 delinquent stallholders at Libertad South Public Market, Burgos North Public Market and Central Market.

In case of failure of concerned stallholders to pay their long overdue market rental fees despite the demand, it is recommended that the City Administrator impose the provision of Section 46 of the Revised Market Code of Bacolod City by revoking or cancelling his lease right and ejectment, after observance of due process.

The City Administrator also revoke, after observance of due process, the lease contract of stall owners who sub-leased their stalls or have their privileges as market stall awardees sold to other persons contrary to Sections 19 and 46 of the Revised Market Code of Bacolod City.
observations.reason:
There were still stallholders with unpaid delinquent accounts due to financial constraints. 

Revocation or cancellation is pending due to the request of stallholders and financial incapability.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Treasurer and the City Accountant reconcile their records per cashbook, general and subsidiary ledgers immediately while documents are still available for references. Henceforth, the two offices must conduct reconciliation at least every end of each quarter and any items subject for correction must be adjusted promptly to present a reliable Cash – Local Treasury balance or the accountable officer’s balance of accountability at any given time as required under Sec. 181 (c), GAAM, Volume I.

Moreover, the City Treasurer require the Cashier to rule, foot and certify the cashbook pursuant to Section 26, Volume II of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: 2017 AAR | Bank Reconciliation Statements of the Office of the City Accountant are being furnished the City Treasurer’s Office every month for reconciliation with their cash books
observations.reason: No adjustment in the Cash Book is being made upon receipt of the Bank Reconciliation Statement from the Office of the City Accountant for correction of reconciling items.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Treasurer immediately send collection or demand letters to 23 delinquent stallholders at Manokan Country.

In case of failure of concerned stallholders to pay their long overdue market rental fees despite the demand, it is recommended that the City Administrator impose the provision of Section 46 of the Revised Market Code of Bacolod City by revoking or cancelling their lease rights and ejectment, after observance of due process.

The City Mayor make sure that contract of lease shall be strictly renewed every two years from date the contract is signed pursuant to the provision of the Contract of Lease.
observations.managementAction: 2016 AAR | Demand letters were sent periodically by the City Treasurer prompting the stall lessees to start payment of rental fees.
observations.reason: There were still stallholders with delinquent accounts not collected due to financial incapability.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare as soon as possible the necessary adjustment to book up the reversion of the remaining unutilized PDAF funds to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund and the City Treasurer to draw the disbursement voucher covering the return of the unutilized PDAF funds to the National Treasury.
observations.managementAction: 2014 AAR | The checks issued for the return of PDAF to the DBM were not remitted such that it became stale. The city is willing to return the unutilized PDAF and will inquire with the DBM on the correct process of returning the funds.
observations.reason: There were projects which remained unimplemented amounting to ₱401,271.86.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that –

The City Mayor/Board of Trustees of the Provident Fund submit immediately the authorization and guidelines from the DBM allowing subsequent government monetary contributions to the Provident Fund.

The City Accountant ensure that no fund transfers to the Provident Fund will be released without prior authority and guidelines issued by the DBM.
observations.managementAction: 2012 AAR | There is an on-going talk between the administration and people in the Provident Fund to refund to the city the government share collected and remitted to the Provident Fund Association
observations.reason: The City is waiting for final decision/legal basis.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the City Mayor:

Justify the P340.00 per square meter purchase price paid for Felisa Solid Waste Disposal Facility;

Justify why the lots were classified as Residential Zone per CPDO certification when the classification in the declaration of the real property taxes was Agricultural; and

Cause the reduction of the purchase price for the acquired property considering that the dumpsite facility cannot be accessed and be useful to the City Government except thru the unappraised roads right of way.
observations.managementAction: 2009 AAR | The Commission Proper thru Decision No. 2020-220 dated January 29, 2020 granted the appeal of Mayor Leonardia, et. al. from ND No. 12-002-101(10) dated February 6, 2012 on the excess payment of parcel of land located in Barangay Felisa, Bacolod City for Solid Waste Disposal Facility in 2008. Accordingly, the said ND amounting to ₱1,676,280.00 is hereby lifted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the City Mayor:

refrain from appropriating further any public funds to be contributed as government share to the Provident Fund.

direct the City Budget Officer and the City Treasurer to refrain from obligating and disbursing any amount already earmarked as government share to the Provident Fund for Calendar Year 2010.

direct the City Accountant to account for the total government share given to the Provident Fund since the time of the creation of the fund in 1995 up to December 31, 2009.
observations.managementAction: 2009 AAR | Legal basis for the City’s contributions to the Provident Fund will be raised with the DBM for resolution inasmuch as it is the DBM that has been authorized to issue the guidelines on monetary contributions of government units to their Provident Funds.
observations.reason: The City is still waiting for the final decision/legal basis.

observations.managementAction: 2008 AAR | Inventory and Inspection Report was already submitted to COA Region VI for disposal.
observations.reason: Waiting for TSO Inspection Report.

observations.managementAction: 2008 AAR | Data on donated properties are not available. No record as to the transfer of property. Identify properties which can be easily processed and make adjustments in the books of the city.
observations.reason: Until now, no action was undertaken by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City General Services Officer:

to secure and complete his file of the owner’s copy of the TCTs owned by the City; and

to regularly reconcile his records of TCTs with that of the City Assessor and the City Accountant and adopt procedures to address the exception noted above.
observations.managementAction: 2008 AAR | GSO will reconcile with the City Assessor’s Office and the OCA.
observations.reason: CGSO has not report regarding this matter.

observations.managementAction:
2007 AAR | Aside from the demand letter sent to the employees, the City Accountant has devised to collect from employee who cannot claim for reimbursement of their traveling expenses unless they have not liquidated their cash advance. Instead of the COA recommendation to deduct from the salary, the non-reimbursement of their traveling/training expenses is being observed so as not to compromise the Net Take Home Pay of the employee..

The Office of the City had obtained a certification from the City Civil Registrar of the deceased employees, however, it does not suffice the requirements of the COA for write-off of their accounts.

Since 2013, the Office of the City Accountant has been in constant follow up the employees accounts and periodically writes reminders/demand letters to concerned employees.
observations.reason:
There is still a total amount of ₱57,814,123.84 remained unliquidated with ₱26,943,552.03 is outstanding for over 1 year. An increase by ₱16,156,810.21 or 38.79% over PYs balance, mainly due to delayed settlement of CAs for operating expenses/time bound activities due to lack of supporting documents and signatures.


There are employees who have retired from service that their addresses cannot be located. There are also employees who had separated/absconded from their work leaving cash advances unliquidated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the City Mayor, together with the OIC GSO, the BAC Chairman and members and the OIC City Engineer review and investigate the breakdown of control in the mode and the process of procurement.

We further recommend that the City Mayor demand from FAT Petron:

the return of the balance of P1.213M;

the return of additional cost incurred due to price increase per liters of fuel of P50,255.48; and

legal interest on the P1.217M from November 2007 up to time of refund.
observations.managementAction: 2007 AAR | Civil Case was filed against FAT Petron Service Station under Civil Case 13-141-74
observations.reason: The City of Bacolod under the former administration has filed a Civil Case No. 13-14174 against Francis Albert A. Torres of FAT Petron Service Station before the Regional Trial Court, Bacolod City pertaining to the said observation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

The City Mayor:

Direct the OIC City Accountant, the GSO and the City Engineer to strictly observe and implement the perpetual method of recording of inventory in accordance with the rules cited above.


File appropriate administrative and criminal charges against persons liable for the shortage of cement stocks at CEO bodega. 

Engr. Belly Aguillon, OIC City Engineer, direct:

The reconciliation of the inventory records of stocks of cement at the CEO bodega and establish who are accountable for the discrepancy of 1,500 bags of cement as reported by the OIC of the Property and Supply Management Section of the CEO.  

The strengthening and strict observance of internal control measures with regards to inventory handling.
observations.managementAction: 2006 AAR | For follow-up with the CLO about the status of the case.
observations.reason: Criminal complaint was filed with the court.

observations.managementAction: 2005 AAR | As of July, 2019 upon furnishing by the GSO of the copy of inventory of PPE, the Office of the City Accountant has made several adjustments to PPE and inventories which have threshold amount of ₱15,000.00 for asset recognition. The Office of the City Accountant to date, has designated a staff who will solely work on the PPEs and Inventories from 2018 and prior years. Reconciliation is on-going
observations.reason: Another request to COA for disposal of unserviceable properties is being prepared.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Create an Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory count of all PPE and submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). 

If possible designate another permanent personnel to handle the General Services Office to cope with the requirements. 	

Instruct the General Services Officer/Supply Officer-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

Require the General Services Officer/Supply Officer-Designate to issue property tags/ stickers for each PPE in order to identify and trace the location of the said PPEs and to issue corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for all the Properties, Plant and Equipment issued to officials and employees who have the custody thereto and to facilitate such undertaking, assign additional competent personnel in order to complete the needed property records.
observations.managementAction: CY2019 AAR | The General Services Office and the Accounting Office are now maintaining Stock and Property Cards and Supplies, and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger cards based on the result of inventory. The Local Chief mandated the Inventory Committee to update and conduct inventory count of all PPE and submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) led by the General Services Officer and Supply Officer. The Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer has regularly reconciled their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.
observations.reason: Management has assigned new personnel to conduct the inventory of PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Require the Municipal Accountant to verify and ascertain that payments for gasoline, oil and lubricants are duly supported with completely filled up driver’s trip tickets for proper evaluation and control on the propriety and reasonableness of fuel consumption;

Direct the personnel of the General Services Unit to conduct an orientation for the vehicle drivers to completely fill up or indicate the following data or information on the Driver’s Trip Tickets every time the government vehicles are used such as the following:

Vehicle Plate Number
Time of departure and arrival of the trip
Balance in Tank at the beginning and end of the trip
Distance Travelled
Odometer reading, if available
Liters of gasoline purchased and consumed for the trip
Place of Destination
Purpose of Travel
Name of Driver and Passengers and their respective signatures

Require the concerned vehicle drivers to submit to the GSU the driver’s trip tickets and the GSU to prepare and submit to the Audit Team, on a monthly basis, the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for proper analysis, verification and the necessary audit action on fuel consumption.
observations.managementAction: CY2019 AAR | The Municipal Accountant required the drivers, accomplished driver’s trip ticket, travel order and other necessary documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the Municipality’s officers and employees to impose proper application of the entitlement of traveling allowance as well as completeness and regularity of the essential documents like travel orders, bus tickets, e-ticket, boarding pass and itinerary of travel. 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely review the liquidation reports submitted by the officers and employees and require immediate refund for excess claims.
observations.managementAction:
CY2019
AAR | The Local Chief Executive directed all officers and employees to strictly adhere to the provisions of EO 77. However, there were some officials and employees who claimed travelling expenses that were not properly complied as stated in the provisions
observations.reason: Management will thoroughly review claims for travelling expenses.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the general and subsidiary ledgers, the different journals and other books of accounts to support the balances reflected in the financial statements. 

Adhere strictly to Section 111 of PD 1445 and Section 12 of the MNGAS for Local Government Units, Volume II, on the maintenance of Subsidiary Ledgers with the balance in the General Ledger and ensure the accuracy of the account.
observations.managementAction: CY2019 AAR | The Municipal Accountant prepared the General Ledgers (GL) and Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) as required.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to Local Chief Executive to: 

a.) 	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit disbursement vouchers after payment with complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant.

b.) 	Direct the Municipal Accountant to:

strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.

closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the latter to submit immediately the required reports within the prescribed period, and to facilitate the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.

c.) 	Augment additional personnel even Job Orders who are Accounting Majors to assist in the preparation of the reports and possibly train them both in the Treasury and the Accounting Department.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2019
AAR | The Municipal Treasure ensures to timely submit the DVs to the Office of the Municipal Accountant. And the Municipal Accounting Office help in finalizing the reports, conducted review and facilitated submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Management exerted effort to comply the submission of mandatory reports within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to properly plan the procurement of goods and supplies and strictly observe the Implementing Rules and Regulation of RA No. 9184 or the Procurement Law. Competitive Bidding should be the primary mode of procurement in compliance with RA 9184 to promote transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process. 

Require the Acting Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to draw check payments directly in the name of the supplier in conformity with existing regulations and withhold the appropriate taxes due the government.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2019
AAR | The BAC properly plan the procurement of goods and supplies and strictly adhere to the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184. The LGU followed the competitive bidding as the primary mode of procurement in compliance with the said law.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and the OIC-Municipal Engineer to comply with the regulations and furnish the Audit Team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within five (5) days after the perfection thereof for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same. Likewise, for those beyond the threshold of the audit team, after auditorial and legal review, it will forwarded to the Technical Audit Sector at the COA, Regional Office X.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2019
AAR | The BAC will comply to the submission of technical documents as required by your office based on the prescribed Bidding procedure and protocol based RA 9184. The BAC Secretariat shall comply with the regulations and furnish the audit team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within the prescribed period for timely review, evaluation and inspection. We will submit to your office copies of the contracts before issuance of award and notice to proceed. The Municipal Engineer, Supply Officer and the End User of the Procurement with the Accounting Office shall facilitate the request and conduct of technical inspection for all implemented projects.
observations.reason: The BAC had exerted effort to submit contracts within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

a.) Direct the General Services Officer-Designate to accomplish properly the purchase orders and see to it that the required conditions are indicated therein. 

b.) Submit the purchase orders to the Office of the Auditor irrespective of the amount within five (5) working days pursuant to the abovementioned regulations.

Submit copies of inspection of deliveries of goods and services within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance to enable this office to conduct timely review and validation.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2019
AAR | The Local Chief Executive has directed the General Services Officer-Designate to accomplish properly the purchase orders and submit the same within five (5) working days.
observations.reason: Management has assigned new personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to Local Chief Executive to direct the Bid and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and procurement-in-charge to comply with the regulations and furnish the Audit Team with the required copies of contract documents and copies of purchase orders and their supporting documents as well as notice of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 AAR | The BAC Chairman, Secretariat and other concerned personnel submitted the copies of contract agreements and PO together with the supporting documents and other reports to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Local Chief Executive to: 

a.) Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to submit the disbursement vouchers pertaining to 20% development fund after payment had been made together with its complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant.  

b.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor, otherwise a Notice of Suspension may be issued for lacked of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2019
AAR | The Municipal Treasure ensures to timely submit the DVs to the Office of the Municipal Accountant. And the Municipal Accounting Office help in finalizing the reports, conducted review and facilitated submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Management exerted effort to comply the submission of mandatory reports within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the Municipal Planning and Development with the Engineering     department to plan in carrying out accurate design and effective production of the projects in order to ensure immediate implementation when the funds are available, and 

Instruct the heads of the implementing offices to expedite the implementation of these projects to provide the expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries on time and to act promptly on issues or constraints causing the delay of the implementation of the projects. 

Require the OIC-Municipal Engineer to:

explain in writing as to the cause of delay in the implementation of the projects.

file and demand payment for liquidated damages for the number of days delayed. If not, issue a Blacklisting Order disqualifying the erring contractor from participating in the bidding of all government projects.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2019 AAR | The Local Chief Executive to has directed the Municipal Planning and Development Office with the Engineering Department to plan in carrying out accurate design and effective production of the projects  in order to ensure immediate implementation when the funds are available
The Management explained that the delay of the project is associated with the finalization of the Project Site and the Peace and Order Situation as these projects were implemented in the Conflict Affected Barangays. Moreover, the Three (3) PAMANA Infrastructure Projects were completed and the Municipal Engineer already submitted the inspection reports of these projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

a.) 	Require the LDRRMO to plan and ensure that the projects for the LDRRMF are supportive to disaster risk management activities as provided under Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, Section 21 of RA 10121 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-001 dated March 25, 2013 and to be cautious in charging expenditures to the LDRRMF. 

b.) Require the LDRRM Council to monitor and evaluate the use and disbursement of LDRRMF as required.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 AAR | The Management has planned and ensured that the projects for the LDRRMF are supportive to disaster risk management activities. The Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council is proactive in mainstreaming DRRM in Bacolod Lanao del Norte and that the disbursements are related to disaster preparedness.
observations.reason: Management will review its LDRRMF utilization report.

observations.recommendation:
We  have recommended to the Local Chief Executive:
	
Direct the focal point person to spearhead the establishment of GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation which will form part of the overall management information system of the agency. 

Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system as required under Section 5.1 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 AAR | The Local Chief Executive has designated the MSWDO, Mrs. Henedina A. Aban as GAD Focal Person. She was tasked to prepare corresponding quarterly GAD Accomplishment Report based from the GAD database and its submission and posting to the Full Disclosure Bulletin Portal. The GAD database was already submitted by newly appointed GAD Focal Person, Ms. Emily Cabug.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the GSO to:


         a) Fully update property card records  especially  on  acquisitions
            and disposals, including unserviceable and items  which  cannot
            be located for reconciliation with Report  on  Physical  Count,
            AREs, ICSs and with subsidiary ledgers maintained at the OCA;


         b) Conduct regular reconciliation with the OCA  to  ascertain  the
            correctness of the PPE accounts balances and for fair financial
            statements presentation;


         c) Maintain property cards on Land, Land  Improvements,  Buildings
            and    Other    Structures    with     complete     data     on
            acquisition/construction cost, appraised value, date  and  mode
            of acquisition for reconciliation with balances  shown  in  the
            Subsidiary Ledgers maintained at the OCA; and


         d) Prepare separate report on physical count of all tangible items
            below the capitalization  threshold  of  ₱15,000.00  and  issue
            Inventory Custodian Slip to persons responsible and accountable
            to establish accountability.


         We further recommended that OCA should reclassify items below  the
   capitalization threshold of ₱15,000  as  semi-expendable  inventory  or
   expense accounts for fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor:


         a) Direct the City Accountant and the City  Engineer’s  Office  to
            exert extra efforts for  the  speedy  reconciliation  of  their
            records regarding completed and on-going projects; and


         b) Address  the  problems  encountered  on  unimplemented  projects
            preferably  on  six  projects  with   total   appropriation   of
            ₱31,850,000.00, and instruct the City Engineer to speed up  its
            implementation so that constituents who  are  beneficiaries  of
            the projects will enjoy the benefits therefrom.

We recommended that:


         a) The City Mayor thru the City Accountant or personnel  in-charge
            require the officials and employees to liquidate and/or  refund
            immediately unliquidated cash advances, especially those  which
            remained outstanding for more than one year, otherwise,  refund
            the amount advanced to avoid institution of administrative  and
            criminal charges against the erring officials; and


         b) The City Accountant ensure that all cash  advances  to  officers
            and employees are  liquidated  within  the  prescribed  periods
            pursuant to Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-
            002 and do not allow additional cash advance to any official or
            employee who have unliquidated cash advances unless  settlement
            or proper accounting thereof is made.

We recommended that the City Mayor:


         a)  Through  the  City  Legal  Office  and  Office  of  the   City
            Accountant,  require  the  NGOs/POs   to   immediately   submit
            liquidation report for  financial  assistance  whose  programs,
            projects or activities were already completed, especially those
            with long overdue accounts.


         b) Designate a focal person/office to take charge in  the  regular
            monitoring and inspection of the accomplishment of the intended
            purpose to which the assistance is granted; and


         c) Through the City Legal Officer, institute legal action  against
            defaulting NGOS/POs which failed to complete a project  covered
            by  the  MOA  or  has  committed  material  violation  on   the
            provisions of the MOA or of COA Circular No. 2007-001.

         We also recommended that only NGOs/POs who have complied with  the
    terms and conditions of the MOA and undertaking, and have fully settled
    their  previous  financial  assistance  be  given   further   financial
    assistance by the City Mayor.

We recommended that the City Mayor:


         a) Demand immediate liquidation of financial assistance  from  the
            various leagues and federations,  and  require  the  return  or
            refund of the unexpended or unutilized amount pursuant to 3.1.2
            of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated  June  14,  2012,  otherwise,
            institute  appropriate  legal   action   against   the   erring
            officials; and


         b) Designate personnel to conduct regular  monitoring  on  project
            implementation to ascertain that  funds  granted  as  financial
            assistance to league/federation  were  utilized  based  on  the
            purpose intended for.


         We  also  recommended  that  only  leagues/federations   who   have
    liquidated previous financial assistance be given  the  opportunity  to
    receive future assistance from the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor:


         a) Thru a  designated  officer  in-charge,  demand  the  immediate
            liquidation  of  financial   assistance   granted   to   various
            barangays, reconcile the  unliquidated  accounts  with  barangay
            records and regularly monitor the compliance by barangays  with
            the terms and conditions in the MOA; and


         b) Disapprove the release of any subsequent  financial  assistance
            to the barangays  with  existing  unliquidated  amount  if  not
            supported  with  Certification  by  Accountant that   previous
            assistance has been liquidated and accounted in the books.

We recommended that the City Accountant:


         a) Prepare aging schedule on dormant  Other  Receivables  accounts
            and exert efforts in securing the required supporting documents
            and other data on existing conditions for  validation  so  that
            request  for  write-off  on  accounts  of  over  10  years   be
            facilitated. Consequently, prepare adjusting entries  for  fair
            presentation of accounts in the financial statements; and


         b) Verify Other Receivables accounts with abnormal credit balances
            to determine valid reasons and conditions for  reclassification
            to appropriate accounts.

We recommended that:


         a) The City Accountant record first  the  procured  and  delivered
            supplies  and  materials  to  inventory  account  to   recognize
            existence of assets, and as expense only  when  it  is  already
            consumed or used; and


         b) The General Services  Officer  maintain  stock  card  for  each
            supply and  other  inventory   items   delivered   and   accepted
            for reconciliation with supplies ledger cards at the Office of
            City Accountant so that correctness of these accounts  balances
            can be verified and validated.

We recommended that the Bids  and  Awards  Committee  prepare  the
    Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) in accordance with  the  prescribed
    format of GPPB for submission by HoPE to GPPB and the  same  be  posted
    for transparency and measurement purposes.

We recommended that the Chairperson on the Secretariat of the City
    GAD Focal Person System closely follow-up with  DILG  the  approval  of
    2019 GAD Plan and Budget for submission to COA Audit  Team  and  submit
    GAD accomplishment report in subsequent years in  accordance  with  the
    prescribed period provided in COA Circular No. 2014-001.


    ...

    We recommended that the Chairperson  of  GAD  Focal  Point  System
    Secretariat closely monitor and exert extra  efforts  for  the  maximum
    utilization of PPAs covered in GAD Plan and Budget and those attributed
    to other funds to ensure full attainment  of  women’s  empowerment  and
    gender equality.

We recommended that the City Planning and Development Officer:


         a) Jointly  conduct  with  City  Engineer  regular  monitoring  and
            evaluation of 20%  Development  Fund  projects  to  immediately
            address  the  causes  of  delays  and   to   fast   track   the
            implementation  of the uncompleted projects; and


         b) Install a project monitoring system to properly keep track  the
            implementation and  to  ensure  the  achievement  of  desirable
            outcomes from the delivery of  socio-economic  development  and
            environmental services to the constituents.

We recommended that the Local School Board closely coordinate with
    the  City  BAC  for  the  timely  processing  of  complete  procurement
    documents and in titling of land to maximize  the  utilization  of  the
    Special Education Fund appropriated for various PPAs.

We  recommended  that  the  City  Health  Officer  facilitate  the
    immediate distribution and use of larvicides/insecticides and test kits
    to the intended recipients/beneficiaries for the  immediate  prevention
    and control of dengue  epidemic,  thus,  30%  Quick  Response  Fund  be
    properly used and applied,  and prevent  possible  loss  or  waste  of
    government funds and resources.


         We also recommended that the  City  Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and
    Management  Officer  thru  the  City  Health  Officer   determine   the
    appropriate quantity of larvicides/insecticides, test  kits  and  other
    commodities needed for dengue prevention and  control  program  in the
    subsequent  periods  to  ensure  the  necessity  and  economy  in   the
    procurement.

We recommended that the Head  of  Department  of  Public  Services
    require the project in-charge to prepare and submit revised Program of
    Work and Detailed Estimate on  Management  and  Operation  of  Sanitary
    Landfill with complete  details,  specifications,  descriptions,  other
    quantifiable and relevant data, incorporating  any  modifications  made
    for proper monitoring and evaluation of actual accomplishments  and  to
    ascertain validity on payment of monthly billings.

We recommended that the Head  of  Department  of  Public  Services
    require the Contractor to prepare  a  revised  Schedule  of  Prices  or
    Program of  Work  and  Detailed  Estimate  of  Expenses  with  complete
    details, specifications and other quantifiable relevant data for proper
    monitoring and evaluation of actual accomplishments as against  monthly
    billing to ascertain validity of payments on  Collection,  Hauling  and
    Disposal of Garbage services.

We recommended that the City Mayor thru the Head of Department  of
    Public Services monitor the immediate implementation by the  Contractor
