    of the  proposed  corrective  actions  by  its  own  Pollution  Control
    Officer.


         ...

         We recommended that the Head of Department of Public Services thru
    the  Contractor,  immediately  act  for  the  demolition  of  temporary
    structures near the sanitary landfill to avoid the imposition of  fines
    and penalty, and possible suspension, cancellation  or  termination  of
    the  contract,  and  to  ensure  effective  and   proper   maintenance,
    management and operations of the facility.

We recommended that the Head  of Department  of Public  Services
    prepare and submit Program of Work and Estimate on the Construction
    of Bacolod  City  Sanitary  Landfill  Cell  No.  2  which  includes
    additional details, specifications and other relevant data and  the
    basis of unit cost on all equipment and consumables, and the number
    of days required to facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation  of
    project accomplishments.


    ...

    We recommended that the  Head  of  Department  of  Public  Services
    (DPS):


    a) Thru the  project  engineer/technical  person  submit  a  status
       report pertaining to the implementation  of  the  project,  with
       supporting documents for evaluation and possible  imposition  of
       liquidated damages; and


    b) Require the  Contractor  to  submit  detailed  and  quantifiable
       accomplishment report with  complete  supporting  documents  for
       verification and validation on any payment of progress billing.

We recommended that the City Mayor thru the City Engineer  closely
    monitor the implementation of the project, and require  the  Contractor
    for the immediate completion of the project in accordance with approved
    plans,  design  and  specifications,  otherwise  suspend  issuance   of
    certificate of completion and acceptance of the project, and impose the
    prescribed liquidated damages under the circumstances.

We recommended that the City Mayor thru the City Engineer  require
    the Contractor to speed-up the processing of the required license  with
    MARINA for the full completion of the Sum-ag Development Project, which
    is the Construction/Fabrication of Floating Restaurant  to  attain  the
    promotion of eco-tourism in the City.


         We recommended that the City Engineer thru  the  Project  Engineer
    coordinate with the City Treasurer for  the  imposition  of  liquidated
    damages from any succeeding payments or billing due to  Contractor,  if
    found warranted.

We recommended that the City Mayor:


         a) Thru the City Budget Officer strictly allocate  in  the  Annual
            Budget the amount equivalent to at least one  percent  (1%)  of
            the  Internal  Revenue  Allotment  (IRA)   for   LCPC   related
            activities; and


         b) Direct the LCPC Secretariat/focal person to  prepare  LDPC  and
            LIPC and submit the approved  copy  to  DILG  and  Auditor  for
            monitoring and evaluation on the implementation of the covering
            PPAs.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Create an Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory count of all PPE and submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
If possible designate another permanent personnel to handle the General Services Office to cope with the requirements.
Instruct the General Services Officer/Supply Officer-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.
Require the General Services Officer/Supply Officer-Designate to issue property tags/ stickers for each PPE in order to identify and trace the location of the said PPEs and to issue corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for all the Properties, Plant and Equipment issued to officials and employees who have the custody thereto and to facilitate such undertaking, assign additional competent personnel in order to complete the needed property records.

We have recommended to Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
Direct the General Services Officer-Designate to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting procedures:
i.) 	Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry.
ii.) Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
iii.) Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

We have recommended to Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Adhere strict compliance with the provisions prescribed by Section 325(a) of R.A. No. 7160, Section 14 of Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 and Section 4.1 and 4.2 of Local Budget Circular No. 98 dated October 14, 2011.
b.) Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Local Finance Committee to perform a thorough review and evaluation of the budget, whether annual or supplemental, prior to its submission to the Sangguniang Bayan to ensure that all Personal Services items are properly identified and classified as PS and that appropriation for PS items shall not exceed the PS limitation at any point in time during the budget year.
c) Make an effort in raising revenue/receipts to be able to implement and provide additional PS costs or by reducing its PS expenses.
d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly record the RATA to proper personal services accounts in order to present the actual PS expenditures of the municipality.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the head of Human Resource Management Office to submit the contract of service for the job order personnel to the Audit Team with specific duties and functions and reconcile the manpower complement based on the actual manpower and instruct to use the computer-generated DTRs based on the captured biometric date as basis for the preparation of payrolls for the establishment of a reliable number of days worked as the basis for the computation of their wages.
Prepare and issue policies and guidelines to address the issue on the necessity and propriety of hiring Job Orders.
Require the Job Order personnel to submit personal records, undergo interview and document the evaluation of performance to give assurance that the risk identified above are addressed inorder to strengthen the controls in the hiring of job order personnel.
Formulate a policy and internal control in the hiring of job order workers such as but not limited to requiring specific and detailed description of duties and   responsibilities to eliminate workings having no definite purpose, if any.
Consider either one of the following:
Appoint Job Order personnel as regular employees if they are qualified and believes that their services are essential and contributory to the improvement of agency’s operations or,
Stop hiring Job Order and fill up the vacant plantilla positions in accordance with the Civil Service Commission rules and regulations. Prioritize the division with undermanned in the filling-up of vacant plantilla position.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the Municipal Accountant to verify and ascertain that payments for gasoline, oil and lubricants are duly supported with completely filled up driver’s trip tickets for proper evaluation and control on the propriety and reasonableness of fuel consumption;
Direct the personnel of the General Services Unit to conduct an orientation for the vehicle drivers to completely fill up or indicate the following data or information on the Driver’s Trip Tickets every time the government vehicles are used such as the following:
Vehicle Plate Number
Time of departure and arrival of the trip
Balance in Tank at the beginning and end of the trip
Distance Travelled
Odometer reading, if available
Liters of gasoline purchased and consumed for the trip
Place of Destination
Purpose of Travel
Name of Driver and Passengers and their respective signatures
Require the concerned vehicle drivers to submit to the GSU the driver’s trip tickets and the GSU to prepare and submit to the Audit Team, on a monthly basis, the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for proper analysis, verification and the necessary audit action on fuel consumption.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipality’s officers and employees to impose proper application of the entitlement of traveling allowance as well as completeness and regularity of the essential documents like travel orders, bus tickets, e-ticket, boarding pass and itinerary of travel.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely review the liquidation reports submitted by the officers and employees and require immediate refund for excess claims.

We have recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the general and subsidiary ledgers, the different journals and other books of accounts to support the balances reflected in the financial statements.
Adhere strictly to Section 111 of PD 1445 and Section 12 of the MNGAS for Local Government Units, Volume II, on the maintenance of Subsidiary Ledgers with the balance in the General Ledger and ensure the accuracy of the account.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the Property Officer to issue property tags/ stickers for each PPE in order to identify and trace the location of the said PPEs and to issue corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for all the Properties, Plant and Equipment issued to officials and employees who have the custody thereto and to facilitate such undertaking, assign additional competent personnel in order to complete the needed property records;
Renew the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) every January of the third year after issuance; and
Strictly comply with the provisions of Section 492 of GAAM Vol. I for the succeeding procurement and issuance of Properties, Plant and Equipment.

We have recommended to Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit disbursement vouchers after payment with complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant.
b.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the latter to submit immediately the required reports within the prescribed period, and to facilitate the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to properly plan the procurement of goods and supplies and strictly observe the Implementing Rules and Regulation of RA No. 9184 or the Procurement Law. Competitive Bidding should be the primary mode of procurement in compliance with RA 9184 to promote transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process.
Require the Acting Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to draw check payments directly in the name of the supplier in conformity with existing regulations and withhold the appropriate taxes due the government.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and the OIC-Municipal Engineer to comply with the regulations and furnish the Audit Team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within five (5) days after the perfection thereof for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same. Likewise, for those beyond the threshold of the audit team, after auditorial and legal review, it will forwarded to the Technical Audit Sector at the COA, Regional Office X.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Direct the General Services Officer-Designate to accomplish properly the purchase orders and see to it that the required conditions are indicated therein.
b.) Submit the purchase orders to the Office of the Auditor irrespective of the amount within five (5) working days pursuant to the abovementioned regulations.
Submit copies of inspection of deliveries of goods and services within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance to enable this office to conduct timely review and validation.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive as Chairman of the LSB to:
To orient the Local School Board on the eligible expenditures allowed on the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations.  If possible, coordinate with the Department of Education so that possible double budgeting and overlapping of charging can be avoided.
Require the Local School Board to strictly follow the provisions of RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars No. 1, s. 2017 in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto. Any issues or conflicts arising from its implementation and cases not covered by the provisions shall be submitted to the DepEd for resolution and in consultation with the municipal government.
To refrain from charging to SEF the programs/projects/activities that is not authorized under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and implement the same in order to maximize the use of the SEF for its intended purpose.
To have a conscious and concerted effort of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute.
Require the Municipal Accountant to review the charging of the Special Education Fund strictly in accordance with the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended to Local Chief Executive to direct the Bid and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and procurement-in-charge to comply with the regulations and furnish the Audit Team with the required copies of contract documents and copies of purchase orders and their supporting documents as well as notice of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same.

We recommended to Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to submit the disbursement vouchers pertaining to 20% development fund after payment had been made together with its complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant.
b.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor, otherwise a Notice of Suspension may be issued for lacked of supporting documents.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development with the Engineering     department to plan in carrying out accurate design and effective production of the projects in order to ensure immediate implementation when the funds are available, and
Instruct the heads of the implementing offices to expedite the implementation of these projects to provide the expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries on time and to act promptly on issues or constraints causing the delay of the implementation of the projects.
Require the OIC-Municipal Engineer to:
explain in writing as to the cause of delay in the implementation of the projects.
file and demand payment for liquidated damages for the number of days delayed. If not, issue a Blacklisting Order disqualifying the erring contractor from participating in the bidding of all government projects.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Require the LDRRMO to plan and ensure that the projects for the LDRRMF are supportive to disaster risk management activities as provided under Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, Section 21 of RA 10121 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-001 dated March 25, 2013 and to be cautious in charging expenditures to the LDRRMF.
b.) Require the LDRRM Council to monitor and evaluate the use and disbursement of LDRRMF as required.

We  have recommended to the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the focal point person to spearhead the establishment of GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation which will form part of the overall management information system of the agency.
Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system as required under Section 5.1 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the General Services Officer to: Fully update records on acquisitions and disposals in the PPE cards for Machinery and Equipment, Furniture and Fixtures, Transportation Equipment and other PPEs for reconciliation with RPCPPE, AREs and subsidiary ledgers maintained at the Office of City Accountant; and Maintain property cards on Land, Land Improvements, Buildings and Other Structures for reconciliation with balances shown in the Subsidiary Ledgers maintained at the Office of City Accountant. We also recommended that the Physical Inventory Committee submit list of PPEs not found during the conduct of physical count to determine losses and liability of persons accountable as provided in Section 149 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 23, 1992.
observations.managementAction: As of July, 2019 upon furnishing by the GSO of the copy of inventory of PPE, the Office of the City Accountant has made several adjustments to PPE and inventories which have threshold amount of ₱15,000.for asset recognition. The Office of the City Accountant to date, has designated a staff who will solely work on the PPEs and Inventories from 2018 and prior years. Reconcilia tion is on-going.
observations.reason: Previously, there was no enough staff to work on the reconcilia tion aside that data from the GSO was incomplete. Agreement with the GSO to furnish the OCA with copies of Property Return Slips was not done. PPE cards were not still fully updated especially pertaining to PYs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the City Mayor to require the City Legal Office the immediate delivery by the Vendor of the TCT duly annotated with court decision/order pertaining to the expropriated three parcels of land intended for widening and improvement of Honorata Manalo Avenue (Formerly Mandalagan-Buri Road), Bacolod City as provided in the Compromise Agreement dated January 17, 2018 for attachment to DV to ascertain the validity and propriety of the payment and to establish ownership by the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the City Engineer’s Office to exert efforts in the reconciliation of their records regarding completed and on-going projects. We recommended also that the City Mayor and the City Engineer immediately address the problems encountered on unimplemented projects and speed up the implementation of both partially and unimplemented projects to avoid losses and to attain the intended purposes.
observations.managementAction: There is a continuous reconcilia tion of the accounts is being made, however there are CIPs which are already reported by the Engineers as completed but no payment has been made to suppliers, thus, the difference of the record of the Engineers and the OCA
observations.reason: There is a need to define the bases for recognitio n of CIP as PPE whether to reconcile during full payment or at the time it is completed. There were still CIP accounts pertaining to PYs not transferre d to asset accounts due to lack of documents/data/accomplish ment report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor: Prioritize the demand for immediate settlement/liquidation of unliquidated cash advances pursuant to Item 6.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 for those granted before December 31, 2011; Direct the City Accountant to ensure that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the previous one given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made pursuant to Section 89, PD No. 1445; Require the City Accountant or assign personnel in-charge to follow-up the demand letters sent to officials and employees who failed to submit liquidation reports of their cash advance after the prescribed period, otherwise, implement the withholding of the payment of salary especially to active officials and employees with outstanding cash advances; Require the City Accountant to send follow-up letter to personnel assigned in other government agencies demanding liquidation/settlemen t/refund of their unliquidated cash advances. Make further representation on this regard to the Head of the Agency concerned and its Accountant; File a request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated cash advances as provided in Section 8.2 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016, which is the “Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash Advances, and Fund Transfers of National Government Agencies (NGAs), Local Government Units (LGUs) and Government-Owned and Controlled Corporations (GOCCs); Require the City Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the unliquidated cash advances, prepare the necessary adjusting entries for any correction, reclassification and recovery/settlement of previously written-off accounts, and prepare aging of dormant unliquidated cash advances to support the request for write-off as provided in Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2016-005; and Require the City Accountant to review closely the accounting entries made on the cash advances and liquidation of the Advances to Officers and Employees and Payroll Fund before approval in eNGAS to immediately adjust and arrive with correct accounts balances during the period.
observations.managementAction: Aside from the demand letter sent to the employees, the City Accountant has devised to collect from employee who cannot claim for reimbursem ent of their traveling expenses unless they have not liquidated their cash advance. Instead of the COA recommenda tion to deduct from the salary, the non-reimbur sement of their traveling/ training expenses is being observed so as not to compromise the Net Take Home Pay of the employee.. The Office of the City had obtained a certificat ion from the City Civil Registrar of the deceased employees, however, it does not suffice the requiremen ts of the COA for write-off of their accounts.. Since 2013, the Office of the City Accountant has been in constant follow up the employees accounts and periodical ly writes reminders/ demand letters to concerned employees.
observations.reason: There is still a total amount of ₱57,814,12 3.84 remained unliquidat ed with ₱26,943,55 2.03 is outstandin g for over 1 year. An increase by ₱16,156,81 0.21 or 38.79% over PYs balance, mainly due to delayed settlement of CAs for operating expenses/t ime bound activities due to lack of supporting documents and signatures. There are employees who have retired from service that their addresses cannot be located. There are also employees who had separated/ absconded from their work leaving cash advances unliquidat ed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: Through the City Legal Office and City Accounting Office, require the NGOs/POs to immediately submit liquidation report for financial assistance whose programs, projects or activities were already completed and with long overdue accounts; Designate a focal person/office to take charge on the regular monitoring and inspection on the accomplishment of the intended purpose; Thru the City Legal Officer, institute legal action against defaulting NGOs/POs which failed to complete a project covered by the MOA or has committed material violation of the provisions of the MOA or of COA Circular No. 2007-001; Approve the grant of another financial assistance only to NGOs/POs that have complied with the terms and conditions of the MOA and undertaking and have fully settled their previous financial assistance; and Require the City Accountant to closely monitor the full settlement of the financial assistance granted to NGOs/POs to determine accountability and ascertain the validity of liquidation.
observations.managementAction: No Financial Assistance is released when the previous one is not liquidated Previous financial assistance were under different administra tion/Barangay, the records of which were not turned-over since all the officers of the barangays are co-terminous with the Punong Barangay, the records of which were not turned-ove r. There is an ongoing demand for liquidatio n of financial assistance to Liga ng mga Barangay, Federation President, Noli Villarosa of Tangub.. The City is no longer giving Financial Assistance to those Leagues/Fed erations that have outstanding unliquidat ed financial assistance.
observations.reason: There were still 29 NGOs/POs with unliquidat ed FAs amounting to ₱6,891,300 .03 pertaining to PYs due to unlocated addresses and change of officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: Demand from the recipient leagues and federations for the immediate liquidation of the financial assistance given or require the return or refund of the unexpended or unutilized amount pursuant to 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, otherwise, appropriate legal action be instituted; Designate a focal person to conduct regular monitoring on project implementation to ascertain that funds granted as financial assistance to league/federation is utilized for the intended purpose; Approve the grant of financial assistance to leagues/federations if duly supported with complete required documents and certification by the City Accountant that fund previously transferred has been liquidated and accounted.
observations.managementAction: Leagues and Federation s with financial assistance dated from 2013 and below are no longer at their listed address more so that the present officers of the leagues are not the same. There is an ongoing demand for liquidatio n of financial assistance to Liga ng mga Barangay, Federation President, Noli Villarosa of Tangub. The City is no longer giving Financial Assistance to those Leagues/Fede rations that have outstandin g unliquidate d financial assistance.
observations.reason: There were 7 Leagues pertaining to PYs which have unsettled FAs amounting to ₱5,195,000 .00 due to unlocated addresses and change of officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: a) Direct the City Accountant to demand the immediate liquidation of fund transfer/financial assistance granted to various barangays in CYs 2015 to 2018 by setting specific timelines for the liquidation considering that the terms of the agreement had already been violated; b) Approve the release of any subsequent fund transfer and the City Accountant certify on the completeness of supporting documents only if supported with Certification by Accountant that previous fund transfer has been liquidated and accounted. Require the City Accountant to exert extra efforts in securing documents especially pertaining to prior years’ financial assistance amounting to ₱515,000 and ascertain if a corresponding liability with the City is recorded in the said barangay so that the City could require the immediate liquidation, otherwise, authority for write-off shall be requested by the City Mayor in accordance with the guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005. Further, prior years’ subsidies from PAGCOR, RPTA, SEF and Trust Funds and those without details should also be written-off to arrive at correct and valid Due from LGUs account; and Immediately assign focal person responsible in regular monitoring on the compliance by barangays and other LGUs with the terms and conditions in the MOA as well as validation of actual accomplishments vis-à-vis purpose for which the fund transfer was granted.
observations.managementAction: Full implementa tion on-going No financial assistance is released without liquidatio n of the previous ones.. Previous financial assistance were under different administra tion/Punon g Barangay, the records of which were not turned-ove r since all the officers of the barangays are co-terminou s with the Punong Barangay. The City Treasurer’s Office are being furnished the City Accountant every month for reconcilia tion with their cash books. There were still unliquidat ed FAs at ₱9,644,308 .54 with total of 25 barangays pertaining to 2018 and PYs or a total of ₱5,194,308 .54.
observations.reason: There was turn-over of records by the previous administra tion in the barangay. There was still unliquidat ed FAs at ₱9,644,308 .54 with total of 25 barangays pertaining to 2018 and PYs or a total of ₱5,194,308 .54.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant: Review and analyze the prior years’ reconciling items as to its actual transactions and description and secure the supporting documents to establish its validity and for proper adjustment/correction per books; and Prepare Journal Entry Voucher to take up all valid book reconciling items at once after the bank reconciliation statement is completed and approved pursuant to Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to ascertain the correctness of balance of Cash in Bank – LCCA in an appropriate period.

We recommended that the City Treasurer send a written notice to the payees for the cancellation of the checks and requiring them to surrender the checks for issuance of a new one. Further, we recommended that the City Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the adjustment of stale checks in the books if the checks are still in the Office of the City Accountant.

We recommended that the City Accountant reclassify the tangible items with total amount of ₱4,222,525.00 as Inventory to ascertain correctness of Inventory and Equipment accounts. We recommended that the General Services Office issue Inventory Custodian Slip to the recipients of distributed tangible items with unit cost below the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00 to establish accountability and to properly monitor existence and location.

We recommended that the General Services Officer and department/office supplies in-charge prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit the same with the original copy of the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) duly received by the end user to report the used/consumed inventories to the City Accountant for expense recognition to arrive at the correct balance of inventory accounts as of December 31, 2018.

We recommended that the City Accountant: Effect the reversion of unpaid obligations that remained outstanding for more than two years totaling ₱26,550,415.87 to the unappropriated surplus pursuant to Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445; Prepare correcting entry on the negative balances in Accounts Payable amounting to ₱20,234,501.71 by debiting Prior Years’ Adjustment account to charge expenses incurred in prior years and to credit Accounts Payable to arrive at a normal balance; and Evaluate the other accounts included in the “Accounts Payable” with total amount of ₱65,220,623.17 and reclassify to Other Payables and other appropriate liability accounts.

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat submit copies of purchase orders, contracts and supporting documents within five working days from the date of issuance of the P.O./Contracts for timely auditorial review as well as the timely conduct of technical evaluation.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City DSSD personnel in-charge to conduct regular monitoring and prepare periodic nutrition status monitoring report of the day care children beneficiaries after the implementation of the Supplementary Feeding Program under BUB Fund, as provided for in the approved Project Proposal, and at the same time to determine the attainment of the objectives of the program.
observations.managementAction: The Dept. of Social Services is keeping tract of funds downloaded from the national government for the CY2019 and control of funds as well as Fund Utilizatio n Reporting are now in place
observations.reason: Liquidatio ns Reports from various Day Care Centers are difficult to collate since the Day Care Centers are scattered throughout the city. There were still monitoring reports not submitted for SFP conducted during 2019 due to voluminous day care centers and data to collect.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: Require the GAD Focal Person to prepare on time the annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) for review and approval by DILG and submit the approved copy and the corresponding Accomplishment Report to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed period provided in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014; Approve charges and disbursements on GAD Fund only if supported with DILG reviewed and approved GPB to ascertain validity and propriety of disbursements.
observations.managementAction: For the CY 2019 the department responsibl e in the control and preparatio n of reports has designated a focal person and report is being made periodical ly and reconciled with the Office of the City Accountant.
observations.reason: The approved GPB and accomplishm ent report were not yet submitted on time in 2019 due to revisions/corrections in the plans/repo rts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant reclassify the procured Truck Mounted Sewer Jetting and Vacuum Combination Equipment charged to Other Machinery and Equipment account amounting to ₱20,980,000.00 to Heavy and Disaster Equipment account to reflect correct accounts balances.

We recommended that the LSB refrain from charging salaries/allowances of locally-hired Child Development Teachers and/or Day Care Workers, etc. under the ECCD Program, to ensure the legality of the disbursements. We also recommended that the City Mayor, City Budget Officer, City Accountant and the City Schools Division Superintendent refrain from approving, obligating, processing and paying subsequent obligations and disbursements without specific appropriation in the Local School Board (LSB) Budget.

We recommended that the City Accountant certify and the City Mayor approve any subsequent payments to the Environmental and Sanitary Services only if supported with documents pertaining to remittance by the contractor/payee to BIR, SSS and Pag-Ibig for complete documentation and to ascertain the validity and propriety of disbursement. We recommended also that the Head of Department of Public Services require the submission by the contractor/payee of documents on the monthly remittance to BIR, SSS and Pag-Ibig in compliance with the terms and conditions of the two Contracts of Services and for attachment to the disbursement voucher.

We recommended that the City Development Council ensure that only projects that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure are included in the 20% Annual Development Plan to facilitate the immediate implementation of the projects. We also recommended that the City Planning and Development Office, the City Engineer’s Office and other offices that are involved in the implementation of 20% Development Fund projects, immediately address the causes of delays and fast track the implementation of the unimplemented projects. Regular monitoring of project implementation should be assigned to specific office to ensure implementation and the achievement of the desirable outcomes from the delivery of socio-economic development and environmental services to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The CPDO is planning to design and install a monitoring system to keep track the accomplishment of projects. The CPDO is directing its effort to improve the Project Monitoring System of the City that will determine the causes of delay of the project implementation to effectively and efficiently monitor the document tracking of projects and its implementation and physical accomplishment as well, with close coordination to the Project Monitoring Committee.
observations.reason: There are projects in 2015-2016- which were fully implemente d, however payment was not made due to lack of GPPB requiremen t on bidding period, Several vouchers have complete supporting documents except deficiency in the bidding, hence were not paid..There are also unpaid obligation s but the projects were already completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) maximize the utilization of the Special Education Fund (SEF) for the intended PPAs to ensure the attainment of the purposes.
observations.managementAction: Program of Works of the covering PPAs are being prepared.
observations.reason: Due to delay in procurement process and lack of final POWs and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor thru the Head of Department of Public Services, submit program of works specifying the detailed estimates of the contract of services on Management and Operation of Landfill Facility. We recommended that the City Mayor thru the DPS submit accomplishment report duly verified by the project in-charge as to the actual accomplishment based on the approved program of works. Further, payments on the bill of quantities should be based on the actual accomplishment with necessary supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: GSO will reconcile with the City Assessor’s Office and the OCA.
observations.reason: There are payments in 2015-2016- which were fully implemente d, however payment was not made due to lack of GPPB requiremen t on bidding period, Several vouchers have complete supporting documents except deficiency in the bidding, hence were not paid..There are also unpaid obligation s but the projects were already completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor thru the Head of Department of Public Services, submit program of works specifying the detailed estimates of the contract of services on Management and Operation of Landfill Facility and that the DPS submit accomplishment report duly verified by the project in-charge as to the actual accomplishment based on the approved program of works. Further, payments on the bill of quantities should be based on the actual accomplishment with necessary supporting documents.

We recommended that the City Mayor closely monitor the liquidation of financial assistance/transfer to NGOS/POs, leagues/confederation s and barangays to ensure the settlement within 60 days upon completion of the project or activity or within 30 days upon receipt of the grant per MOA to ensure the implementation of the intended purpose and to safeguard the fund from possible loss.

We recommended that the City Treasurer immediately send collection or demand letters to 820 delinquent stallholders at Libertad South Public Market, Burgos North Public Market and Central Market. In case of failure of concerned stallholders to pay their long overdue market rental fees despite the demand, it is recommended that the City Administrator impose the provision of Section 46 of the Revised Market Code of Bacolod City by revoking or cancelling his lease right and ejectment, after observance of due process. The City Administrator also revoke, after observance of due process, the lease contract of stall owners who sub-leased their stalls or have their privileges as market stall awardees sold to other persons contrary to Sections 19 and 46 of the Revised Market Code of Bacolod City.
observations.reason: There were stallholde rs with unpaid delinquent accounts due to financial constraints. Revocation or cancellati on is pending due to the request of stallholde rs and financial incapabili ty.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer and the City Accountant reconcile their records per cashbook, general and subsidiary ledgers immediately while documents are still available for references. Henceforth, the two offices must conduct reconciliation at least every end of each quarter and any items subject for correction must be adjusted promptly to present a reliable Cash – Local Treasury balance or the accountable officer’s balance of accountability at any given time as required under Sec. 181 (c), GAAM, Volume I. Moreover, the City Treasurer require the Cashier to rule, foot and certify the cashbook pursuant to Section 26, Volume II of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconcilia tion Statements of the Office of the City Accountant are being furnished the City Treasurer’ s Office every month for reconcilia tion with their cash books.
observations.reason: No adjustment in the Cash Book is being made upon receipt of the Bank Reconciliat ion Statement from the Office of the City Accountant for correction of reconcilin g items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer immediately send collection or demand letters to 23 delinquent stallholders at Manokan Country. In case of failure of concerned stallholders to pay their long overdue market rental fees despite the demand, it is recommended that the City Administrator impose the provision of Section 46 of the Revised Market Code of Bacolod City by revoking or cancelling his lease right and ejectment, after observance of due process.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent periodical ly by the City Treasurer prompting the stall lessees to start payment of rental fees.
observations.reason: There were stallholde rs with delinquent accounts not collected due to financial constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare as soon as possible the necessary adjustment to book up the reversion of the remaining unutilized PDAF funds to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund and the City Treasurer to draw the disbursement voucher covering the return of the unutilized PDAF funds to the National Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The checks issued for the return of PDAF to the DBM were not remitted such that it became stale..The city is willing to return the unutilized PDAF and will inquire with the DBM on the correct process of returning the funds.
observations.reason: Waiting for reply from DBM on the refund of unutilized PDAF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor/Board of Trustees of the Provident Fund submit immediately the authorization and guidelines from the DBM allowing subsequent government monetary contributions to the Provident Fund. The City Accountant ensure that no fund transfers to the Provident Fund will be released without prior authority and guidelines issued by the DBM.
observations.managementAction: There is an on-going talk between the administrat ion and people in the Provident Fund to refund to the city the government share collected and remitted to the Provident Fund Association.
observations.reason: The City is waiting for final decision/legal basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: Justify the P340.00 per square meter purchase price paid for Felisa Solid Waste Disposal Facility; Justify why the lots were classified as Residential Zone per CPDO certification when the classification in the declaration of the real property taxes was Agricultural; and Cause the reduction of the purchase price for the acquired property considering that the dumpsite facility cannot be accessed and be useful to the City Government except thru the unappraised roads right of way.
observations.managementAction: Justificat ion was already submitted by the City thru the Legal Office.
observations.reason: Subject to result of final decision from COA Commission Proper.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: refrain from appropriating further any public funds to be contributed as government share to the Provident Fund; direct the City Budget Officer and the City Treasurer to refrain from obligating and disbursing any amount already earmarked as government share to the Provident Fund for Calendar Year 2010; and direct the City Accountant to account for the total government share given to the Provident Fund since the time of the creation of the fund in 1995 up to December 31, 2009.
observations.managementAction: There is an on-going talk between the administrat ion and people in the Provident Fund to refund to the city the government share collected and remitted to the Provident Fund Association.
observations.reason: The City is still waiting for the final decision/legal basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: direct the City Legal Officer to file appropriate criminal charges against the persons liable and responsible for the above-discussed deficiencies; direct the CGSO to revisit and review the procedures involved in the property management and strengthen the internal control to address the lapses in the procedures; direct the CGSO to conduct an inventory of all unserviceable motor vehicles that had been returned to the CGSO, protect and prevent the cannibalization of these vehicles and speed up their disposal.
observations.managementAction: Inventory and Inspection Report was already submitted to COA Region VI for disposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Property Officer: to inspect the above donated properties to determine their existence and whereabouts, their present condition, the provision of protection further deterioration and cannibalization; prepare, maintain record and submit the documents for these properties like the Property Ledger Cards, ARE, Property Return Slip; request for the proper disposal if found unserviceable; make a report on the irregular and improper use, losses and cannibalization of the said properties and recommend for the filing of charges against all persons liable; and revisit and review the system of property management observed by the agency and recommend measures to correct the lapses in the system. We further recommended that the City Mayor take action and implement the COA recommendations concerning exceptions on the property management of present and prior years; and adopt measures to correct the system of property management and install internal control measures to safeguard the properties of the City of Bacolod.
observations.managementAction: Data on donated properties are not available. No record as to the transfer of property. Identify properties which can be easily processed and make adjustments in the books of the city.
observations.reason: Until now, no action was undertaken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City General Services Officer: to secure and complete his file of the owner’s copy of the TCTs owned by the City; and to regularly reconcile his records of TCTs with that of the City Assessor and the City Accountant and adopt procedures to address the exception noted above.
observations.managementAction: GSO will reconcile with the City Assessor’s Office and the OCA.
observations.reason: CGSO has not report regarding this matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: withhold payments of any money due to the nine (9) accountable officers enumerated above; designate only permanent employees to act as special disbursing officers; require the immediate settlement of all outstanding cash advances; stop the granting of additional cash advances to officials and employees with unsettled cash advances; refrain from processing and approving reimbursements of travel or other expenses of officials/employees with unsettled cash advances.
observations.managementAction: For follow-up with the CLO about the status of the case. Civil Case was filed against FAT Petron Station under Civil Case 13-141-74. The Office of the City had obtained a certificat ion from the City Civil Registrar of the deceased employees, however, it does not suffice the requiremen ts of the COA for write-off of their accounts.. Since 2013, the Office of the City Accountant has been in constant follow up the employees accounts and periodical ly writes reminders/ demand letters to concerned employees.
observations.reason: There is still a total amount of ₱57,814,12 3.84 remained unliquidat ed with ₱26,943,55 2.03 is outstandin g for over 1 year. An increase by ₱16,156,81 0.21 or 38.79% over PYs balance, mainly due to delayed settlement of CAs for operating expenses/t ime bound activities due to lack of supporting documents and signatures. There are employees who have retired from service that their addresses cannot be located. There are also employees who had separated/ absconded from their work leaving cash advances unliquidat ed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor, together with the OIC GSO, the BAC Chairman and members and the OIC City Engineer review and investigate the breakdown of control in the mode and the process of procurement. We further recommend that the City Mayor demand from FAT Petron: the return of the balance of P1.213M; the return of additional cost incurred due to price increase per liters of fuel of P50,255.48; and legal interest on the P1.217M from November 2007 up to time of refund.
observations.managementAction: For follow-up with the CLO about the status of the case. Civil Case was filed against FAT Petron Station under Civil Case 13-141-74. The City of Bacolod under the former administra tion has filed a Civil Case No. 13-14174 against Francis Albert A. Torres of FAT Petron Service Station before the Regional Trial Court, Bacolod City pertaining to the said observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: Direct the OIC City Accountant, the GSO and the City Engineer to strictly observe and implement the perpetual method of recording of inventory in accordance with the rules cited above; File appropriate administrative and criminal charges against persons liable for the shortage of cement stocks at CEO bodega; The reconciliation of the inventory records of stocks of cement at the CEO bodega and establish who are accountable for the discrepancy of 1,500 bags of cement as reported by the OIC of the Property and Supply Management Section of the CEO; The strengthening and strict observance of internal control measures with regards to inventory handling.
observations.managementAction: For follow-up with the CLO about the status of the case. Civil Case was filed against FAT Petron Station under Civil Case 13-141-74. Engr. Belly Aguillon, OIC City Engineer, direct: The reconciliation of the inventory records of stocks of cement at the CEO bodega and establish who are accountable for the discrepancy of 1,500 bags of cement as reported by the OIC of the Property and Supply Management Section of the CEO. The strengthening and strict observance of internal control measures with regards to inventory handling.
observations.reason: Criminal complaint was filed with the court.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor: the GSO to determine officials accountable/liable to refund the value of the parts cannibalized and/or the value of the motor vehicle lost; the City Mayor create a special team to conduct/canvass the prevailing market prices of secondhand/recondition ed units, parts/assembly/ accessories on actual costs of missing items of the unserviceable motor vehicles discussed; the OIC GSO, submit recommendation for the disposal of one unit Mitsubishi road grader and Sakai roller; the end-users of the Honda and Suzuki motorcycles included in the unserviceable properties for disposal, submit justification why the said motorcycles were found in their possession, still serviceable and used by them, without notice to the GSO; the City Mayor initiate the filing of appropriate administrative and criminal charges against accountable official/employees responsible for the cannibalization/loss of government properties described above; the OIC of the General Services Office (GSO), establish controls, provide proper procedures and documentation in the return of unserviceable properties, most especially with regards to the motor vehicles and heavy equipment, from the time of the return up to the storage and disposal. Likewise, the OIC of the GSO define job accountabilitie s/responsibilit ies of the personnel involved in the property custodianship.
observations.managementAction: Inventory and Inspection Report was already submitted to COA Region VI for disposal. The OIC of the General Services Office (GSO) will reconcile with the Office of the City Assessor and the OCA.
observations.reason: Another request to COA for disposal of unserviceab le properties is being prepared.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

a.)	The Local Chief Executive should direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the complete and latest physical inventory count of all PPE and submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). 

b.)	Instruct the General Services Officer-Designate/ Supply Officer and the Acting Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.
observations.managementAction: CY2018 AAR | The General Services Office and the Accounting Office are now maintaining Stock and Property Cards and Supplies, and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger cards based on the result of inventory. The Local Chief issued Executive Order creating the Inventory Committee with a mandate to update and conduct inventory count of all PPE and submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) led by the General Services Officer and Supply Officer. The Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer has regularly reconciled their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to direct the General Services Office to update the list of properties that needs to be insured and submit the required documents to GSIS. This will ensure that all government properties are adequately covered by insurance in order to protect the interest of the government. 

Likewise, require the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate budget for the processing and payment of premiums to ensure that government properties are fully covered with insurance.
observations.managementAction: CY2018 AAR | The Local Chief Executive has directed the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate budget for the processing and payment of premiums for the insurance of government properties insurance. The MBO gather the needed documents for the application of GSIS Insurance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Local Chief Executive to: 

a.)	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit disbursement vouchers after payment with complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant.  

b.)	Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.

c.)	Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the latter to submit immediately the required reports within the prescribed period, and to facilitate the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: CY2018 AAR | The Municipal Accounting Office help in finalizing the reports, conducted review and facilitated submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Management exerted effort to comply the submission of mandatory reports within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

a.)	Require the Human Resource Management Officer to compute the accumulated 	leave credits of LGU employees. 

b.)	Require the Municipal Accountant to record the Terminal Leave Benefits due to the employees to ensure proper recording and monitoring of earned leave benefits, in the absence of budget, disclosed it in the Notes to Financial Statements. 

c.)	Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Human Resource Management Officer to institute prioritization of payments to the employees soon to retire.
observations.managementAction: CY2018 AAR | The Human Resource Management Officer computed accumulated leave credits of the LGU employees and a JEV was prepared by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

a.)	Direct the head of Human Resource Management Office to submit the contract of service of the job orders and consultants with specific duties and functions and reconcile the manpower complement based on the actual manpower.

b.)	Consider either one of the following:

•	Appoint Job Order personnel as regular employees if they are qualified and believes that their services are essential and contributory to the improvement of agency’s operations or, 
 
•	Stop hiring Job Order and fill up the vacant plantilla positions in accordance with the Civil Service Commission rules and regulations. Prioritize the division with undermanned in the filling-up of vacant plantilla position. 

c.)	Formulate a policy and internal control in the hiring of job order workers such as but not limited to requiring specific and detailed description of duties and   responsibilities to eliminate workings having no definite purpose, if any.
observations.managementAction:
CY2018 AAR | The Human Resource Management Office submitted the contract of service of the job orders and consultants as of December 31, 2018. However, there is no reconciliation as to the manpower complement based on the actual manpower. 
Moreover, the Management does not formulate a policy and internal control in the hiring of job order workers such as but not limited to requiring specific and detailed description of duties and   responsibilities to eliminate workings having no definite purpose, if any.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:

a.)	Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to abide the Implementing Rules and Regulation on RA No. 9184 or the Procurement Law. Bidding should be the primary mode of procurement in compliance with RA 9184. 

b.)	Require the Acting Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to draw check payments directly in the name of the supplier in conformity with existing regulations and withhold the appropriate taxes due the government.
observations.managementAction: CY2018 AAR | The Bids and Awards Committee have attended refresher and training course for RA 9184. The BAC has now recommended and implemented alternative mode of procurement of goods and services that is above P100,000.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to comply with the regulations and furnish the audit team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same. Otherwise, the Audit Team will issue Notice of Suspension.
observations.managementAction: CY2018 AAR | The BAC will comply to the submission of technical documents as required by your office based on the prescribed Bidding procedure and protocol based RA 9184. The BAC Secretariat shall comply with the regulations and furnish the audit team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within the prescribed period for timely review, evaluation and inspection. We will submit to your office copies of the contracts before issuance of award and notice to proceed. The Municipal Engineer, Supply Officer and the End User of the Procurement with the Accounting Office shall facilitate the request and conduct of technical inspection for all implemented projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Local School Board and other employees concern to strictly adhere with the provision of Sec. 100 of RA 7160, DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 and other existing budgeting, accounting, and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: CY2018 AAR | The Local School Board submitted to the Office of the Auditor the copy of the approved Local School Board Budget and Utilization Report of the SEF Budget of CY 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Local Chief Executive to direct the Bid and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and procurement-in-charge to comply with the regulations and furnish the Audit Team with the required copies of contract documents and copies of purchase orders and their supporting documents as well as notice of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same.

We recommended to Local Chief Executive to:

a.)	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the disbursement vouchers pertaining to 20% development fund after payment with complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant.

b.)	Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: CY2018 AAR | The BAC will comply to the submission of technical documents as required by your office based on the prescribed Bidding procedure and protocol based RA 9184. The BAC Secretariat shall comply with the regulations and furnish the audit team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within the prescribed period for timely review, evaluation and inspection. We will submit to your office copies of the contracts before issuance of award and notice to proceed. The Municipal Engineer, Supply Officer and the End User of the Procurement with the Accounting Office shall facilitate the request and conduct of technical inspection for all implemented projects/

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

a.)	Direct the Municipal Planning and Development with the Engineering  department to plan in carrying out accurate design and effective production of the projects in order to ensure immediate implementation when the funds are available, and 

b.)	Instruct the heads of the implementing offices to expedite the implementation of these projects to provide the expected benefits to the intended beneficiaries on time and to act promptly on issues or constraints causing the delay of the implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction:
CY2018 AAR | The Local Chief Executive to has directed the Municipal Planning and Development Office with the Engineering Department to plan in carrying out accurate design and effective production of the projects  in order to ensure immediate implementation when the funds are available
The Management explained that the delay of the project is associated with the finalization of the Project Site and the Peace and Order Situation as these projects were implemented in the Conflict Affected Barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management to plan and ensure that the projects for the LDRRMF are supportive to disaster risk management activities as provided under Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and Section 21 of RA 10121 and to be cautious in charging expenditures to the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: CY2018 AAR | The Management has planned and ensured that the projects for the LDRRMF are supportive to disaster risk management activities. The Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council is proactive in mainstreaming DRRM in Bacolod Lanao del Norte and has strengthened Community Education and Public Awareness through Print and News Broadcast Media.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:

Direct the focal point person to spearhead the establishment of GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation which will form part of the overall management information system of the agency. 

Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system as required under Section 5.1 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: CY2018 AAR | The Local Chief Executive has designated the MSWDO, Mrs. Henedina A. Aban as GAD Focal Person. She was tasked to prepare corresponding quarterly GAD Accomplishment Report based from the GAD database and its submission and posting to the Full Disclosure Bulletin Portal. Delayed updating of the GAD database could be attributed to her health condition and that the Local Chief Executive has appointed a new GAD Focal Person in the person of Ms. Emily Cabug for the timely submission and updating of GAD database.

a.) Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to abide the Implementing Rules and Regulation on RA No. 9184 or the Procurement Law. Bidding should be the primary mode of procurement in compliance with RA 9184. 

b.) Require the Acting Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to draw check payments directly in the name of the supplier in conformity with existing regulations and withhold the appropriate taxes due the government.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee have attended refresher and training course for RA 9184. The BAC has now recommended and implemented alternative mode of procurement of goods and services that s above P100,000.
observations.reason: (Reiterated in this 2018 AAR)

observations.recommendation: Require the Local School Board and other employees concern to strictly adhere with the provision of Sec. 100 of RA 7160, DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 and other existing budgeting, accounting, and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: To correct this, they promise to submit to the Audit Team, a copy of the approved Local School Board Plan. The Accounting Office will also furnish its corresponding Quarterly Accomplishment and Utilization Report as these items were also posted in the full disclosure bulletin and portal.
observations.reason: (Reiterated in this 2018 AAR)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require  the  General  Services
Officer to:


      a) Fully update records on acquisitions and disposals in  the  PPE
         cards for Machinery  and  Equipment,  Furniture  and  Fixtures,
         Transportation Equipment and other PPEs for reconciliation with
         RPCPPE, AREs and with  subsidiary  ledgers  maintained  at  the
         Office of City Accountant; and


      b) Maintain property cards on Land, Land  Improvements,  Buildings
         and Other Structures for reconciliation with balances shown  in
         the  Subsidiary  Ledgers  maintained  at  the  Office  of  City
         Accountant.


      We also recommended that the Physical Inventory  Committee  submit
list of PPEs  not  found  during  the  conduct  of  physical  count  to
determine losses and liability of persons accountable  as  provided  in
Section 149 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 23, 1992.

We recommended the City Mayor to require the City Legal Office the
immediate delivery by the Vendor of the TCT duly annotated  with  court
decision/order pertaining to the expropriated  three  parcels  of  land
intended  for  widening  and  improvement  of  Honorata  Manalo  Avenue
(Formerly Mandalagan-Buri  Road),  Bacolod  City  as  provided  in  the
Compromise Agreement dated January 17,  2018 for  attachment  to  DV  to
ascertain the validity and propriety of the payment  and  to  establish
ownership by the City.

We strongly  recommended  that  the  City  Mayor  direct  the  City
Accountant and the City Engineer’s Office to exert efforts  in  the
reconciliation  of  their  records  regarding  completed  and  on-going
projects.


We recommended also that the City  Mayor  and  the  City  Engineer
immediately  address  the  problems  encountered  on  unimplemented
projects and speed up the implementation of  both  partially  and
unimplemented projects to avoid losses and to attain the intended
purposes.

We recommended that the City Mayor:


      a) Prioritize the demand for immediate  settlement/liquidation  of
         unliquidated cash advances pursuant to Item 6.0 of COA  Circular
         No. 2012-004 for those granted before December 31, 2011;


      b) Direct the City Accountant to ensure  that  no  additional  cash
         advance shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the
         previous one  given  to  him  is  first  settled  or  a  proper
         accounting thereof is made pursuant to Section 89, PD No. 1445;


      c) Require the City Accountant or assign  personnel  in-charge  to
         follow-up the demand letters sent to  officials  and  employees
         who failed to submit liquidation reports of their cash advance
         after  the  prescribed   period,   otherwise,   implement   the
         withholding of the  payment  of  salary  especially  to  active
         officials and employees with outstanding cash advances;


      d) Require  the  City  Accountant  to  send  follow-up  letter  to
         personnel  assigned  in  other  government  agencies   demanding
         liquidation/settlement/refund   of   their   unliquidated   cash
         advances.  Make further representation on  this  regard  to  the
         Head of the Agency concerned and its Accountant;


      e) File a request for authority to write-off  dormant  unliquidated
         cash advances as provided in Section 8.2 of  COA  Circular  No.
         2016-005, dated December 19, 2016, which is the “Guidelines and
         Procedures on the Write-off  of  Dormant  Receivable  Accounts,
         Unliquidated Cash  Advances,  and  Fund  Transfers  of  National
         Government Agencies (NGAs), Local Government Units  (LGUs)  and
         Government-Owned and Controlled Corporations (GOCCs);


      f) Require the City Accountant to  conduct  regular  and  periodic
         verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of  the
         unliquidated cash advances,  prepare  the  necessary  adjusting
         entries    for    any    correction,    reclassification     and
         recovery/settlement of previously  written  off  accounts,  and
         prepare aging of dormant unliquidated cash advances  to  support
         the request for write-off as  provided  in  Section  7  of  COA
         Circular No. 2016-005; and


      g) Require the City Accountant to review  closely  the  accounting
         entries made on  the  cash  advances  and  liquidation  of  the
         Advances to Officers and  Employees  and  Payroll  Fund  before
         approval in eNGAS to immediately adjust and arrive with  correct
         accounts balances during the period.

We recommended that the City Mayor:


      a) Through the City  Legal  Office  and  City  Accounting  Office,
         require the NGOs/POs to immediately submit  liquidation  report
         for financial assistance whose programs, projects or  activities
         were already completed and with long overdue accounts;


      b) Designate a focal person/office to take charge on  the  regular
         monitoring and inspection on the accomplishment of the intended
         purpose;


      c) Thru the City Legal  Officer,  institute  legal  action  against
         defaulting NGOs/POs which failed to complete a project  covered
         by the MOA or has committed material violation of the provisions
         of the MOA or of COA Circular No. 2007-001;


      d) Approve the grant  of  another  financial  assistance  only  to
         NGOs/POs that have complied with the terms  and  conditions  of
         the MOA and undertaking and have fully  settled  their  previous
         financial assistance; and


      e)  Require  the  City  Accountant  to  closely  monitor  the  full
         settlement of the financial assistance granted to  NGOs/POs  to
         determine  accountability  and   ascertain   the   validity   of
         liquidation.

We recommended that the City Mayor:


      a) Demand from the  recipient  leagues  and  federations  for  the
         immediate liquidation of  the  financial  assistance  given  or
         require the return or refund of the  unexpended  or  unutilized
         amount pursuant to 3.1.2 of COA  Circular  No.  2012-001  dated
         June  14,  2012,  otherwise,  appropriate   legal   action   be
         instituted;


      b) Designate a focal  person  to  conduct  regular  monitoring  on
         project implementation  to  ascertain  that  funds  granted  as
         financial assistance to league/federation is utilized  for  the
         intended purpose; and


      c)   Approve   the    grant    of    financial    assistance    to
         leagues/federations if duly supported  with  complete   required
         documents and certification by the City  Accountant  that  fund
         previously transferred has been liquidated and accounted.

We recommended that the City Mayor:


      a) Direct the City Accountant to demand the immediate  liquidation
         of  fund  transfer/financial  assistance  granted   to   various
         barangays in CYs 2015 to 2018 by setting specific timelines for
         the liquidation considering that the terms of the agreement had
         already been violated;


      b) Approve the release of any subsequent  fund  transfer  and the
         City Accountant  certify  on  the  completeness  of  supporting
         documents only if supported with  Certification  by  Accountant
         that previous fund transfer has been liquidated  and  accounted
         for in the books;


      c) Require the City Accountant to exert extra efforts in  securing
         documents  especially  pertaining  to  prior  years’   financial
         assistance  amounting  to   ₱515,000   and   ascertain   if   a
         corresponding liability with the City is recorded in  the  said
         barangay  so  that  the  City  could  require   the   immediate
         liquidation,  otherwise,  authority  for  write-off   shall   be
         requested by the City Mayor in accordance with  the  guidelines
         provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005.  Further,  prior  years’
         subsidies from PAGCOR, RPTA, SEF  and  Trust  Funds  and  those
         without details should also be written-off to arrive at correct
         and valid Due from LGUs account; and


      d)  Immediately  assign  focal  person  responsible   in   regular
         monitoring on the compliance by barangays and other  LGUs  with
         the terms and conditions in the MOA as well  as  validation  of
         actual accomplishments vis-à-vis purpose  for  which  the  fund
         transfer was granted.

We recommended that the City Accountant:


      a) Review and analyze the prior years’ reconciling items as to its
         actual transactions and description and secure  the  supporting
         documents  to   establish   its   validity   and   for   proper
         adjustment/correction per books; and


      b) Prepare Journal  Entry  Voucher  to  take  up  all  valid  book
         reconciling  items  at  once  after  the  bank   reconciliation
         statement is completed and approved pursuant to Section 3.3  of
         COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to ascertain  the
         correctness of balance of Cash in Bank – LCCA in an appropriate
         period.

We recommended that the City Treasurer send a  written  notice  to
the payees for the cancellation of the checks  and  requiring  them  to
surrender the checks for issuance of a new one.


Further, we recommended that the City Accountant prepare a Journal
Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the adjustment of  stale  checks  in  the
books if the checks are still in the Office of the City Accountant.

We recommended that the City Accountant  reclassify  the  tangible
items with total amount of  ₱4,222,525.00  as  Inventory  to  ascertain
correctness of Inventory and Equipment accounts.


We recommended that the General Services  Office  issue  Inventory
Custodian Slip to the recipients of  distributed  tangible  items  with
unit cost below the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00 to establish
accountability and to properly monitor existence and location.

We   recommended   that   the   General   Services   Officer   and
department/office supplies in-charge prepare the  Summary  of  Supplies
and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit the same with the original  copy
of the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) duly received by the  end  user
to report the used/consumed inventories  to  the  City  Accountant  for
expense recognition to arrive  at  the  correct  balance  of  inventory
accounts as of December 31, 2018.

We recommended that the City Accountant:


      a)  Effect  the  reversion  of  unpaid  obligations  that  remained
         outstanding for more than two years totaling ₱26,550,415.87  to
         the  unappropriated  surplus  pursuant   to   Section   98   of
         Presidential Decree No. 1445;


      b) Prepare correcting entry on the negative  balances  in  Accounts
         Payable amounting to ₱20,234,501.71  by  debiting  Prior  Years’
         Adjustment account to charge expenses incurred in  prior  years
         and to credit Accounts Payable to arrive at  a  normal  balance;
         and


      c) Evaluate the other accounts included in the  “Accounts  Payable”
         with total amount of ₱65,220,623.17  and  reclassify  to  Other
         Payables and other appropriate liability accounts.

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat submit copies of  purchase
orders, contracts and supporting documents  within  five  working  days
from the date of issuance of the P.O./Contracts for  timely  auditorial
review as well as the timely conduct of technical evaluation.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City DSSD  personnel
in-charge to conduct regular monitoring and prepare periodic  nutrition
