             existence  and  status  of  income-generating   or   livelihood
             projects of beneficiaries  under  the  SLP  of  Asosasyon  Sang
             Magagmay  Nga  Mangingisda  and  Bacolod  Pantawid   Livelihood
             Assistance to determine whether the objective on the  grant  of
             seed capital/loan assistance for self-sufficiency was attained;
             and


          b) The CSSDO demand from the beneficiaries the loan  repayment  of
             seed capital to ensure rollback to other members and groups and
             to attain the intended purpose of SLP.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City Treasurer immediately send collection
    or demand letters to 820  delinquent  stallholders  at  Libertad  South
    Public Market, Burgos North Public Market and Central Market.


          In case of failure of concerned stallholders  to  pay  their  long
    overdue market rental fees despite the demand, it is  recommended  that
    the City Administrator impose  the  provision  of  Section  46  of  the
    Revised Market Code of Bacolod City by revoking or cancelling his lease
    right and ejectment, after observance of due process.


          The City  Administrator  also  revoke,  after  observance  of  due
    process, the lease contract of stall owners who sub-leased their stalls
    or have their privileges as market stall awardees sold to other persons
    contrary to Sections 19 and 46 of the Revised Market  Code  of  Bacolod
    City.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the  City  Accountant  prepare  Journal  Entry
    Voucher to take up all valid book reconciling items at once  after  the
    bank reconciliation process is finished pursuant to Section 3.3 of  COA
    Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 in  order  to  ascertain  the
    correctness of balances of Cash in Bank – LCCA.


          Further, we recommended that the City Accountant immediately  make
    written  representation  to  the  bank  regarding  the   various   long
    outstanding bank reconciling items not  acted.  Otherwise,  appropriate
    adjustments must be prepared for bank discrepancies that the bank  will
    definitely not consider.   Henceforth,  the  City  Accountant  promptly
    inform the bank of any error or  discrepancy  noted  in  the  statement
    while the documents are still available to ensure  that  correction  is
    effected in the subsequent bank statement and within the time frame  to
    submit the data needed for reconciliation. The discrepancies should  be
    thoroughly investigated to determine the person(s) responsible for  the
    error, either the city or the bank so it could be promptly instituted.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City Treasurer  and  the  City  Accountant
    reconcile their records per cashbook, general  and  subsidiary  ledgers
    immediately  while  documents  are  still  available  for   references.
    Henceforth, the two offices must conduct reconciliation at least  every
    end of each quarter and  any  items  subject  for  correction  must  be
    adjusted promptly to present a reliable Cash – Local  Treasury  balance
    or the accountable officer’s balance of  accountability  at  any  given
    time as required under Sec. 181 (c), GAAM, Volume I.


          Moreover, the City Treasurer require the Cashier to rule, foot and
    certify the cashbook pursuant to Section 26, Volume II of the Manual on
    NGAS for LGUs.

Recommendation:

          The City Accountant maintain subsidiary ledger for  other  revenue
    collection and disbursing officers for reconciliation purposes with the
    cashbooks to fully ascertain the correctness of their  accountabilities
    and account balances and to safeguard fund from possible loss.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City GAD focal person prepare  the  Annual
    GAD Plan and Budget using the prescribed format  under  Joint  Circular
    No. 2013-01 of the  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA,  submit  it  to  the  Provincial
    Planning and Development Office then to the DILG  Regional  Office  for
    review and  approval.  Only  programs,  projects  and  activities  that
    directly address gender issues and concerns should be included.




          Prepare and submit the annual  GAD  accomplishment  report  within
    five (5) working days from the end of January of the  preceding  year.
    The report should contain actual accomplishments based on PPAs  in  GAD
    Plan with corresponding actual amount paid thereon.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City allocate  in  the  Annual  Budget  at
    least 1% of IRA for LCPC related activities pursuant to Section  15  of
    Republic Act No. 9344.


          Further, the City direct the focal person of LCPC to prepare  LDPC
    and LIPC and maximize the LCPC Fund to identified PPAs provided in DILG
    Memorandum Circular No.  2012-120 to ensure full implementation  on  the
    protection of children.

Recommendation:

          We recommended  that  the  City  Mayor  cause  the  remittance  of
    premiums,  contributions  and  loan  repayments  due  to   GSIS   after
    reconciliation and determination by the City Accountant of the  correct
    amount to avoid further  penalties  and  to  ensure  the  availment  of
    benefits by the officers and personnel members.




          We recommended that the City Accountant secure data supporting the
    Schedule of Due to GSIS on all funds to determine the correct amount of
    unremitted premiums, contributions and loan  repayments  to  GSIS.  The
    personnel in-charge reconcile the accounts with debit balance to arrive
    at a correct amount of Due to GSIS.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City Mayor cause the remittance of premium
    contributions due to PhilHealth after reconciliation and  determination
    by the City Accountant of the correct amount to avoid further penalties
    and to ensure the availment of program benefits by the members.


          We recommended that the City Accountant secure data supporting the
    Schedule of Due to PhilHealth on all funds  to  determine  the  correct
    amount of unremitted premium contributions  to  PhilHealth  and  paying
    members. The personnel in-charge  reconcile  the  accounts  with  debit
    balance to arrive at a correct amount of Due to PhilHealth.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City BAC Secretariat Office submit  copies
    of purchase orders, contracts  and  supporting  documents  within  five
    working days from the date of issuance of the P.O./Contracts for timely
    auditorial  review  as  well  as  the  timely  conduct   of   technical
    evaluation.


          We recommended further that BAC Secretariat ensure that  the  date
    of receipt is indicated upon signing for conformity by the supplier  on
    the penalty clause in the PO before submission to the  COA  Office  and
    for complete documentation.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City Accountant reclassify Other  Payables
    account in the Trust Fund to Trust Liabilities-DRRMF pertaining to  the
    transfer of unexpended balance  of  DRRMF  and  charge  the  subsequent
    transfers to said account to reflect the correct account balance.


          We recommended further that the  City  Accountant  reclassify  the
    procured IT Equipment  items  charged  to  IT  Equipment  and  Software
    account amounting  to  ₱277,416.00  to  Disaster  Response  and  Rescue
    Equipment (1-07-05-090) account to reflect correct account balances.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City Planning and Development Office,  the
    City Engineer’s Office and other  offices  that  are  involved  in  the
    implementation of 20% Development Fund  projects,  immediately  address
    the causes  of  delays  and  fast  track  its  implementation.  Regular
    monitoring of project implementation should  be  assigned  to  specific
    office to achieve  desirable  outcomes  from  the  delivery  of  socio-
    economic development and environmental services to the constituents.


          We recommended further that management should be  more  aggressive
    in  the  implementation  of  projects  to  attain  higher   performance
    efficiency level.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that City of Bacolod and  the  Local  School  Board
    refrain from charging expenses which are not among the primary  purpose
    of the SEF and strictly adhere to the regulations on the prioritization
    of expenses charged against SEF as provided under Section 272 of the
    Local Government Code (RA 7160) and Item No. 4.0 of Joint Circular  No.
    01 s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG, dated January 19, 2017.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that:

          a) The monitoring officers issue Daily Monitoring  Certificate  to
             ascertain the adequacy/sufficiency of the  garbage  collection,
             hauling  and  disposal  services  of  the  contractor  and  for
             appropriate action of the DPS; and


          b) The City Mayor thru the Solid Waste Management  Board  and  DPS
             conduct  ocular  inspection  on  the  markings   and   covering
             materials  on  all  collection  trucks  to   determine   actual
             condition and require the contractor to replace  old  materials
             to ensure efficient delivery of garbage collection, hauling and
             disposal services. Review on the actual performance against the
             obligations of the contractor provided in the Contract shall be
             regularly made by the Board and DPS to determine compliance and
             to impose penalty in case  of  violations  and  for  corrective
             action.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
a.) The Local Chief Executive should ensure that the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, and Section 4(6) and 89 of P.D. 1445 in granting cash advances be complied with.
b.) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit all the liquidations reports on time pursuant to Section 5 of COA Circular 97-002.
c.) Require the Acting Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the granting and liquidation of cash advances. Send letter of demand to employees of their unliquidated cash advances.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
a.) The Local Chief Executive should refrain from granting of cash advances to Elective Officials other than their traveling expenses.
b.) Require the Acting Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the granting and liquidation of cash advances. Send letter of demand to employees of their unliquidated cash advances.

Recommendations:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) 	Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for each Revenue Collectors.
b.) Require the Revenue Collectors to submit their Report of Collections and Deposits to the accounting division individually.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
a.)	The Local Chief Executive should create an Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory count of all PPE and submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
b) 	Instruct the General Services Officer-Designate/Supply Officer and the Acting Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the General Services Officer-Designate and the Acting Municipal Accountant to maintain Stock and Property Cards and Supplies and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, pursuant to paragraphs 3 and 4, Section 114, Chapter 7, Volume 1 of the National Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units. The required records shall also be the basis by the General Services Office for its reconciliation with the actual physical inventory.

Recommendations:
We recommended to Local Chief Executive to:
a.) 	Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
b.) 	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit on time and forward to the Accounting Office the financial reports to facilitate the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Local Chief Executive to:
a.) 	Direct the Municipality’s officers and employees to impose proper application of the entitlement of traveling allowance as well as completeness and regularity of the essential documents like travel orders, bus tickets, e-ticket, boarding pass and itinerary of travel.
b.) 	Require the employees concerned to refund the excess amount of the travelling expenses claimed.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct annual physical count of the City’s properties and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 on the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I so the Audit Team has ample time to validate the reported balances of PPE. Further, he must maintain property cards for land, land improvements, leased improvements including buildings, health centers, markets, slaughterhouses and other structures and ensure that updating of property cards on furniture & fixtures, Office and IT equipment, motor vehicles & other transportation equipment, machineries and heavy equipment are regularly undertaken.

Lastly, the City Accountant and the GSO implement reconciliation of their records on PPEs at least once a year to fully eliminate discrepancies in PPE inventory records.
observations.managementAction:

CMO endorsed to COA on 10/8/17 the annual physical inventory report as of August 23, 2017 of all PPEs submitted by GSO.
observations.reason: Other PPE accounts such as Land, Land Improvemen ts, Buildings and Other Structures, and Books were not included in the Annual Physical Inventory Report. Property cards were not updated and not reconciled with PPE records in the Office of the City Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant and the CEO exert sincere efforts in the reconciliation of their records regarding completed and on-going projects. The City Accounting Office to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers transferring all other completed projects to the appropriate PPE and Infrastructure Assets accounts. Henceforth, the City Accounting Office and the CEO regularly reconcile their records so that the affected accounts are accordingly adjusted and in order to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended also that the CEO speed up the implementation of both partially and unimplemented projects to attain the intended objectives.

Referred to OIC-City Engineer Noel V. Impreso on Nov. 20, 2017 requesting status of the corresponding projects. Copy furnished COA on Nov. 21, 2017.
observations.reason: The CEO failed to submit accomplishment report on the status of the projects implemented as basis by the Office of the City Accountant for closing completed CIP projects and for reconcilia tion.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

The City Mayor demand immediate settlement/liquidation of unliquidated cash advances pursuant to Item 6.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 for those granted before December 31, 2011; The City Mayor and the City Accountant ensure that no additional cash advance shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the previous one given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made pursuant to Section 89, PD No. 1445; The City Mayor require the City Accountant to send demand letters to officials and employees who failed to submit liquidation reports of their cash advances after the prescribed period for them to liquidate their cash advances or implement the withholding of the payment of salary especially to active officials and employees with outstanding cash advances; The City Accountant likewise send the bill to personnel assigned in other government agencies demanding liquidation/settlement/refund of their unliquidated cash advances. Make representation on this regard to the Head of the Agency concerned and its Accountant; and The City Accountant maintain adequate subsidiary ledgers for cash advances and undertake appropriate adjustment for erroneous accounts recorded in the account advances to officers and employees and special disbursing officers to ensure that said account is properly presented in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction:
2016 AAR, 2015 AAR, 2014 AAR, 2013 AAR, 2012 AAR, 2005 AAR

Reply dated April 21, 2015 received by COA on the same day. The Office of the City Accountant sent demand/rem inder letters to concerned officers and employees to liquidate their unsettled cash advance informing them of the provisions of COA Circulars regarding this account.
observations.reason: Other accounts were not sent with demand letter due to the unknown address particul arly those connected with the NGAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the incumbent City Mayor demand immediate settlement/liquidation of unliquidated cash advances for Intelligence Operations and for APEC, both were chargeable against Confidential or Intelligence Fund pursuant to General Guidelines of COA Circular No. 2003-003 dated July 30, 2003 and Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

The City Mayor and the City Accountant ensure that cash advances charged to Confidential Funds are settled in accordance with the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Per DSSD Office the liquidation report with supporting documents on the CA on CIF was submitted by the Chairman-COF on Sept. 23, 2016 to the COA – Head Audit Unit, Quezon City per photocopy of LBC Express, Inc. OR No. SBN081000001449.
observations.reason: Other accounts were not fully supported; Credit advice for the liquidation of the cash advance was not yet released.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor assign an office to take charge in the close monitoring of project implementation funded out of the fund granted and conduct regular inspection on the accomplishments of the NGOs/POs. It is also recommended that the City Accounting or Legal Office follow-up the liquidation of long outstanding fund transfer to NGOs/POs which remained unliquidated.

We recommended further that the City Accountant or Legal Office follow-up the liquidation of the outstanding balances and demand/remind the NGOs/POs to liquidate the financial assistance granted to them and provide copy furnished COA.
observations.managementAction:
2016 AAR, 2015 AAR, 2014 AAR, 2013 AAR, 2012 AAR

The Office of the City Accountant sent Demand/reminder letters to various NGOs/POs to liquidate the financial assistance granted to them, copy furnished the COA.
observations.reason: There were still NGOs/POs not liquidated the financial assistance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor demand from the recipient leagues and federations for the immediately submission of liquidation reports on the financial assistance given or require the return or refund of the unexpended or unutilized amount pursuant to 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, otherwise appropriate legal action be instituted.

We recommended also that the City Mayor designate personnel to conduct constant monitoring of project implementation to ascertain that funds granted as financial assistance is utilized for the intended purpose.

Reply furnished to COA.
observations.reason: The concerned leagues have not liquidated the financial assistance granted and have not submitted the liquidation reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The City Accountant strongly demand the immediate liquidation of fund transfer/financial assistance granted to various barangays for CY 2016 amounting to ₱3,700,050.00 and for CY 2015 at ₱1,401,240.00 by setting specific timelines for the liquidation considering that the terms of the agreement had already been violated; The City Accountant must secure documents especially pertaining to prior years’ financial assistance amounting to ₱1,315,000.00 wherein the names of barangays are specifically indicated in the SL and ascertain if a corresponding liability with the City is recorded in the books of said barangay so that the City could require the immediate liquidation. Otherwise, authority for write-off shall be requested by the City Mayor in accordance with the guidelines under Item 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and The City Mayor assign active official or personnel responsible in monitoring compliance with the terms and conditions in the MOA as well as validation of actual accomplishments vis-à-vis purpose for which the fund transfer was granted.

The City Accountant sent demand/reminder letters to barangays and coordinated with the barangay section of the City Treasurer’s Office to monitor compliance.
observations.reason: There were still barangays that have not fully liquidated financial assistance and some prior years’ documentation remain incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The City Accountant ensure that all the basic requirements for procurement through public bidding are complete before certifying the disbursement vouchers pursuant to Items 9.0, 9.1 and 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; Additionally, adequate controls should be instituted in the disbursement of government funds to attain the objectives of the program and projects of the City, safeguard its resources and promote efficiency and economy in the use thereof; The City Mayor approve only the payment of procured items through public bidding if duly supported with complete and appropriate supporting documents; and The BAC Chairman or the BAC Secretariat submit to COA Office the PO and contract together with supporting documents within five days after its perfection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

The Office is preparing and consolidating supporting documents and the BAC Secretariat ensures submission to COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Treasurer immediately send collection or demand letters to 23 delinquent stallholders at Manokan Country.

In case of failure of concerned stallholders to pay their long overdue market rental fees despite the demand, it is recommended that the City Administrator impose the provision of Section 46 of the Revised Market Code of Bacolod City by revoking or cancelling their lease rights and ejectment, after observance of due process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer follow-up with the SP the issuance of resolution for the return/transfer of dormant funds to the National Agencies which granted the fund or revert back the dormant accounts to the National Treasury or Current Account of the General Fund of the City based on the MOA.

Justification and supporting documents were already submitted by BAC which is under evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up all valid book reconciling items at once after the bank reconciliation process is finished pursuant to Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 in order to ascertain the correctness of balances of Cash in Bank – LCCA. Further, we recommended that the City Accountant immediately make written representation to the bank regarding the various long outstanding bank reconciling items not acted. Otherwise, appropriate adjustments must be prepared for bank discrepancies that the bank will definitely not consider. Henceforth, the City Accountant promptly inform the bank of any error or discrepancy noted in the statement while the documents are still available to ensure that correction is effected in the subsequent bank statement and within the time frame to submit the data needed for reconciliation. The discrepancies should be thoroughly investigated to determine the person(s) responsible for the error, either the city or the bank so it could be promptly instituted.

The Office of the City Accountant is preparing adjusting entries and shall submit representation to the bank for the long outstanding reconciling items.

We recommended that the City Treasurer and the City Accountant reconcile their records per cashbook, general and subsidiary ledgers immediately while documents are still available. Henceforth, the two offices must conduct reconciliation at least every end of each quarter and any items for adjustment must be corrected and resolved promptly in order to present reliable Cash – Local Treasury balance or the accountable officer’s balance of accountability at any given time as required under Sec. 181 I, GAAM, Volume I.

Moreover, the City Treasurer require the Cashier to rule, foot and accomplish the certification in the cashbook pursuant to Section 26, Volume II of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.

The Office of the City Accountant and City Treasurer are coordinating on the reconciliation and preparing supporting documents to reconcile the differences. The City Treasurer explained that differences may be due to timing differences and cut off dates.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the DRRM Officer facilitate the immediate installation of the Automatic Transfer Switch and the distribution of Emergency Back Pack Kits to the identified rescuers and responders so the purpose of the purchase will be realized and also to prevent wastage of government resources.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council prepare the annual LDRRM Investment Plan pursuant to 5.1.2 and Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 by specifying therein the projects, programs and activities to be implemented under the Mitigation Fund. The Annual Work and Financial Plan of the DRRM Fund prepared by the DRRM Office should only serve as a supporting document to the LDRRMFIP considering that it is not required under the said COA Circular.

Requested from GSO an annual physical inventory report as of December 31, 2016 of all PPEs for reference in the reconciliation of this account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU thru the CSSDO monitor regularly the existence of income-generating or livelihood projects to determine whether the objective of the loan assistance for income augmentation on the beneficiaries was attained; The CSSDO officer in-charge demand from the beneficiaries the full and timely payment of loan amortization to ensure the relending to other members and groups and to safeguard the fund from possible loss and mismanagement. The individual beneficiaries remit directly to the City Treasurer’s Office on time and in full the monthly amortization paid by the beneficiaries.

2016 AAR

Requested from DSSD Head on October 5, 2017 for a copy of their reply to COA on this AOM.
observations.reason: Monitoring on the implementation of the intended projects was not fully conducted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the City Mayor ascertain if the City will continue the transfer or donation of the four units motorcycles to MTCC, Bacolod City. In case the City pursue said donation or assistance to MTCC, Bacolod City, the donation or transfer must be covered by a Deed of Donation duly ratified by the SP of the City to make the transfer valid as provided in Section 186 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 23, 1992.

The Heads of Offices of the City ascertain the requirement needs of their Office when preparing any request for approval of the City Mayor to avoid wastage of government resources. Equipment or other government property shall be issued only in accordance with the purpose and for the intended users they were procured.
observations.managementAction:
2016 AAR

GSO will reconcile with the City Assessor’s Office and the OCA. Requests were made and documentation being compiled.
observations.reason: Transfer or donation of the 4 units of Kawasaki motorcycle to the MTCC was still not acted by the City Mayor and required documentation not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant exert efforts for the timely preparation and submission of trial balances and financial reports under the PPSAS to maintain its relevance and usefulness in management decision making and in the conduct of audit.
observations.managementAction:
2016 AAR

The City prepared and submitted trial balance, financial reports and statements for all funds and these have been furnished to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant undertake action to submit the paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls and supporting documents in subsequent periods to the Auditor’s Office within 10 days of each month after recording in the books of accounts for the timely conduct of audit.
observations.managementAction:
2016 AAR

The City Accountant undertook action and submitted the required documents in subsequent periods.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City GAD focal person submit to COA Audit Team annual GAD Plan and Budget within five working days from the receipt of the approved plan from the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) and the accomplishment report for CY 2016 and subsequent years pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
observations.managementAction:
2016 AAR

The program and the plan were prepared and submitted to COA. The GAD focal person is coordinating with the concerned offices.
observations.reason: Annual GAD Plan and Budget for the current appropriation of ₱100,000,000.00 was not submitted by the focal person to the COA on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CPDO, the CEO and other offices that are involved in the implementation of 20% DF projects, immediately address the causes of delays and fast track the implementation of the unimplemented projects. Regular monitoring of project implementation should be assigned to specific office to ensure proper implementation and the achievement of the desirable outcomes from the delivery of socio-economic development and environmental services to the constituents.
observations.managementAction:
2016 AAR, 2015 AAR, 2014 AAR, 2013 AAR, 2012 AAR

The CPDO has started regular monitoring and coordination with the City Engineer’s Office to evaluate the status of the projects and determine any deficiencies to fast track implementation.
observations.reason: There were 133 projects not implemented under the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that City of Bacolod and the Local School Board refrain from charging non-related expenses to the SEF and strictly adhere to the regulations on the prioritization of expenses charged against SEF as provided under Article 363 (C) of the IRR of RA 7160 and Item No. 4.0 of DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998.
observations.managementAction:
2016 AAR, 2015 AAR, 2014 AAR, 2013 AAR, 2012 AAR

AOM and Notice of Suspensions were issued and City Accountant requested legal basis and supporting documents. The City is evaluating submitted documents.
observations.reason: There were expenses not included in the allowable and enumerated items in the Joint Circular No. 01 Series 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG and R.A. 7160 charged to SEF such as professional fees of teachers and penalty on water expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor thru the Solid Waste Management Board facilitate the speedy siting and establishment of a sanitary landfill to conform to the requirements of Sections 40 and 41 of RA 9003 in order to achieve an effective solid waste disposal system for the City. The City Officials involved in solid waste management to comply with the requirements of said Act to avoid administrative sanction.
observations.managementAction:
2016 AAR

The City Mayor and Solid Waste Management Board are facilitating activities and coordinating with concerned agencies for the siting and establishment of a sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: Sanitary landfill was not yet established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor or the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) determine whether the BAC and TWG members have been remiss in the performance of their functions in the procurement of the learning materials. Further, he must require the BAC and TWG members to submit their valid justifications for not able to ascertain the deficient eligibility documents submitted by D and L Printing and Publishing House. The City Mayor strengthen the procurement function of the present BAC and TWG members or may designate new BAC and TWG members if warranted to increase operational efficiency and effectiveness in the City’s procurement process to ensure that only eligible and qualified prospective bidders are allowed to participate in the bidding of the various procurement needs of the City. The BAC Chairman must recommend to the Head of Procuring Entity (HOPE) or the City Mayor to impose an administrative penalty of suspension to D & L Printing and Publishing House for submitting eligibility requirements containing false information or falsified documents and enforce the forfeiture of bid security or the performance security posted by the concerned bidder pursuant to Sec. 69.1 and 69.2 of R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction:
2015 AAR

Justifications and supporting documents were already submitted by BAC which is under evaluation. A new set of BAC and TWG members were appointed by the City Mayor.
observations.reason: The City Mayor/HOPE has not yet determined whether the BAC and TWG members have been remiss in their functions. Evaluation is being made to assess the operational efficiency and effectiveness of the procurement process. Previous BAC members retired and some actions pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The City Mayor designate Appraisal Committee to assess the current value of the three gymnasiums in order to present a reliable value of the properties in the books. The OIC, City Accountant prepare adjustment on the cost of the three gymnasiums based on the appraisal report of the Committee by debiting the appraised value of the three gymnasiums and a corresponding deduction on unidentified PPE-building account under the SEF books. The OIC, City Accountant immediately make adjustment by adding the cost of Improvement of the three gymnasiums and Construction of one Covered Court including purchase of Geographical Information System as addition to PPE accounts to reflect reliable balance of Property, Plant and Equipment accounts. In addition, it is also essential to prepare adjustment for all maintenance and operating expenses charged against the DRRMF to reflect the correct amount of DRRM expenses for the year 2014. The City Accountant adhere to the guidelines to transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund and the DRRMF-MOOE for the current year including CYs 2013 and 2012 DRRMF balances to the Special Trust Fund under the Account “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund Books pursuant to 5.1.10 COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction:
2014 AAR

The City prepared adjustment entries and started reclassification. The appraisal and supporting documents are being prepared.
observations.reason: Reversion was not fully undertaken and some adjustments remain pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC, City Accountant immediately remind the DepEd, Bacolod City to submit a report of disbursements certified by the DepEd Accountant, approved by the Superintendent and duly audited by the Audit Team Leader and submit a copy of Credit Notice or copy of Official Receipt issued for the refund of unexpended balance of fund transferred pursuant to Item 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Should DepEd, Bacolod City opted to comply with the Affidavit of Undertaking, then the City Accountant require that Office to submit the pertinent documents for the liquidation of the amount transferred/granted as financial assistance. Likewise, the City Accountant ensure that no succeeding release of financial assistance shall be allowed unless the previous one was fully liquidated or accounted for and require that each fund transferred to national agency like DepEd be supported with a Memorandum of Agreement pursuant to Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction:
2014 AAR, 2008 AAR

The City Accountant immediately sent a letter to the Bacolod City Superintendent as a way of reminding to submit the liquidation reports. Several follow-ups were made.
observations.reason: Liquidation was not fully undertaken and some documents remain outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare as soon as possible the necessary adjustment to book up the reversion of the remaining unutilized PDAF funds to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund and the City Treasurer to draw the disbursement voucher covering the return of the unutilized PDAF funds to the National Treasury.

2014 AAR

The City prepared checks in the total amount of ₱1,934,322.00 for the return of the unutilized PDAF funds. However, the amount of ₱1,834,322.00 totaling six checks became stale.
observations.reason: Reversion was not fully undertaken due to stale checks and procedural delays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City’s GAD focal person ensure that programs and projects are implemented in accordance with approved GAD Plan and Budget of the City to address the gender issues and concerns specified therein.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The payment of job hires be supported with specific job description to ensure that expenditures are valid charges against the LDRRM fund. The OIC, City Accountant ensure that program, project and activities to be charged against the LDRRM Fund conform with the guidelines in 5.0 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 26, 2013. Notices of Suspension totaling ₱897,164.00 be settled through compliance with the requirements indicated therein.

2014 AAR

The City prepared checks in the total amount of ₱1,934,322.00 for the return of the unutilized PDAF funds. Notices of Suspension were issued and are to be settled through compliance.
observations.reason: Reversion was not fully undertaken and some notices pending compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The Department of Public Services, City Environment and Natural Resources Office and the General Services Office as well as the Bacolod City Solid Waste Management Board should consider the following: Develop strategies to check the major activities of the contractor, define clearly the activities to be monitored to ensure delivery of satisfactory service and provide parameters to assess the efficiency of the contractor and provide for a Monitoring Team/monitors in specific areas that they could possibly cover within a day to properly conduct monitoring activities and establish a representative percentage for each activity to be monitored to arrive at a valid conclusion on the performance of the contractor and require them to prepare a monitoring report at the end of the day. Document violations committed by the contractor and submit a report to the immediate Supervisor. Require the Monitoring Team to submit semi monthly report to the Solid Waste Management Board and other offices concerned as supporting document of the contractor’s claim and guidance or reference in evaluating the contractor’s performance. Enhance feedback mechanism wherein constituents could report the complaints on the failure to collect waste or unsatisfactory performance of the contractor through hotlines or regular surveys.
observations.managementAction:
2014 AAR, 2013 AAR

The City Mayor informed that three officers were assigned in monitoring garbage collections. The officers reported that the contractor was meted a onetime penalty of more than ₱200,000.00 due to non and delayed collection of garbage. The Monitoring Team is being organized and monitoring reports are prepared.
observations.reason: Monitoring is undertaken by the DPS but there were terms and conditions in MOA not complied with to fully assess the efficiency of collection and hauling accomplishments of the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor efficiently utilize the loan proceeds for purpose for which the loan was secured to ensure the effective delivery of planned program to target beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction:
2014 AAR

The City has started paying the loans and partially utilized the loan proceeds.
observations.reason: Loans are partially paid and partially utilized; full utilization pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The City Treasurer strictly require collecting officers to turnover/remit their collections timely and intact particularly at year end to avoid accumulation of cash in vault to huge amount as at financial statement date and to further avert funds from possible misappropriation, misapplication or loss for any illegitimate reason pursuant to Section 29 on the Manual on NGAS for LGUs Volume I. The Liquidating Officer verify collections turned over by the collectors per Report of Collection and Deposit as against the Official Receipts issued and check the Statement of Accountable Forms to determine the movement of accountable forms in the possession of the collectors including inventory of forms on hand. The MITCS Office prepare a report on the result of its verification of the Official Receipts as reported by the collectors in the RCD especially in case of adverse or unfavorable observations so management can install necessary controls to address the risks.
observations.managementAction:
2014 AAR

The Office of the City Treasurer coordinated with the Office of the City Accountant and the MITCS and started implementation of the new system and forms prescribed by BLGF. New forms of RCD issued by BLGF were made available towards the end of 2014 and the office started using the new form for Receipt of Collection and Deposit.
observations.reason: New forms were issued and implementation began; full compliance pending and collection processes being strengthened.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant require the Chairs and members of the BAC and Technical Working Group to refund the excess honoraria received. We further recommended that the City Accountant ensure that payment of honoraria to the Chairs and Members of the BAC and TWG is in accordance with the guidelines prescribed in Budget Circular No. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005.

The OCA is currently paying the honoraria of the BAC and its technical working group based on their monthly collection for ITBs and bid document fees. The excess payment to the BAC & Technical working group is being deducted from their honoraria.
observations.reason: Some BAC members started paying the disallowance through payroll deduction. However, the City was not able to collect the disallowance from former BAC members and retired members.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Government GAD focal person prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report following the form prescribed in Annex B of Item 5.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-01 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW (now Philippine Commission on Women). Moreover, the consolidated Annual GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2013 of concerned offices be prepared in accordance with what is prescribed under the Joint Circular and be accompanied by required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor/Board of Trustees of the Provident Fund submit immediately the authorization and guidelines from the DBM allowing subsequent government monetary contributions to the Provident Fund. observations.reason: The Board of Trustees of the Provident Fund failed to submit until now the guidelines from the DBM allowing government monetary contributions to the Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant and the General Services Officer to determine the causes of discrepancies between the Accounting and GSO records and require them to submit a report on the result of their verification. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC City Accountant, the GSO and the City Engineer to strictly observe and implement the perpetual method of recording of inventory in accordance with the rules cited above. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC City Accountant submit the required documents for the above transactions, and require the submission of proper documentation to be attached to the voucher before transactions are passed in pre-audit, paid and recorded in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The City Mayor direct the GSO to determine officials accountable/liable to refund the value of parts cannibalized and/or the value of the motor vehicle lost; The City Mayor create a special team to conduct/canvass the prevailing market prices of secondhand/reconditioned units, parts/assembly/accessories on actual costs of missing items of the unserviceable motor vehicles discussed; The OIC GSO submit recommendation for the disposal of one unit Mitsubishi road grader and Sakai roller and provide proper procedures and controls in the return of unserviceable properties, most especially with regards to the motor vehicles and heavy equipment, from the time of the return up to the storage and disposal. Likewise, the OIC GSO define job accountabilities/responsibilities of the personnel involved in the property custodianship. The City Mayor initiate the filing of appropriate administrative and criminal charges against accountable official/employees responsible for the cannibalization/loss of the government properties; and The OIC GSO establish controls, provide proper procedures and documentation in the return of unserviceable properties, particularly motor vehicles and heavy equipment.

2005 AAR

A report was submitted to COA Region VI about 3 years ago for their disposal. A warehouseman is now assigned at the GSO bodega to monitor/record and accept returned properties. The OCA established a team to oversee disposal and monitor unserviceable items.
observations.reason: Awaiting reply of the request for disposal from COA, Region VI. Some administrative actions taken and reports submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC of the General Services Office (GSO) for the above donated properties to inspect and determine their existence and whereabouts, their present condition, the provision of protection further to prevent deterioration and cannibalization; prepare, maintain record and submit the documents for these properties like the Property Ledger Cards, ARE, Property Return Slip; request for the proper disposal if found unserviceable; make a report on the irregular and improper use, losses and cannibalization of said properties and recommend for the filing of charges against all persons liable; revisit and review the system of property management and recommend measures to correct the lapses in the system.
observations.managementAction:
2008 AAR

Data on donated properties are not available. No record as to the transfer of property. Identify properties which can be easily processed and make adjustments in the books of the city. OCA sent a letter to COA Region VI and a report was accomplished and submitted to COA about 3 years ago for their disposal.
observations.reason: Until now, no action was undertaken by management to account for donated properties.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office should intensify its effort to collect sufficient funds in the succeeding year to cover the cash overdraft.

b.) The Local Chief Executive should adopt austerity measures to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft in its future operations.

c.) 	Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to regularly remit all trust liabilities to the agencies concerned to avoid utilization of such funds for other purposes. 

d.) The OIC-Municipal Accountant should analyzed and determine the cause/s of the abnormal balances of some trust liability accounts and make the necessary adjustments to establish the correct balance of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Posting of RPT Tax Incentives for diligent payers on conspicuous places.

Updating of Local Revenue Code.

Conduct of Business-One-Stop Activity
Revenue Collectors deployed for mapping update and collection.

Local Finance Committee (LFC) with the LCE convened monthly to update fiscal status of the LGU.

Adoption of e-budget scheme.

Statement of allotment, obligation and balances prepared and endorsed to LFC with MTO and MACCO reports monthly fiscal status.

Updated weekly and monthly report is submitted and endorsed to the LCE and the LFC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the OIC-Municipal Accountant to comply with and observe strictly to the provision of COA Circular No. 97-002. If the cash advances remain outstanding for more than a month after the completing its purpose, demand letters should be sent out requiring the officials/employees to submit an accounting or liquidation thereof within seventy-two (72) hours. Otherwise, the whole amount should be deducted from his/her salary.
observations.managementAction: The cash advances granted to official and employees was mostly incurred only for the month of December 2016. Since there is a grace period of 30 days the Liquidation Report of the said cash advances was done on the following month because official and employees were not granted another cash advances if no liquidation were being made for the previous cash advances

Report of Collections and Deposits to the accounting division individually submitted to MACCO.

The required records shall also be the basis by the General Services Office for its reconciliation with the actual physical inventory.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office should intensify its effort to collect sufficient funds in the succeeding year to cover the cash overdraft.
b.) The Local Chief Executive should adopt austerity measures to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft in its future operations.
c.) 	Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to regularly remit all trust liabilities to the agencies concerned to avoid utilization of such funds for other purposes.
d.) The Municipal Accountant should analyzed and determine the cause/s of the abnormal balances of some trust liability accounts and make the necessary adjustments to establish the correct balance of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to comply with and observe strictly to the provision of COA Circular No. 97-002. If the cash advances remain outstanding for more than a month after the completing its purpose, demand letters should be sent out requiring the officials/employees to submit an accounting or liquidation thereof within seventy-two (72) hours. Otherwise, the whole amount should be deducted from his/her salary.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for each Revenue Collectors.
Require the Revenue Collectors to submit their Report of Collections and Deposits to the accounting division individually.

Recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive should create an Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory count of all PPE and submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
Instruct the General Services Officer/Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their records and review the composition of the PPE account to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant should maintain Stock and Property Cards and Supplies and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, pursuant to paragraphs 3 and 4, Section 114, Chapter 7, Volume 1 of the National Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units. The required records shall also be the basis by the General Services Office for its reconciliation with the actual physical inventory.

Recommendations:
We have recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
The Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the latter to submit immediately the required reports within the prescribed period, and to facilitate the recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended to require the following:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to  transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMC Fund to a Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund account solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC’s within the next five (5) years pursuant to Section  21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No.2012-002.
Require the LDRRMC to incorporate the programs, projects and activities of the unexpended LDRRMF to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) which is prepared annually.
Instruct the Local Disaster Risk Management Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit the required reports under COA Circular N0. 2012-002, such as the LDRRMFIP (Annex A) and the Report on Utilization of DRRMF using the Annex B Format should be prepared and submitted to the agencies stated above for compliance.

Recommendation:
We recommended to require the LGU GAD Focal Point (GFP) to submit timely the GAD Accomplishment Report as illustrated under Annexes of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 20013-01.

Recommendation:

          We  recommended  that  the  Inventory  Committee  conduct   annual
    physical count of the City’s properties and submit the RPCPPE not later
    than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 on the  MNGAS  for
    LGUs, Volume I so the  Audit  Team  has  ample  time  to  validate  the
    reported balances of PPE.


          Further,  he  must  maintain  property  cards   for   land,   land
    improvements, leased improvements including buildings, health  centers,
    markets, slaughterhouses and other structures and ensure that  updating
    of property cards on furniture & fixtures,  Office  and  IT  equipment,
    motor vehicles & other transportation equipment, machineries and  heavy
    equipment are regularly undertaken.


          Lastly, the City Accountant and the GSO  implement  reconciliation
    of their records on PPEs at  least  once  a  year  to  fully  eliminate
    discrepancies in PPE inventory records.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City Accountant and the CEO exert  sincere
    efforts in the reconciliation of their records regarding completed  and
    on-going projects. The City Accounting Office to prepare Journal  Entry
    Vouchers transferring all other completed projects to  the  appropriate
    PPE and Infrastructure Assets accounts.


          Henceforth, the City  Accounting  Office  and  the  CEO  regularly
    reconcile their records so that the affected accounts  are  accordingly
    adjusted and in order to have a fair presentation of  the  accounts  in
    the financial statements.


          We recommended also that the CEO speed up  the  implementation  of
    both partially  and  unimplemented  projects  to  attain  the  intended
    objectives.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that:

          a) The  City  Mayor  demand  immediate  settlement/liquidation  of
             unliquidated cash advances pursuant to Item 6.0 of COA Circular
             No. 2012-004 for those granted before December 31, 2011;


          b)  The  City  Mayor  and  the  City  Accountant  ensure  that  no
             additional cash advance shall be allowed  to  any  official  or
             employee unless the previous one given to him is first  settled
             or a proper accounting thereof is made pursuant to Section  89,
             PD No. 1445;


          c) The City Mayor require  the  City  Accountant  to  send  demand
             letters  to  officials  and  employees  who  failed  to  submit
             liquidation reports of their cash advances after the prescribed
             period for them to liquidate their cash advances  or  implement
             the withholding of the payment of salary especially  to  active
             officials and employees with outstanding cash advances;
          d) The  City  Accountant  likewise  send  the  bill  to  personnel
             assigned    in    other    government    agencies     demanding
             liquidation/settlement/refund  of   their   unliquidated   cash
             advances. Make representation on this regard to the Head of the
             Agency concerned and its Accountant; and


          e) The City Accountant maintain adequate  subsidiary  ledgers  for
             cash  advances  and  undertake   appropriate   adjustment   for
             erroneous accounts recorded in the account advances to officers
             and employees and special disbursing officers  to  ensure  that
             said account is properly presented in the financial statement.

Management Comment:

          The City Accountant, during exit conference, informed that  Demand
    Letter was already sent three times to the accountable officer  of  PNP
    pertaining to Intelligence Operations. Liquidation Report  was  already
    forwarded to the Commission on Audit thru the Office of  the  Chairman.
    If necessary, the Office of the City Mayor shall issue demand letter to
    ensure liquidation.

           Based on the letter dated June 22, 2017 of Pacita S. Tero,  City
      Government Department Head  II,  Department  of  Social  Services  and
      Development, accountable officer of the cash advance on APEC  at  ₱3.5
      Million, she had already sent liquidation report on September 22, 3016
      to the  Chairman  of  the  Commission  on  Audit,  thru  the  Head  of
      Confidential and Intelligence Funds Audit Unit.

           The City Mayor should continuously monitor  the  liquidation  of
      the said cash advances with the accountable officer follow-up with the
      CIFAU, COA on the status of her liquidation pertaining to APEC.

    Recommendation:

          We recommended that the  incumbent  City  Mayor  demand  immediate
    settlement/liquidation of unliquidated cash advances  for  Intelligence
    Operations and for APEC, both were  chargeable  against  Confidential  or
    Intelligence Fund pursuant to General Guidelines of  COA  Circular  No.
    2003-003 dated July 30, 2003  and  Joint  Circular  No.  2015-01  dated
    January 8, 2015.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City Mayor assign an office to take charge
    in the close monitoring of project implementation  funded  out  of  the
    fund granted and conduct regular inspection on the  accomplishments  of
    the NGOs/POs. It is also recommended that the City Accounting or  Legal
    Office follow-up the liquidation of long outstanding fund  transfer  to
    NGOs/POs which remained unliquidated.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the  City  Mayor  demand  from  the  recipient
    leagues and federations for the immediately submission  of  liquidation
    reports on the financial assistance given  or  require  the  return  or
    refund of the unexpended or unutilized amount pursuant to 3.1.2 of  COA
    Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, otherwise, appropriate legal
    action be instituted.


          We recommended also that the City  Mayor  designate  personnel  to
    conduct constant monitoring of project implementation to ascertain that
    funds granted as financial assistance  is  utilized  for  the  intended
    purpose.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that:


          a) The City Accountant strongly demand the  immediate  liquidation
             of  fund  transfer/financial  assistance  granted  to   various
             barangays for CY 2016 amounting to  ₱3,700,050.00  and  for  CY
             2015 at ₱1,401,240.00 by setting  specific  timelines  for  the
             liquidation considering that the terms  of  the  agreement  had
             already been violated;


          b) The City Accountant must secure documents especially pertaining
             to prior years’ financial assistance amounting to ₱1,315,000.00
             wherein the names of barangays are  specifically  indicated  in
             the SL and ascertain if a corresponding liability with the City
             is recorded in the books of said  barangay  so  that  the  City
             could require the immediate liquidation. Further, prior  years’
             subsidies from PAGCOR, RPTA, SEF  and  Trust  Funds  and  those
             without details should be reclassified or accordingly  adjusted
             considering that their existence is doubtful or she may request
             for the write-off of dormant accounts following the  procedures
             under Item 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December  19,
             2016; and


          c) The City Mayor assign active official or personnel  responsible
             in monitoring compliance with the terms and conditions  in  the
             MOA as well as validation of actual  accomplishments  vis-à-vis
             purpose for which the fund transfer was granted.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that:


          a) The City Accountant ensure that all  the  basic  requirements  for
             procurement  through  public  bidding   are   complete   before
             certifying the disbursement vouchers pursuant to Items 9.0, 9.1
             and 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012;


          b) Additionally, adequate controls should  be  instituted  in  the
             disbursement of government funds to attain  the  objectives  of
             the program and projects of the City, safeguard  its  resources
             and promote efficiency and economy in the use thereof;
          c) The City Mayor approve  only  the  payment  of  procured  items
             through public bidding if  duly  supported  with  complete  and
             appropriate supporting documents; and


          d) The BAC Chairman or the BAC Secretariat submit  to  COA  Office
             the PO and contract together with supporting  documents  within
             five days after its perfection pursuant  to  COA  Circular  No.
             2009-001.

Management Comment:

          Letter of the City Treasurer dated June 22, 2017, stated  that  he
    caused the preparation and duly served collection/demand letters to  23
    delinquent stallholders of the Manokan Country. Machine copies of  said
    received  collection/demand  letters  were  attached  and   were   also
    furnished to the City Administrator for ready reference and/or basis of
    actions  that  may  be  adopted  in  the  event  that   the   concerned
    stallholders will continue to  defy  and/or  fail  to  pay  their  long
    overdue accounts despite valid demand.

    Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City Treasurer immediately  send  collection
    or demand letters to 23 delinquent stallholders at Manokan Country.


          In case of failure of concerned stallholders  to  pay  their  long
    overdue market rental fees despite the demand, it is  recommended  that
    the City Administrator impose  the  provision  of  Section  46  of  the
    Revised Market Code of Bacolod City by  revoking  or  cancelling  their
    lease rights and ejectment, after observance of due process.


          The City Mayor make sure that contract of lease shall be  strictly
    renewed every two years from date the contract is  signed  pursuant  to
    the provision of the Contract of Lease.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City Treasurer follow-up with the  SP  the
    issuance of resolution for the return/transfer of dormant funds to  the
    National Agencies which granted the fund or  revert  back  the  dormant
    accounts to the National Treasury or Current  Account  of  the  General
    Fund of the City based on the MOA.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the  City  Accountant  prepare  Journal  Entry
    Voucher (JEV) to take up all valid book reconciling items at once after
    the bank reconciliation process is finished pursuant to Section 3.3  of
    COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 in order to ascertain the
    correctness of balances of Cash in Bank – LCCA.


          Further, we recommended that the City Accountant immediately  make
    written  representation  to  the  bank  regarding  the   various   long
    outstanding bank reconciling items not  acted.  Otherwise,  appropriate
    adjustments must be prepared for bank discrepancies that the bank  will
    definitely not consider.   Henceforth,  the  City  Accountant  promptly
    inform the bank of any error or  discrepancy  noted  in  the  statement
    while the documents are still available to ensure  that  correction  is
    effected in the subsequent bank statement and within the time frame  to
    submit the data needed for reconciliation. The discrepancies should  be
    thoroughly investigated to determine the person(s) responsible for  the
    error, either the city or the bank so it could be promptly instituted.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City Treasurer  and  the  City  Accountant
    reconcile their records per cashbook, general  and  subsidiary  ledgers
    immediately while documents are still available.  Henceforth,  the  two
    offices must conduct reconciliation at least every end of each  quarter
    and any items for adjustment must be corrected and resolved promptly in
    order to  present  reliable  Cash  –  Local  Treasury  balance  or  the
    accountable officer’s balance of accountability at any  given  time  as
    required under Sec. 181 (c), GAAM, Volume I.


          Moreover, the City Treasurer require the Cashier to rule, foot and
    accomplish the certification in the cashbook pursuant  to  Section  26,
    Volume II of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the  DRRM  Officer  facilitate  the  immediate
    installation of the Automatic Transfer Switch and the  distribution  of
    Emergency Back Pack Kits to the identified rescuers and  responders  so
    the purpose of the purchase  will  be  realized  and  also  to  prevent
    wastage of government resources.


          We recommended that the LDRRM Council  prepare  the  annual  LDRRM
    Investment Plan pursuant to 5.1.2 And Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-
    002 by specifying therein the projects, programs and activities  to  be
    implemented under the Mitigation Fund.  The Annual Work  and  Financial
    Plan of the DRRM Fund prepared by the DRRM Office should only serve  as
    a supporting document to  the  LDRRMFIP  considering  that  it  is  not
    required under the said COA Circular.

Recommendations:

          We recommended that:


          a) The LGU thru the  CSSDO  monitor  regularly  the  existence  of
             income-generating or livelihood projects to  determine  whether
             the objective of the loan assistance for income augmentation on
             the beneficiaries was attained.


          b) The CSSDO officer in-charge demand from the  beneficiaries  the
             full and timely payment of  loan  amortization  to  ensure  the
             relending to other members and groups and to safeguard the fund
             from possible loss and mismanagement.


          c)  The  individual  beneficiaries  remit  directly  to  the  City
             Treasurer’s Office on time and in full the monthly amortization
             paid by the beneficiaries.

Recommendation:

          We recommended the following:


          a) The City Mayor ascertain if the City will continue the transfer
             or donation of the four  units  motorcycles  to  MTCC,  Bacolod
             City.  In case the City pursue said donation or  assistance  to
             MTCC, Bacolod City, the donation or transfer must be covered by
             a Deed of Donation duly ratified by the SP of the City to  make
             the transfer valid as provided in Section 186 of  COA  Circular
             No. 92-386 dated October 23, 1992.


          b) The Heads of Offices of  the  City  ascertain  the  requirement
             needs of their Office when preparing any request  for  approval
             of the City Mayor to avoid wastage of government resources.
          c) Equipment or other government property shall be issued only  in
             accordance with the purpose and for  the  intended  users  they
             were procured.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City  Accountant  exert  efforts  for  the
    timely preparation and  submission  of  trial  balances  and  financial
    reports under the PPSAS to maintain its  relevance  and  usefulness  in
    management decision making and timely conduct of audit.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City Accountant undertake action to submit
    the paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls and  supporting  documents  in
    subsequent periods to the Auditor’s Office within 10 days of each month
    after recording in the books of accounts  for  the  timely  conduct  of
    audit.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City GAD focal person submit to COA  Audit
    Team annual GAD Plan and Budget  within  five  working  days  from  the
    receipt of the approved plan from the Philippine  Commission  on  Women
    (PCW) and the accomplishment report for CY 2016  and  subsequent  years
    pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the CPDO, the CEO and other offices  that  are
    involved in the implementation of 20% DF projects, immediately  address
    the  causes  of  delays  and  fast  track  the  implementation  of  the
    unimplemented projects. Regular monitoring  of  project  implementation
    should be assigned to specific office to ensure  proper  implementation
    and the achievement of the desirable  outcomes  from  the  delivery  of
    socio-economic  development   and   environmental   services   to   the
    constituents.

Management Comment:

          The Schools Division Superintendent, in her letter dated  June  5,
    2017, informed that the line items that have  been  identified  are  no
    longer included in the CY 2017 SEF budget. Further, she stated that  it
    is  her  desire  that  the  basic  function  of  the  SEF  be  utilized
    accordingly in accordance with its purpose.


    Recommendation:

          We recommended that City of Bacolod and  the  Local  School  Board
    refrain from charging non-related expenses  to  the  SEF  and  strictly
    adhere to the regulations on the  prioritization  of  expenses  charged
    against SEF as provided under Article 363 (C) of the IRR of RA 7160 and
    Item No. 4.0 of DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998 dated April
    14, 1998.

Recommendation:

          We recommended that the City Mayor thru the Solid Waste Management
    Board facilitate the speedy siting  and  establishment  of  a  sanitary
    landfill to conform to the requirements of Sections 40  and  41  of  RA
    9003 in order to achieve an effective solid waste disposal  system  for
    the City.  The City Officials involved in  solid  waste  management  to
    comply with the  requirements  of  said  Act  to  avoid  administrative
    sanction.

The Municipal Government intensify its effort to collect sufficient funds in the succeeding year to cover the cash overdraft.

Likewise, we recommended that the Management adopt austerity measures to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft in its future operations.
observations.managementAction: The management still has an overdraft but already secured a Sanggunian Bayan Resolution on cash back-up and adopted a Public Fund Management Improvement Plan (PFMIP).
observations.reason: Management request for additional funding to curtail the overdraft.

observations.recommendation: The Management to comply with and observe strictly to the provision of COA Circular No. 97-002.  If the cash advances remain outstanding for more than a month after the completing its purpose, demand letters should be sent out requiring the officials/employees to submit an accounting or liquidation thereof within seventy-two (72) hours,  Otherwise, the whole amount should be deducted from his/her salary.
observations.managementAction: The management managed to reduce balance of cash advances in the amount of P71,375.
observations.reason: Management strictly monitors the liquidation report on cash advances, thus reducing the balance.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Mayor to  instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of paying government/ employer contributions in excess of P 100.00 per month per employee as provided in Section 2, Rule IV of EO No. 90, DBM & COA Joint Circular No. 93-2 and Republic Act 9676.
observations.managementAction: The management reduced the monthly contribution to what is prescribed under the law.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal School Board of Municipality of Bacolod to comply strictly
 with the provisions of Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998 of the DBM, DECS & DILG and Section 272 of RA 7160, that Special Education Fund shall be utilized exclusively for the purpose for which it was created to promote the quality of service in every public schools.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped utilizing the Special Education Fund for purposes for which it was not created.

observations.recommendation: The Supply/Property Officer to record the supplies and materials acquisitions using the perpetual inventory method as required under the New Government Accounting System (NGAS).  This is done by debiting the inventory account for all receipts of report supplies and materials.  Any issuances of supplies and materials should be made thru a report to be submitted on a monthly basis to the accounting unit by the supplies materials unit.  In which case, the entry should be debited to supplies expense account and a credit to inventory account.
observations.managementAction: Supplies and materials that are not issued directly to the users are properly recorded in the Inventory account.

observations.recommendation: Strictly comply with the provision of Section 119 of PD 1445.  Refrain from using current appropriation/allotment for payment of prior year’s obligations/transactions.  Take up in the books of accounts all valid and legal expenditures that are not paid at the end of the calendar year shall be properly obligated as accounts payable in accordance with laws and regulations to preclude distortion of the current annual budget and possible misstatement of income.  Effect the necessary adjusting journal entries to rectify the error.  Inform all cost center heads in advance to submit supporting documents to the budget office for obligation of the expenses already incurred during the year to be charged to the current year appropriation.
observations.managementAction: Expenses were paid and recorded on the calendar year which it was incurred.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the MunicipalAccountant should
Strictly comply with the provision of Section 119 of P.D. 1445.  Refrain from using current appropriation/ allotment for payment of prior year’s obligations/ transactions.
Take up in the books of accounts all valid and legal expenditures that are not paid at the end of the calendar year shall be properly obligated as accounts payable in accordance with laws and regulations to preclude distortion of the current annual budget and possible misstatement of income.
Effect the necessary adjusting journal entries to rectify the error.
Inform all cost center heads in advance to submit supporting documents to the budget office for obligation of the expenses already incurred during the year to be charged to the current year appropriation.
